Summary: Unrecognized property error when generating PLC code for atomic subcharts that contain an annotation
Description: If a Stateflow chart has an atomic subchart that contains an annotation, generating PLC code for that chart causes an error: Unrecognized property 'IsGrouped' for class 'Stateflow.Annotation'
Workaround: Use comments instead of annotations in the atomic subchart.?

Summary: Execution of real-time models that contain interrupt triggered function-call subsystems call the hardware start function twice
Description: Real-time model execution calls thehardware start function twice when these conditions are both true:  The model contains a     function-call subsystem triggered by the device.   The device driver has a     hardware start function.

Summary: Cluster profile validation fails when using a proxy
Description: If you are using a proxy server, then cluster profile validation can fail with the following error: This cluster requires that your MathWorks account is associated with a MATLAB Distributed Computing Server license managed online.
Workaround: To work around this problem:    Add the following MATLAB code in your startup.m file. For more information about startup.m, see?https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/matlab_env/startup-options.html#brlkmbe-1.    % Fix proxy issues created by the authentication WS clientif verLessThan('distcomp', '6.12')    ac = parallel.internal.webclients.currentAuthenticationClient();else    ac = parallel.internal.mwa.currentAuthenticationClient();endfield = getDeclaredField(getClass(ac), 'fClient');field.setAccessible(true);awsc = field.get(ac);ep = awsc.getEndpoint();dmwscc = com.mathworks.mlwebservices.ws_client_core.DefaultMathWorksWebServiceClientConfigurator(ep);dmwscc.configureClient(awsc);Restart MATLAB and then run code on your cluster.

Summary: System Design Description report for models with requirements might produce Java exception
Description: Simulink Design Description reports for models with requirements may generate nested tables.  These nested tables cause the PDF engine to produce Java stack overflow error exception.Java exception occurred:?java.lang.StackOverflowError?at org.apache.fop.complexscripts.util.CharScript.dominantScript(CharScript.java:730)?at org.apache.fop.fonts.GlyphMapping.processWordMapping(GlyphMapping.java:121)?at org.apache.fop.fonts.GlyphMapping.doGlyphMapping(GlyphMapping.java:89)?at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.inline.TextLayoutManager.processWord(TextLayoutManager.java:973)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.inline.TextLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(TextLayoutManager.java:809)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.inline.InlineLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(InlineLayoutManager.java:329)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.inline.InlineLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(InlineLayoutManager.java:329)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.inline.LineLayoutManager.collectInlineKnuthElements(LineLayoutManager.java:708)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.inline.LineLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(LineLayoutManager.java:634)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.BlockLayoutManager.getNextChildElements(BlockLayoutManager.java:145)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.BlockStackingLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(BlockStackingLayoutManager.java:292)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.BlockLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(BlockLayoutManager.java:115)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.BlockLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(BlockLayoutManager.java:107)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.table.TableCellLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(TableCellLayoutManager.java:224)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.table.RowGroupLayoutManager.createElementsForRowGroup(RowGroupLayoutManager.java:119)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.table.RowGroupLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(RowGroupLayoutManager.java:65)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.table.TableContentLayoutManager.getKnuthElementsForRowIterator(TableContentLayoutManager.java:580)at org.apache.fop.layoutmgr.table.TableContentLayoutManager.getNextKnuthElements(TableContentLayoutManager.java:504)
Workaround: If you are using the Report Explorer, generate the System Design Description report using "Acrobat (PDF)" file format instead of "Direct PDF (from template)".If you are using the MATLAB Report Generator DOM API, do not add width style for ColSpec for nested tables.

Summary: Parameter Estimation tool and Sensitivity Analysis tool do not save sessions with multiple experiments
Description: When you try to save a session with multiple experiments in the Parameter Estimation tool, or multiple signal matching requirements in the Sensitivity Analysis tool, the following error is thrown in the command line: Warning: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event ListItemSelected defined for class toolpack.component.TSDropDownPopup:  Subscripted assignment between dissimilar structures. Error in sldodialogs.data.DesignData/saveSession (line 58) ? ? ? ? ? ? LE(ct) = rmfield(le, 'OperatingPointSetup');   ...  Error in javaaddlistener>@(o,e)cbBridge(o,e,response) (line 47) ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? @(o,e) cbBridge(o,e,response));? > In toolpack.component.TSDropDownPopup>LocalListItemSelected (line 111) ? In hgfeval (line 62) ? In javaaddlistener>cbBridge (line 52) ? In javaaddlistener>@(o,e)cbBridge(o,e,response) (line 47)??

Summary: Error when generating code that incorrectly says there are unsaved changes
Description: When you generate code, you can see an error saying that the model has unsaved changes, even when you have not made any changes.? This can happen when the model contains library links to a library which is saved in an MDL file and contains both Model blocks and Stateflow blocks. You see an error like the following: Can not update the model reference target ... because it has unsaved changes.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, save the library in an SLX file and then generate code.

Summary: HDL Code Generation Report displays Japanese comments incorrectly in the generated code
Description: If you use Japanese comments in your model and select? Generate Traceability Report ?with? Line Level  as the? Traceability Style  in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the japanese comments are annotated incorrectly in the generated code.?
Workaround: Use?Comment Based as the??Traceability Style when you select?Generate Traceability Report and generate HDL code for your model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inactive Variant Bus signals with composite ports may generate uninitialized variables
Description: Simulink model with variant bus signals using composite ports may fail build with uninitialized variables in generated code when all of the below conditions are true: A non-virtual bus feeds the composite ports in a subsystem Some of the composite ports which select signals from buses are inactive Composite ports which select other remaining signals from the same bus are active

Summary: ScansOutputByHardware and ScansQueued values might be incorrect during a background audio output session
Description: Using startBackground  with an audio output session, the values of the properties?ScansOutputByHardware  and ScansQueued  are incorrect, with the following symptoms: ScansOutputByHardware  remains 0 for almost the entire duration of the output. ScansQueued  remains at maximum value for almost the entire duration of the output.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Uninitialized local variables generated with Variant Subsystem when CombineOutputUpdateFcns is selected in Configuration Parameters
Description: For ERT-based targets, uninitialized local variables may be generated in code for Simulink models with Variant Subsystem when all the following conditions are met:  The variant subsystem has the GeneratePreprocessorConditionals  parameter selected.   Configuration Parameters> Code Generation >Interface ?pane>Single output/update function  is selected. A direct feedback loop exists from an output port of the variant subsystem to the input port.  A variant subsystem choice involved in the direct feedback loop consistently breaks the loop from within the choice itself.  A variant subsystem choice not involved in the direct feedback?loop has a state update block (example,Unit Delay) connected to a block capable of creating local variable.
Workaround: Any of following workarounds help avoiding the incorrect behavior:In the Code Generation > Interface pane, disable Single output/update function.Ensure that the algebraic loop is not broken from within the variant subsystem choice.Break the algebraic loop by connecting the Unit Delay block between the output and input of the variant subsystem.

Summary: invalid unordered_map<K,?T> key error during code generation
Description: An error may occur during code generation for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a For Each subsystem. The For Each subsystem input signal has a Reusable custom storage class or is a partitioned input signal from a Data Store Read block, an in-place MATLAB Function block, or an in-place MATLAB System block.  The error message states invalid unordered map <K, ?T> key .

Summary: regionprops3 returns incorrect results for 'ConvexImage'
Description: The?'ConvexImage'  property of regionprops3  returns incorrect results for non-square sized regions in a binary volume.

Summary: No indication MATLAB is busy when using the?Run Section button in the MATLAB Editor
Description: When running code using the Run Section ?button in the MATLAB Editor, MATLAB does not show any indication that it is busy. The code runs, but there are no changes to the display to indicate that the code has run.

Summary: Live Editor does not open on Windows Server 2016
Description: The Live Editor fails to open on Windows Server 2016. This failure might be accompanied by the following error message: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.NullPointerException?  at com.mathworks.mde.liveeditor.LiveEditorApplication.openAsLiveCode(LiveEditorApplication.java:524)?  at com.mathworks.mde.liveeditor.OpenAsLiveCodeActionProvider$1.run(OpenAsLiveCodeActionProvider.java:71)?  at com.mathworks.mde.liveeditor.OpenAsLiveCodeActionProvider$1.run(OpenAsLiveCodeActionProvider.java:62)?  ... ?

Summary: Unable to install support packages to the default directory on MAC and Linux
Description: When trying to install a support package on Mac or Linux, the installation might fail with the installation logs indicating an invalid support package root directory:  No such file or directory?? This can occur if the userpath had been cleared with the command userpath('clear') .
Workaround: To work around this issue, execute a userpath('reset') command in MATLAB and then try installing again.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results when simulating a Reset Function block containing conditionally executed subsystems
Description: When a Reset Function block contains conditionally executed subsystems, such as Enabled Subsystem, If-Action Subsystem, Switch Case Action Subsystem, or Function-Call Subsystem blocks, the conditions?that activate the subsystems are ignored during simulation, and the contents inside these subsystems always execute, regardless of the condition values. The code generated for the Reset Function block is not affected by this issue. Therefore, a mismatch between the results from simulation and code generated can be observed.

Summary: VideoReader fails to read MP4 files with no audio stream
Description: On the Windows? platform, the VideoReader  function fails to read MP4 files that do not contain an audio stream and returns this error message: Error using VideoReader/init (line 601) Unexpected exception in plug-in: 'Unable to determine the number of channels in the audio stream Reason: Unknown error 0xC00D36E6' Error in VideoReader (line 171) obj.init(fileName);

Summary: rmidocrename produces broken URLs when applied to links where destination documents are URLs
Description: Simulink Requirements product on Microsoft Windows makes a mistake?of replacing?"/" with "\"? in URLs.?? ?Details: When using rmidocrename to update parts of URLs, the result is a broken URL For example, if your links point to  "https://application.server.com/proj1/config1/component1/..."?? and you now want to target the same document in the same project and the same component but with a different configuration config2, running the following command will produce invalid links: >> rmidocrename(MODEL, '/config1/', '/config2/')?  The modified URLs will look like "https:\\application.server.com\proj1\config2\component1",? which is incorrect.

Summary: Extrinsic function error when running a model-referenced Fuzzy Logic Controller block in Accelerator, SIL, or PIL simulation mode
Description: Simulink throws an error when you run a model in which both of the following conditions are true: A Fuzzy Logic Controller block is inside of a referenced model You simulate the model in Accelerator, SIL, or PIL mode  Under these conditions, the software attempts to compile an extrinsic function, which causes the following error: Error:The extrinsic function 'feval' is not available for standalone code generation. It must be eliminated for stand-alone code to be generated. It could not be eliminated because its outputs appear to influence the calling function. Fix this error by not using 'feval' or by ensuring that its outputs are unused.?
Workaround: Open the fuzblkobsolete block library. At the MATLAB command line, type?fuzblkobsolete.In your model, replace the Fuzzy Logic Controller block with the FIS Wizard block from the fuzblkobsolete library, and Specify the?FIS structure parameter of the new block.

Summary: Misleading "must be a valid scope identifier" message from incomplete real-time interrupt configuration
Description: If the real-time interrupt configuration for the model selects the Real-time interrupt source ?as Auto (PCI only) ?and does not select a specific I/O board generating the interrupt , the model build fails with an error message similar to:? "The left-hand side of a . operator must be a valid scope identifier"   The real-time interrupt configuration for the model must indicate the board from which the interrupt comes. To resolve the issue, use Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Simulink Real-Time Options > Real-time interrupt source  to select a board as the interrupt source.

Summary: Accessing compound HDF5?datasets containing long member names crashes MATLAB?
Description: Attempting to read or query information from HDF5 files about compound datasets, that have member names longer than 63 characters, crashes MATLAB?. For example, any of the following code snippets will result in a crash: dsetInfo = h5info('myfile.h5', '/dsetWithLongFieldNames');  OR  data = h5read('myfile.h5', '/dsetWithLongFieldNames');  OR  fileID = H5F.open('myfile.h5');dsetID = H5D.open(fileID, '/dsetWithLongFieldNames');data = H5D.read(dsetID);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model with variant subsystem might generate uninitialized local variable with build warnings
Description: Models with variant subsystem generates uninitialized?local variables when all the following conditions are true:  Function call subsystem as variant choices  Function call signal routed through data inputs of variant subsystem  Bus signal routed through the output of a variant subsystem Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals ?is selected   The following warning is displayed during the build process when?Code Generation>Build process>Generate code only  is not selected in Configuration Parameters  : warning: 'rtb_FcnCallSubsysAtOut1_0' is used uninitialized in this function

Summary: MATLAB crashes when a parallel for-loop has variable bounds and requires large local memory exceeding the threshold
Description: For a parallel for-loop requiring large local memory, GPU Coder maps the for-loop into a kernel and allocates the local variables on the GPU's global memory. In previous versions, GPU coder only supported loops with known maximum bounds, so the number of threads launched for the kernel is assumed to be a compilation time constant. However, with support for loops with variable bounds, this assumption is no longer valid and parallel for-loops with variable bounds and large local memory requirements may cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: A block copy crashes MATLAB when the block has an output signal using a custom storage class
Description: When you copy a Simulink block, MATLAB crashes when both conditions are true: At least one block output is connected to a named signal line. The Signal Properties window was used to specify a custom storage class for the signal line.

Summary: Simulink linking option missing in RMI dialogs and menus
Description: Some Simulink options and shortcuts might not be in RMI dialogs and menus if you?complete either of these actions:?? Open the?Requirements context menu for a selection in an?M-file before loading Simulink. Launch the Requirements Editor (for a non-Simulink source) before loading Simulink.  The options missing are:? Link to Selection in Simulink  option in the Requirements context menu. Simulink option in the Document Type  drop-down selector when you create a new link in the Link Editor dialog box.
Workaround: Execute the following command to refresh context menus:>> rmiml.selectionLink([])?

Summary: Out-of-memory error when running the Semantic Segmentation Using Deep Learning example.
Description: The SemanticSegmentation Using Deep Learning  example runs out of memory whenprocessing too many batched images. It also can run out of memory if the images are too large. If you do not change the MiniBatchSize  property to a smaller value, then the example tries to process too many images at one time. The example displays the following error when trying to evaluate the trained network: >> pxdsResults = semanticseg(imdsTest,net,'WriteLocation',tempdir,'Verbose',false);?  Error using nnet.internal.cnngpu.poolingMaxBackward2D  Out of memory on device. To view more detail about available memory on the GPU, use 'gpuDevice()'. If the problem persists, reset the GPU by calling 'gpuDevice(1)ˇŻ.    Error in nnet.internal.cnn.layer.util.MaxPooling2DWithOutputIndicesGPUStrategy/forward (line 22)  ? ? ? ? ? ? argmax = nnet.internal.cnngpu.poolingMaxBackward2D(...    Error in nnet.internal.cnn.layer.MaxPooling2D/predict (line 100)  ? ? ? ? ? ? Z = this.ExecutionStrategy.forward( ...    Error in nnet.internal.cnn.DAGNetwork/activations (line 331)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? outputActivations = thisLayer.predict(XForThisLayer);    Error in DAGNetwork/activations (line 849)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? YBatch = this.PrivateNetwork.activations(X, layerIndex, layerOutputIndex);    Error in semanticseg>iClassifyImagePixels (line 384)  ? ? allScores = activations(net, X, name, ...? ?    Error in semanticseg>iProcessImageDatastoreInParallel (line 616)  ? ? ? ? L = iClassifyImagePixels(X, net, params);    Error in semanticseg (line 183)  ? ? ? ? filenames = iProcessImageDatastoreInParallel(imds, net, params);
Workaround: To fix this error, set the MiniBatchSize property in the semanticseg function to a value smaller than the default value of 128, for example 4:pxdsResults = semanticseg(imdsTest, net, 'MiniBatchSize', 4, 'WriteLocation', tempdir, 'Verbose', false);?You many need an even smaller value for? MiniBatchSize, depending on the amount of GPU memory on your system.??

Summary: Quick Insert does not show list of recommended blocks
Description: Quick Insert fails to list recommended blocks when invoked from the end of an unconnected signal line. Normally, Quick Insert displays a list of recommendations based on the upstream block in the signal path.

Summary: GPU coder does not recognize custom files with the .hpp or .cu extension when using the codegen command
Description: When using the codegen  command, you can specify custom files to include in the build.?However, GPU Coder throws an error when you include the following types of files: CUDA file (.cu) Header file (.hpp)

Summary: MATLAB can crash when a submodel?contains a constant signal?feeding a string block and runs in accelerator mode
Description: MATLAB can crash when simulating a model reference hierarchy in which?the following conditions are met: A submodel of the hierarchy runs in accelerator mode The submodel?contains a constant?signal feeding a string block The constant string signal is not an output signal from a String Constant block  For example,?a?model reference?hierarchy?submodel that contains?a Constant block feeding a To String block?running in accelerator mode can cause a crash.

Summary: Variant Subsystems with?export function models as choices might cause build failure
Description: Models containing variant subsystems fails build process when all of the conditions are true :  Variant subsystem has export function models as choices  Variant subsystem has default variant control specified   Example error message:  CGIR assertion 'ifNode != nullptr && mergeNode != nullptr' failed in 'b:\matlab\src\rtwcg\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_variant_transform.cpp:267'

Summary: Synchronous machine Canay Inductance causes decrease in fault current and affects the speed of the machine in the example model power_machines
Description: Synchronous Machine parameter mapping algorithm (from standard to fundamental) was updated in R2018a to include the affect of Canay inductance. With this change, the machines fault current has decreased significantly and the speed of the machine in the example model ?'power_machines'?decreases incorrectly.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: GPU Coder creates a kernel with uninitialized variables under certain conditions.
Description: Under certain conditions, GPU Coder? may create a kernel without including the necessary parameter in the parameter lists. Instead, it creates uninitialized local variables that results in generating a warning during code compilation and incorrect results at run-time. For example, if the MATLAB code contains imperfectly nested parallel loop that is transformed to a perfectly nested loop, compilation of the generated code results in the "Variable <name> is used before its value is set" warning and incorrect results at run-time.

Summary: ?Code generator triggers software assertion for models that include a Simulink function?that calls?another?Simulink function
Description: The code generator can trigger a software assertion when generating C or C++ code for an Export-Function model that contains a Simulink function that calls another Simulink function: CGIR assertion 'isa(aConst)' failed in 'export/include/cg_ir/const/Fcn.hpp:539'?  This can occur for valid combinations of Simulink functions and Simulink function callers, where the caller (Function Caller block, Stateflow chart, or MATLAB function block) is in a global Simulink function (Simulink Function block, exported Stateflow graphical function, or exported Stateflow MATLAB function) or in a root-level scoped Simulink Function block.

Summary: Margin, border, and padding formats corrupt PDF style sheet
Description: Specifying a border, margin, or padding format in a PDF style sheet corrupts the style sheet. The first use of a style containing one of these formats overrides their occurrence in any other style. For example, consider the following style sheet: p.GreenStyle {??? color: green;??? font-size: 12pt;??? font-style: italic;??? top-margin: 16pt;??? bottom-margin: 0pt;?}?  p {??? color:blue;??? font-size: 11pt;??? font-style: normal;??? top-margin: 0pt;??? bottom-margin: 10;}?  If a report?applies?GreenStyle  to?the first paragraph in a report, all subsequent paragraphs?have a 16pt  top margin and a 0pt  bottom?margin, including paragraphs that specify another style or?do not specify a style.?
Workaround: Specify CSS?border, margin (represented by the DOM OuterMargin format), and padding (InnerMargin) formats programmatically, for example,import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('myreport', 'pdf', 'mytemplate');pStyle = {OuterMargin('0pt', '0pt', '10pt', '0pt')};append(d, Paragraph('Green Style', 'GreenStyle'));para = append(d, Paragraph('Base Style'));para.Style = [para.Style pStyle];para = append(d, Paragraph('Base Style'));para.Style = [para.Style pStyle];close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);?

Summary: DOM API HTML and HTMLFile objects incorrectly convert?vertical-align?format
Description: The DOM API HTML  and HTMLFile  objects may incorrectly convert the? vertical-align:super ?or?vertical-align:sub ? format. These values can also corrupt a DOM-generated Word document.
Workaround:
Instead of using vertical-align:super or vertical-align:sub?format in?HTML and HTMLFile objects, use the HTML or tag, respectively. For example, instead of using

This text contains superscript text.

use

This text contains superscript text.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Running the code generated from a SegNet network produces incorrect results
Description: For the code generated from a SegNet deep learning network, successive runs of the predict  method returns incorrect results. For example, if predict? was? called on successive frames of an input video, it returns incorrect outputs for the successive frames.

Summary: Unnecessary include of Rte_Type.h in model_types.h
Description: For AUTOSAR model builds, the header file Rte_Type.h  is included in model _types.h  file, even if model _types.h  file does not depend on contents of Rte_Type.h  file.
Workaround: Download the attached .zip file.Unzip the attachment and save the workaroundForBugReport1740170.p file to a folder on your MATLAB path.?Run this script in each MATLAB session before opening your model.

Summary: Use of tall arrays and mapreduce with MATLAB Parallel Cloud produces an error
Description: If you use features such as tall arrays and mapreduce  that use the isequal  method with MATLAB Parallel Cloud clusters, you see the following error:   Error using containers.Map/subsref  The specified key is not present in this container.     Features that use this functionality throw this error when using a parallel pool running on MATLAB Parallel Cloud as their execution environment. For example:   pool = parpool('MATLAB Parallel Cloud');   ds = datastore('airlinesmall.csv');   T = tall(ds);

Summary: Test harnesses and their file dependencies do not appear in the Simulink Project dependency analysis
Description: If the Simulink model contains a test harness but no file dependencies, the test harness does not appear in the Simulink Project dependency analysis. Any file dependencies of the test harness do not appear either.?

Summary: XCP Data Acquisition block does not let you specify the DAQ List Priority?
Description: The XCP Data Acquisition block does not provide a mask parameter to specify the DAQ List Priority value.?  The priority value defaults to 0. This value could cause?an unexpected accumulation of?XCP packets before transmission to the master.

Summary: USB-Ethernet drivers are not supported in R2017b
Description: USB-Ethernet drivers for communication between the development computer and target computer are not supported in R2017b. This issue applies for custom target computers. This issue does not apply to Speedgoat target machines, as USB-Ethernet driver support does not apply to these target computers.
Workaround: For custom target computers, use the Ethernet card for communications between the development computer and target computer.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation?might occur for S-Functions that use?ssSetInputPortOverWritable
Description: For an S-Function that sets?ssSetInputPortOverWritable  and has multiple input ports, reuse different than the specification might occur?causing incorrect generated code.  To trigger this bug, the?S-Function must meet these conditions: S-Function block output must be set to SS_REUSABLE_AND_LOCAL.  At least one S-Function input port must be set to overwritable. Output port must set a specific input port to overwrite?another specific?input port. One other input port must have the same datatype and size as one of the input ports in the overwrite specification.   For example, a particular block with two inputs may specify that only the second output should overwrite the second input using these functions: ? ?? ?ssSetOutputPortOptimOpts(S, 0, SS_REUSABLE_AND_LOCAL);  ? ? ssSetOutputPortOptimOpts(S, 1, SS_REUSABLE_AND_LOCAL);  ? ? ssSetInputPortOverWritable(S, 0, 0); // First input port not overwritable  ? ? ssSetInputPortOverWritable(S, 1, 1); // Second input port is overwritable  ? ? ssSetOutputPortOverwritesInputPort(S, 0, -1); // First output port can't overwrite  ? ? ssSetOutputPortOverwritesInputPort(S, 1, 1); // Second output port can overwrite second input port  The generated code may end up sharing the first output port variable?with the second input port variable instead. ?If the block implementation writes the first output port before the second input port is read, an incorrect answer occurs.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow absolute time temporal logic incorrectly evaluated for small integer types
Description: Stateflow transitions guarded by absolute time temporal logic might be evaluated as true early if all of the following conditions are true: The chart uses MATLAB as the action language. The first argument P  to the temporal logic operator is a Stateflow data of small integer type (for example, uint8). The time unit used in the absolute time temporal logic operator is greater than the sample time Ts  of the chart (for example, using after(P, msec)  with Ts  < 0.001).  Instead of staying P / Ts  number of chart wakeups in that state, the transition will be taken on the value of P / Ts  wrapped to the data type of P .
Workaround: In the temporal logic operator, cast the first argument?P?to uint32, for example:after(uint32(P),msec)?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Assignments to a handle class object in a MATLAB Function block might be incorrectly missing when there is an alias to the?object
Description: The code generator contains an optimization that removes redundant assignments to a handle class object. The code generator might incorrectly remove an assignment when all of these conditions are true:? There are at least two consecutive writes to the handle class object. There is at least one read from an alias to the handle class object.  For example, consider this MATLAB function in a MATLAB Function block:   function A? = model_1738576(A)??? handle_A = handleClassObj;??? handle_A.value = A;??? handle_alias_A = createMCOSAlias( handle_A );??? handle_A.value(3,4) = handle_alias_A.value(1,4);??? handle_A.value = handle_alias_A.value;??? A = handle_alias_A.value;end?    The incorrectly generated code, where the write to handle_A.value(3,4)  is removed, might look like:  static void model_1738576_output(void){? uint32_T rtb_A[48];? handleClassObj_model_1738576_T handle_A;? handleClassObj_model_1738576_T *handle_alias_A;? memcpy(&rtb_A[0], &model_1738576_P.IA2_2_1[0], 48U * sizeof(uint32_T));? memcpy(&handle_A.value[0], &rtb_A[0], 48U * sizeof(uint32_T));? handle_alias_A = model_1738576_createMCOSAlias(&handle_A);? handle_A = *handle_alias_A;? memcpy(&rtb_A[0], &handle_alias_A->value[0], 48U * sizeof(uint32_T));? .........}  The correctly generated code looks like: static void model_1738576_output(void){? uint32_T rtb_A[48];? handleClassObj_model_1738576_T handle_A;? handleClassObj_model_1738576_T *handle_alias_A;? int32_T i;? memcpy(&rtb_A[0], &model_1738576_P.IA2_2_1[0], 48U * sizeof(uint32_T));? memcpy(&handle_A.value[0], &rtb_A[0], 48U * sizeof(uint32_T));? handle_alias_A = model_1738576_createMCOSAlias(&handle_A);? handle_A.value[38] = handle_alias_A->value[36];? handle_A = *handle_alias_A;? ......... }?  To reproduce this issue, see the example in the attached zip file example_1738576.zip

Summary: MATLAB crash?when running MEX generated with integrity checks disabled
Description: MATLAB might crash when you?run MEX that was generated with integrity checks?disabled. The crash can occur even when the code does not have run-time errors.?
Workaround: Turn on the IntegrityChecks MATLAB Coder configuration setting and regenerate the MEX file. The equivalent setting in the MATLAB Coder app is Ensure memory integrity.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect behavior for the code generated for a Probe block whose input has a constant sample time.
Description: When a Probe block connects to the output of a block with constant sample time, ?and the Probe block outputs the sample time of its input signal, the expected value of that Probe block output is [Inf 0] . Normal and Accelerated simulation modes provide the expected result.?However, ?in the generated code the value of that Probe block output will be [Inf -1].
Workaround: To check if the input to the Probe block has a constant sample time, consider checking only whether the first element of the output array?of the Probe block is Inf, since the constant sample time is the only sample time that uses Inf as the value of the first element.

Summary: Initialize block or Terminate block in model produces build error
Description: If a Simulink Real-Time model contains an Initialize block or a Terminate block, builds of the model fail.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for the n-D Lookup table block with Interpolation method?Nearest?and Remove protection against out-of-range input in generated code?check box selected
Description: Incorrect?code for the n-D Lookup table block can generate incorrect output when all of the following conditions are true:? The Interpolation method ?is set to Nearest  The Index search method ?is set to Linear   search  The Remove protection against out-of-range input in generated code ?check box is selected The value of any input is nearest?to the last index value ofits breakpoints
Workaround: Do one of the following:Set the?Index search method?to be?Binary?searchClear?the?Remove protection against out-of-range input in generated code?check boxReplace the n-D Lookup Table block with one or more Prelookup blocks connected to?an Interpolation Using Prelookup block?and match the corresponding settings
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 6, R2018a Update 5, R2018b

Summary: Java error when using Document Index tab in Outgoing Link dialog with link to DNG
Description: When you try to create a requirements link with items in a DOORS Next Generation (DNG) project using the Outgoing Links dialog, if you switch to the Document Index tab and the selected DNG project does not have any Modules, a Java log window with a null pointer exception error appears.

Summary: Incorrect code may be generated for fraction output of Prelookup block?in models containing multiple similar Prelookup blocks
Description: Incorrect code may be generated for the?fraction output of Prelookup blocks?under these conditions Model contains at least two Prelookup blocks Index search method  of each Prelookup block is set to Evenly spaced points  Data type of input signal and Breakpoints  data type?are not the same Breakpoints  data type of each block is a fixed-point data type The word length of?one or more?fixed-point data type differs from the word length of the other Breakpoints  data types The word length of each of the fixed-point data types must have the same container word length
Workaround: Set the word length of?all Prelookup Breakpoints data types to the same?length.
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 7, R2018a Update 5, R2018b

Summary: QR and MLDIVIDE (backslash) with gpuArray inputs returns wrong answers for matrix sizes between 16 and 96
Description: For a QR factorisation of the form [Q,R] = qr(A)  with an input gpuArray   A  of size M-by-N where if A is single and real, and M or N are between 16 and 96, or if A is double or complex, and M or N are between 16 and 64,  then the R output is incorrectly returned as an all-zero matrix. For a direct rectangular system solve of the form X = A\b  with an input gpuArray   A  of size M-by-N where N > M and if A is single and real, and M is between 16 and 96, or if A is double or complex, and M is between 16 and 64,  then the result X is incorrectly returned as an all-NaN array. An equivalent bug exists for MRDIVIDE (rectangular solves of the form X = b/A ). For versions of MATLAB between R2015a and R2016b, the problem only occurs at the largest size stated above.

Summary: Error when packaging a deployable archive with a function signature file using the Production Server Compiler app
Description: Sometimes, when you package a deployable archive with a function signature file using the Production Server Compiler app, you get the following error message without additional information:  Failed to build CTF file. ?  There are two scenarios when the error details are omitted: The function signature file contains a signature for a MATLAB function that was not added to the "EXPORTED FUNCTIONS" list. The number of input or output parameters specified in a function signature does not match the actual number of parameters in the function implementation.?
Workaround: Generate a new function signature file template with correct signatures by following these steps:Note the file name of the existing signature file.Click "Remove File" to remove the signature file from the project.??Click "Create File" to generate a new signature file.?Use a diff tool to compare the new file and the old file, and identify any extra functions or incorrect numbers of input or output parameters.

Summary: audioread?returns empty matrix when reading certain MP4 files?on Windows?
Description: The  audioread  function returns an empty matrix when reading certain MP4 files on the Windows? platform.   S pecifically, on Windows 10 systems, if the file contains a mid-stream format change, then audioread  returns an empty matrix.

Summary: VideoReader causes MATLAB? to crash when reading certain Motion JPEG AVI files on Windows?
Description: VideoReader  crashes MATLAB when reading certain MotionJPEG AVI files on Windows platforms. S pecifically, MATLAB might crash on Windows 10 systems running the Intel Media SDK.
Workaround: Turn off? the Hardware Acceleration option using>>matlab.video.read.UseHardwareAcceleration('off')?;

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for variant subsystem with default variant and specific optimization model parameter setting
Description: Incorrect code may be generated for a model that meets these conditions:  Optimize block operation order in the generated code    is set to  Improved Execution Speed.  Starting In R2018a, setting?Optimization Level ? to  Maximum ?will ?automatically set? Optimize block operation order in the generated code  to Improved Execution Speed.  These settings are default for new models created in R2018a.  The model contains a variant subsystem?that meets all of these conditions:  The Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  parameter is selected.  One of the variants uses  (default)  variant condition.  One of the non-default variants has less outputs than the default variant.    When the above conditions are true, preprocessor conditions are generated and the default variant?is not the last variant.??The generated code may look like: model_step() {?  ...?#if Condition == 1?   ? ? variant1(&out1, &out2);  #else  ? ? variant3(&out1, &out2);  #endif?  #if Condition == 2?  ? ? variant2(&out1);  #endif?  ?...  }??  In the above code, when Condition  is equal to 2 , both variant3()  and variant2()  are active. ?The correct code is:? model_step() {?  ...?  #if Condition == 1?  ? ? variant1(&out1, &out2);  #elif Condition == 2?  ? ? variant2(&out1);  #else  ? ? variant3(&out1, &out2);  #endif?  ?...  }??
Workaround: To avoid this issue, perform one of the following actions:Set the Optimize block operation order in the generated code parameter to?Off.Set block priority of the default variant to a larger number than that of the other variants.On the variant subsystem block parameters dialog box, clear?the?Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals?parameter on the variant subsystem block.Replace default variant with negation of variant conditions. For example, ~(Condition==1 || Condition==2)).

Summary: fitsread returns an error when reading character data from certain FITS files
Description: The?fitsread ?function returns an error when reading character data from a binary table. This error is observed when the FITS file has more than 10,000 rows. For example, the following read operation returns an error:   data = fitsread('test.fits','binarytable'); Error message:   Error using zeros  Input following 'like' is not a numeric or logical array.  Error in matlab.io.fits.readCol (line 63)      coldata = zeros(repeat, numrows, 'like', coldata);  Error in fitsread>read_bintable_hdu (line 567)      [coldata,nuldata] = fits.readCol(fptr,tableColumns(j));  Error in fitsread (line 133)      data = read_bintable_hdu(info,hdu_index,raw,tableColumns,tableRows);

Summary: Input command prompt not displayed when invoked from graphics, uimenu, or App designer callbacks.
Description: When running the input  command from graphics, uimenu, and App designer callbacks, the prompt is not displayed. The input  command runs successfully and waits for user input, but without the prompt it appears that the?input  command failed. function test    f = figure;    f.ButtonDownFcn = @func;endfunction func(~,~,~)    disp('button was down')    b = input('input prompt here :', 's');    disp(['the input was: ' b])end

Summary: Constant block within Simulink Function block?does not output expected value in accelerator mode when Simulink Function block is called from Initialize Function block
Description: Let's consider the model which satisfies all the following conditions: A Constant block with a non zero value?is within a Simulink Function block The Constant block has the 'Sample time' parameter set to 'Inf' The Simulink Function block containing this Constant block is being called from an Initialize Function block The model is being simulated in Accelerator mode  In such cases, during model initialization, one would expect the Constant block to execute and output the value specified through its dialog. However,?during model initialization, the Constant block outputs?a value of zero, irrespective of what is specified in its dialog.
Workaround: As a workaround, set the 'Sample time' parameter of the Constant block within the Simulink Function block to -1 instead of Inf.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect arguments passed across subsystem boundary for?atomic subsystem in referenced model
Description: When producing code for a referenced model that meets all of the following conditions, the code generator can omit pointer initialization from the generated step entry-point function for that model: Model configuration parameters Language  and Code interface packaging ?are?set to C++  and C++ class  , respectively.  Contains an atomic subsystem that connects to a root-level Inport or Outport block. For that atomic subsystem, block parameter Function packaging  is set to  Nonreusable function .  The missing pointer initialization causes the step function to not pass the?correct arguments across the subsystem boundary.?
Workaround: Set the subsystem block parameter Function packaging to Reusable function or Inline.

Summary: Error during parallel build of model hierarchy
Description: MATLAB returns an error during simulation or code generation when all of these conditions apply: Configuration setting?Enable parallel model reference builds ?is selected for the top model. The model hierarchy includes at least three levels. Simulation or code generation builds model reference simulation targets for all referenced models in the hierarchy. The slprj directory and mex files are removed after the build. The slxc file for a referenced model does not exist in the simulation cache directory, but exists for all other models.?  The subsequent build aborts with this error: fatal error C1083: Cannot open include file: 'rtwtypes.h': No such file or directoryNMAKE : fatal error U1077: '"C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio 14.0\VC\BIN\amd64\cl.EXE"' : return code '0x2'Stop.The make command returned an error of 2'An_error_occurred_during_the_call_to_make' is not recognized as an internal or external command,?operable program or batch file.?

Summary: Slow performance when selecting the root node of a Simulink Data Dictionary in Model Explorer
Description: In Model Explorer, when you select the root node of a Simulink Data Dictionary to display the dictionary reference hierarchy tree, you might experience performance degradation if the dictionary includes a large number of references to other dictionaries.? Reference dictionaries can reference other dictionaries, so the slowdown can occur for both of these circumstances: A hierarchy with many referenced dictionaries at the top level. A deep hierarchy of dictionaries referring to other dictionaries.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Change log:March 23, 2018: attachment_1732613_15a_2018-02-23.zip, attachment_1732613_15b_2018-02-23.zip, attachment_1732613_16a_2018-02-23.zip, attachment_1732613_16b_2018-02-23.zip, attachment_1732613_17a_2018-02-23.zip: initial version?

Summary: Requirements Consistency check fails after creating link
Description: When you run the "Identify selection-based links having description fields that do not match their requirements document text" Model Advisor check, the report might mistakenly flag a link as inconsistent, even though neither model?nor document were modified since the link was created. This occurs when a new link was created by dragging and dropping the requirement from the Requirements View pane?onto the related block.
Workaround: Because this check is not applicable to requirements imported into Simulink Requirements, you can instead?use the Simulink Requirements Import procedure to update the imported references after the external document was modified. Then use the Requirement Set comparison between older and newer versions to identify items that changed and examine related links.

Summary: normalizeWords("alize") crashes MATLAB
Description: Calling normalizeWords  on the word "alize"  (or other capitalizations, such as "Alize" ) crashes MATLAB. This happens for both string and tokenizedDocument input. The problem is limited to this particular input.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when simulating referenced model instances in accelerator mode if they contain shared local data stores
Description: If multiple models in the same model referencing hierarchy reference a model that uses shared local data stores, MATLAB crashes if you simulate these models in accelerator mode. For example, if:? top.slx references mid1.slx and mid2.slx? mid1.slx and mid2.slx reference sub.slx? sub.slx contains a shared local data store memory  MATLAB crashes when you simulate top.slx in either of the following cases:?Simulation > Mode  for top.slx is Accelerator  Simulation > Mode  for top.slx is Normal  but the Simulation mode ?parameter for both mid1.slx and mid2.slx is configured to Accelerator

Summary: Inconsistent test results report for MISRA C 2012 rules
Description: When executing tests to create the qualification report for the Polyspace checker MISRA C:2012, the results sheet Matrix_misra_C_2012.txt  indicates that no rules are fully checked and that most of the rules are only partly checked. For example:   +---------------------------+--------+  | Rule checker support      | Number |  +---------------------------+--------+  |    Fully checked          |     0  |  |    Partly checked         |   136  |  |    Not checked            |    23  |  +---------------------------+--------+ The Tools Requirements document for Polyspace Bug Finder, called qualkitdo_bugfinder_tr.pdf , at chapter 2.6.3, has the complete list of partly and fully supported MISRA-C:2012 Directives and Rules. The list of fully implemented directives and rules is 104, partly checked 38 and not checked 17.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when using temporal operators with different data types
Description: In a model with a Stateflow chart that contains multiple uses of temporal operators with arguments of different data types, MATLAB can crash when you Run  or Update Diagram .

Summary: Code generation errors out for Simscape model with "Generate makefile" option unchecked
Description: Code generation for a Simscape model generates an error when all of the following conditions are true: The model uses the Template Makefile approach to generate code. The "Template makefile" Model Configuration Parameter specifies a TMF that does not exist or is not in a folder on the MATLAB search path. The "Generate makefile" option is unchecked in the Model Configuration Parameters and, therefore,?the "Template makefile" parameter is grayed out.  Code generation fails?and this error message appears: Unable to locate template makefile: tmfname .tmf?
Workaround: Set the "Template makefile" Model Configuration Parameter to a TMF that exists and is compatible with the chosen "System target file".

Summary: Error when updating a test harness following a clone or rename operation
Description: When you run or perform an Update Model on a cloned or renamed Simulink Test harness with Simulink Dashboard blocks, MATLAB might throw the following error:? Cannot change the 'StreamingClients' parameter while the model 'ClonedHarness' is running??

Summary: Incorrect check color?on memcpy function when destination and source come from a stubbed function
Description: Polyspace Code Prover can show an incorrect result color for the?Invalid use of standard library routine (STD_LIB ) check on calls to the Standard Library function?memcpy . The incorrect result color happens in very specific situations. For instance, in this example, the check determines if there is memory overlap between the destination and source arguments of the?memcpy? function. The result of the check appears red, indicating a definite error. However, because the definition of?foo() ?is not given, the software does not know if the?memcpy  source and destination arguments coming from?foo() ?overlap or not. The result of the?STD_LIB ?check is supposed to be orange, indicating a potential error. #include  extern struct MyStruct * foo(unsigned short index); void main(){    int index;    for(index=2; index>0; index--)    {      memcpy(foo(index), foo(index-1), sizeof(struct MyStruct)); /* STD_LIB check supposed to be unproven (orange) */    }}

Description: This error occurs when using uigetdir   in a deployed application: Error using matlab.ui.internal.dialog.FolderChooser/ doShowDialog (line 70)?Java exception occurred:?java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: eval requires a valid MCR id when running in standalone mode.?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.NativeMatlab.PostMatlabMessage(Native Method)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.NativeMatlab.postMatlabMessage(NativeMatlab.java:224)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.NativeMatlab.postMatlabMessage(NativeMatlab.java:240)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.MatlabLooper.postMatlabMessage(MatlabLooper.java:150)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.MatlabMCR.postAsync(MatlabMCR.java:1546)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.MatlabMCR.eval(MatlabMCR.java:518)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.Matlab.eval(Matlab.java:1197)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.MatlabPath$PathCallback.completed(MatlabPath.java:1449)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.MatlabMCR$InterestHandler.messageReceived(MatlabMCR.java:1667)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.NativeMatlab.ProcessPendingEvents(Native Method)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.NativeMatlab.processPendingEvents(NativeMatlab.java:508)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities$MatlabLatch.safeWait(AWTUtilities.java:892)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities$MatlabLatch.waitFcn(AWTUtilities.java:907)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities$Latch.acquire(AWTUtilities.java:770)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities$Invoker.invoke(AWTUtilities.java:500)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities.invokeAndWaitInternal(AWTUtilities.java:250)?????????at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities.invokeAndWait(AWTUtilities.java:111)?Error in matlab.ui.internal.dialog.FolderChooser/ show (line 48)?Error in uigetdir_helper (line 32)?Error in uigetdir (line 61)?Error in myuigetdir (line 2)?MATLAB:Java:GenericException??
Workaround: Create and use the function?uigetdir_workaround instead of the?uigetdir?function.??uigetdir_workaround uses the Java Swing JFileChooser?object directly to?open a folder selection dialog box.?To create?uigetdir_workaround?, copy this code into a file called?uigetdir_workaround.m:function [pathname] = uigetdir_workaround(start_path, dialog_title)% Pick a directory with the Java widgets instead of uigetdirimport javax.swing.JFileChooser;if nargin == 0 || isempty(start_path)? ? start_path = pwd;endjchooser = javaObjectEDT('javax.swing.JFileChooser', start_path);jchooser.setFileSelectionMode(JFileChooser.DIRECTORIES_ONLY);if nargin > 1? ? jchooser.setDialogTitle(dialog_title);endjchooser.setMultiSelectionEnabled(false);status = jchooser.showOpenDialog([]);if status == JFileChooser.APPROVE_OPTION? ? pathname = jchooser.getSelectedFile();? ? pathname = char(pathname);elseif status == JFileChooser.CANCEL_OPTION? ? pathname = 0;else? ? error('An error occurred while choosing a directory.');end?

Summary: Mixed scalar and empty inputs to graph constructor, digraph constructor, and addedge function return wrong results
Description: When you create a new graph or add edges to an existing graph using graph , digraph , or addedge , the edges are specified by two inputs, s  and t , specifying the source and target nodes of each edge. If one of s  and t  specifies exactly one node, and the other is empty, then this should  specify an empty list of edges. Instead, the aforementioned functions return incorrect results: graph(s,t) ?and digraph(s,t)  return a graph with a self-loop on node 1. addedge(g,s,t)  returns a graph with a self-loop on node 1 added to g . Additionally, in the special case where?s ?is empty and?t ?is a node name,?addedge ?produces the error:?Index exceeds array bounds .

Summary: Cannot simulate termination event for Model block with periodic event ports in top model SIL mode
Description: Your model is subject to this bug when it contains a Model block with the following parameter settings: Simulation mode:  Software-in-the-loop (SIL) Code interface:  Top model Show model initialize port:  on Show model periodic event ports:  on  The model produces an error similar to the one below when the Model block receives a function-call at its initialize port during simulation. Error reported by S-function 'ControllerJMAAB_sbs' in 'RTEModelJMAAB_NoEst/TmpSFcnForModelReference_SW Component1': pXILService is NULL!   Optionally, if the Model block also has the parameter Show model terminate port  set to 'on', the above error message?is thrown when the Model block receives a function-call at its terminate port during simulation.

Summary: Simulating modelswith blocks from SimEvents library in Fast Restart mode may produce incorrect results
Description: If the simulation of a model in Fast Restart mode generates a run-time error in any of the blocks from the SimEvents library, then the model may produce incorrect results in subsequent runs in Fast Restart mode.

Summary: Function not called via mismatched function pointer with -permissive-function-pointer
Description: Suppose you use a function pointer to call some functions. The function pointer prototype has certain pointer types (other than void* ) as arguments or return type, but the functions called have different pointer types for the corresponding arguments or return type.? If you use the option -permissive-function-pointer , Code Prover should recognize the function calls via function pointers despite type mismatches. When the type mismatch involves pointer types other than void* , Code Prover does not consider the function calls via function pointers despite using this option. For example, here the function pointer has a pointer to an int  array as argument but the corresponding called function has an int**  pointer as argument. typedef int ty_array[];typedef void (*fptr)(ty_array*);typedef int *ty_ptr;void foo(ty_ptr* x);? int *var; fptr f_toptr; f_toptr = foo;f_toptr(&var); /* foo() should be called when using -permissive-function-pointer */

Summary: When importing requirements from ReqIF file, attributes of Enum type are not imported correctly
Description: Enum type attributes imported from ReqIF files will have a?Plain Text?String type?in the resulting SLREQX file.  Additionally,?because some?ReqIF files?define?separate unique alphanumeric keys?that map to?user-configured enumerated?values in the source Requirements Management?application, when?Simulink Requirements incorrectly treats?imported values as?basic text attributes, the?UI will display the bare?alphanumeric keys instead of the actual enumerated?values.

Summary: Code generation fails?for models?which use symbolic dimension with leading or trailing 1-s in the specification
Description: MATLAB may crash during code generation for Simulink models using a combination of symbolic dimensions with and without trailing 1-s (for example: [1,SymbolA] and SymbolA or [SymbolB,1] and SymbolB). This is because of an?incompatibility between the specified symbolic expression and the?corresponding computed numeric dimension.

Summary: Root Outport block cannot accept Mux block output with number of dimensions greater than 1
Description: Simulink incorrectly assumes the root Outport to be a 1-D signal when your model meets the?following criteria: One of the following is true: Model contains a?root Outport block with option?Ensure outport is virtual ?selected.  The root Outport block is?connected to an?Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block.  And one of the following?is true: Original source of the signal to the Outport block is a Ground block. Outport block is unconnected and the option?Port?dimensions  is specified as 2?or greater. The source signal goes through a Mux block with at least two input signals?that are combined into a 2-D output signal.

Summary: Incorrect results on the second run of Fast Restart for some Stateflow charts
Description: On the second (and subsequent) runs while using Fast Restart, Stateflow transitions guarded by absolute time temporal logic might be taken at the wrong time if all of the following conditions are true: Execute (enter)?Chart?At?Initialization? is enabled The chart uses absolute time temporal logic The simulation start time is non-zero

Summary: Computer shuts down or does not respond when simulating a Simulink Desktop Real-Time model
Description: Real-time simulation of a Simulink Desktop Real-Time model might fail to start, report a timeout, or cause the computer to shutdown or restart?after updates are applied to the Windows operating system, or after the system has been upgraded to a newer release of Windows 10.
Fixed In: R2016b Update 7, R2017a Update 4, R2017b Update 7, R2018a Update 3, R2018b

Summary: Code generation?fails when top model contains protected model that?references a?model
Description: Suppose you have a?Model block that references a protected model, modelRefProtected . The protected?model?also references a child model, modelChild .?If you?insert the Model block into a model, modelTop ,?and then?try to generate code for the model, the code generation process produces this error:? ### Build procedure for model: 'modelTop' aborted due to an error.  Model 'modelChild' does not exist

Summary: Code Generation Advisor hangs when reviewingreferenced models.
Description: When the Code Generation Advisor reviewsmodels that contain referenced models, the process?stalls with an errormessage.

Summary: 'Indices' error when running a model with Selector and Assignment blocks if variable named int32 exists in base workspace
Description: Simulink throws an error when you run or update a model that contains Selector or Assignment blocks if a variable?named int32  exists in the base workspace. A sample error message is: Invalid parameter 'Indices' specified in 'untitled/Selector'?

Summary: eigs errors if files named?c.m or normRes.m?are on the MATLAB path
Description: If files named?c.m  or normRes.m ?are available on the MATLAB path, then any call to the function eigs  results in an error. For a file named c.m , the error returned by eigs  is:  ? ? "c" previously appeared to be used as a function or command, conflicting with its use ? ?  here as the name of a  variable.? ? A possible cause of this error is that you forgot to initialize the variable, or you? ?   have initialized it  implicitly using load or eval.   For a file named?normRes.m , the error returned by  eigs  is:  ? ? Variable "normRes" has been previously used as a function or command name.
Workaround: If possible, rename the file c.m or normRes.m on the path. To find all such files on the path with these names, use the commands:>> which -all c.m>> which -all normRes.m

Summary: Fast Block LMS Filter block errors for Reset port choice
Description: In Fast Block LMS Filter block, changing the? Reset port choices to "Rising edge", "Falling edge", or "Either edge" and then clicking OK or Apply button might result in an error: Output argument "strOut (and maybe others) not assigned during call to "dspblkfblms>transformResetString"

Summary: Orphaned section titles in Report API PDF report
Description: If a Section  title and its following content, such as a?SimulinkObjectProperties  reporter, cannot fit on the same page in PDF output, the section title can be orphaned. An orphaned section title appears at the end of a page and the section content appears on the following page.
Workaround: Create an mlreportgen.report.Report template and unzip it to customize. >> mlreportgen.report.Report.createTemplate('myreport.pdftx','pdf');>> unzipTemplate('myreport.pdftx');Edit myreport\stylesheets\root.css by updating the p.LinkTargetWrapper style to add keep-with-next format.      p.LinkTargetWrapper {??          line-height: 0pt;??          -fo-keep-with-next.within-page: always;      }Zip the custom template.      >> zipTemplate('myreport.pdftx','myreport');Use this custom template to generate the report.      >> slreportgen.report.Report('output','pdf','myreport.pdftx');

Summary: False positive in MISRA C++:2008 checker for rule 9-3-3 when calling a non-const function of base class from a derived class
Description: MISRA C++: 2008 rule 9-3-3 states, "If a member function can be made static then it shall be made static, otherwise if it can be made const then it shall be made const." The rule checker can sometimes show a violation on a non-const  member function that cannot be declared as const. The function cannot be declared as const because it belongs to a derived class and calls a non-const  member function of the base class. namespace test {  class Base {     public: Base();       void start();  };   class Derived: public Base {    public:       Derived(): Base() {}       void foo() {  // should not raise a 9-3-3 violation here         start();       }   }; };

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might not preserve data integrity for data transfers between asynchronous function-call subsystems
Description: Data integrity might not be preserved when these conditions exist: Model includes multiple asynchronous function-call subsystems. Data transfers occur between the subsystems. One block provides the asynchronous trigger for the subsystems. The subsystems are deployed as tasks that can be interrupted. A source task writes data to be read by a destination task.? The source task can preempt the destination task while the destination task is reading data.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possiblewrong answer in code generated from MATLAB code containing a persistenthomogenous cell array of System objects with?handle?object properties
Description: The generated code can produce wrong answers if the MATLAB code contains this code pattern: The MATLAB code has a persistent homogenous cell array of MATLAB?System objects. A property of the System objects is a handle class object which is assigned in setupImpl . The MATLAB code calls the step  method on the elements of the cell array in a loop. The loop has a dynamic index that is determined at run time.  ?For example, the code generated from this MATLAB code produces wrong answers: function foo(n)        persistent c;    if isempty(c)        c = create(3);        end        use(c,n);            endfunction c = create(n)    c = cell(1,n);    for i = coder.unroll(1:n)        c{i} = OuterSysObj;    endendfunction use(c,n)        for i = 1:n        c{i}.step(i);    endend classdef OuterSysObj < matlab.System    properties        p;    end    methods(Access = protected)        function setupImpl(o)            o.p = MyHandleClass;        end        function y = stepImpl(o,n)            y = n;        end    endend
Workaround: Unroll the loop?in your MATLAB code that contains the?call to the step method.

Summary: Unresolved dependency files cause Simulink Real-Time Explorer start up error
Description: Issues with unresolved dependency files (for example, a DLL in an unexpected location) can cause Simulink Real-Time Explorer to report a start up error.  The error message takes the form: "Object reference not set to an instance of an object"   After you respond to the message by clicking the OK button, another error message appears of the form: "Unable to load one or more of the requested types. Retrieve the LoaderExceptions property for more information"
Workaround: As a workaround, follow these instructions:Make sure MATLAB is closed.Open? Windows File Explorer and change the folder to the following MATLAB folder:<$:\matlabroot>\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\xpcmngr\bin\win64Select the file "System.Windows.Interactivity.dll" and copy itChange the folder location to the following MATLAB folder:<$:\matlabroot>\bin\win64Paste the file in the location specified in step 4.Open MATLAB.

Summary: If you load a MAT-file that contains an object that uses ConstructOnLoad and has a private constructor, MATLAB can crash when you pass the object to a function such as isequal
Description: When defining a class that uses the ConstructOnLoad  class attribute and that has a private constructor, loading a MAT-file?that contains aninstance of this class can result in a failure to load the variable correctly. Specifically, the variable is loaded as an empty instance of that class type.  MATLAB can subsequently crash when using the isequal  function, either directly or indirectly with a MATLAB variable that references an empty class instance.

Summary: Compiler warnings due to a missing cast from pointer-to-const to pointer
Description: In rare cases, the code generator may produce code like the following static void example_1722468(const creal_T x_data[], const int32_T *x_size, creal_T y_data[], int32_T *y_size)?  {? emxArray_creal_T_myStruct_T locStruct;?  [SNIP]?  locStruct.size = x_size;   The assignment locStruct.size = x_size? discards the const qualifier from  x_size ?as  locStruct.size ?is not const. This may lead to a C or C++ compiler warning similar to the following:  warning: assignment discards ˇ®constˇŻ qualifier from pointer target type?  In this situation the generated code is missing a needed cast for const-correctness: locStruct.size = (int32_T *)x_size; ?

Summary: Simulation errors when the MATLAB Drive Connector and 3D Engine are both enabled?
Description: If you run a simulation with the MATLAB Drive Connector and 3D Engine both enabled, the simulation can error with this message:   MATLAB System Block '.../Simulation 3D Engine' error occurred when invoking 'setupImpl' method of 'Simulation3DEngine'
Workaround: Exit MATLAB Drive Connector.Restart MATLAB.Run this MATLAB script:dcpsInfoRepoProcess = System.Diagnostics.Process.GetProcessesByName('DCPSInfoRepo');if dcpsInfoRepoProcess.Length > int32(0)    dcpsInfoRepoProcess = dcpsInfoRepoProcess(1);    dcpsInfoRepoProcess.Kill();    dcpsInfoRepoProcess.WaitForExit(10000);endRun the desired?Simulink model with the 3D Engine enabled.

Summary: generateAudioPlugin -win32 plugin generation not working with Microsoft Visual Studio 2017
Description: Attempting to generate a 32-bit plugin using?generateAudioPlugin -win32  with the Microsoft Visual Studio 2017 compiler fails with an error message similar to  NMAKE : fatal error U1052: file 'MyPluginClass_rtw.mk' not found

Summary: MATLAB Variables or Data Objects displayincorrect MATLAB expression for n-D data
Description: The string displayed in the Value field when exploringa MATLAB Variable or Data Object might have mismatched parentheses for n-D(n>2) data of non-double type. The underlying data for the object isunaffected, but evaluating the Value string shown on the UI in MATLAB wouldresult in an error.

Summary: Cannot link a range in?MATLAB code?to a selection in?the Requirements Editor, when a requirement link already exists for this location in the MATLAB file
Description: You cannot select a range of MATLAB code and execute "Link to Selection in Requirements Editor" if the selected range in MATLAB has existing outgoing traceability link(s).  You will encounter a hard error about mismatched structure fields during concatenation, for example: Number of fields in structure arrays being concatenated do not match.   Concatenation of structure arrays requires that these arrays have the same set of fields.??

Summary: Build process on Linux fails for?model with C++ S-functions?
Description: On a Linux system, when you build a model that contains an S-function block, the build process fails?if: The S-function is a?C++ function.   On?the model's Configuration Parameters >  Code Generation  pane, you: Cleared the Generate makefile  check box. Specified the use of template makefiles.   You see?the following error:   File I/O error on file ''  Operation that caused an error: open  The error that occurred was:  Cannot open file. Existence? Permissions? Memory? . . .

Summary: MATLAB crashes when clicking elements in a model that has Dashboard blocks connected to elements inside a deleted subsystem
Description: MATLAB might crash when you click on a Dashboard block or an element connected to a Dashboard block if one or more Dashboard blocks in the model are connected to an element inside a subsystem that you previously deleted.

Summary: Redirecting incoming links may crash MATLAB if you enable Change Information display
Description: There are two workflows to redirect incoming links that can cause this issue:  If you import requirements as references from an external document to a Simulink Requirements Set and select?Redirect Links to Imported References ?from the context menu of the Requirements Set in Requirements Browser.  If you have a Simulink Test license, you can redirect links that point to an internal test harness object to an external test harness object by exporting the test harness. All the links that point to Simulink objects in the internal test harness will redirect to the Simulink objects in the exported model.   In the above two workflows, MATLAB may crash if you choose to display Change Information for these links and access this information via the Requirements Editor  or the Requirements Perspective .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Rate Transition block fails to?protect data integrity for asynchronous tasks
Description: Code generated for the Rate Transition block does not protect data integrity when all of the following conditions are true:  Input to Rate Transition block is connected to an Asynchronous Task Asynchronous Task feeding Rate Transition block has a higher priority than the task connected to the Rate Transition output port? Data passed through Rate Transition block is not scalar  If the lower priority task is interrupted by the higher priority Asynchronous Task more than once while the lower priority task is copying from the Rate Transition buffer, the lower priority task might receive corrupted data.

Summary: When run repeatedly, evaluate and infeasibility can give wrong results
Description: For problem-based optimization, if you run evaluate  or infeasibility  with different input values, the results after the first run can be inaccurate. For example: ? x = optimvar('x',3,1);? expr = sum(x); ? pt.x = [0;0;0]; ? value1 = evaluate(expr,pt)  ? value1 =  ? ?? ? 0  This result is correct. However, run evaluate ?on a?different point: ? pt.x = [1;1;1]; ? value2 = evaluate(expr,pt)  ? value2 =  ? ?? ? 0  This is erroneous.?
Workaround: To correctly evaluate an OptimizationExpression or an OptimizationConstraint repeatedly, rerun the code that generates the optimization variables and expressions before each call to evaluate or infeasibility.For the example above:?x = optimvar('x',3,1);expr = sum(x); pt.x = [0;0;0]; value1 = evaluate(expr,pt);x = optimvar('x',3,1);expr = sum(x);pt.x = [1;1;1];value2 = evaluate(expr,pt)value2 =???? 3?To run repeated evaluations more easily, write a function that generates the optimization variables and expressions. For example,function value = evaluateMyExpr(pt)x = optimvar('x',3,1);expr = sum(x);value = evaluate(expr,pt);endCall the function this way.?pt.x = [0;0;0]; value1 = evaluateMyExpr(pt);pt.x = [1;1;1];value2 = evaluateMyExpr(pt);
