Description: Simulink might generate incorrect code or produce incorrect results when simulating in Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator modes for models in which all of the following conditions are true: A Direct Lookup Table (n-D) block?is?inside a For Iterator Subsystem  The For Iterator signal is feeding the?index port of a Direct Lookup Table (n-D) block  The For Iterator?signal is being monitored by a Scope or Viewer   In the attached example model, the Lookup Table output is meant to accumulate over?multiple iterations. In normal mode simulation, the output value?is 106, which is the correct value. In accelerator mode, the output value is 300, which is the incorrect value. This issue also exists in the generated code:  ? /* Outputs for Iterator SubSystem: '<Root>/For Iterator Subsystem' incorporates:  ? ?* ?ForIterator: '<S1>/For Iterator'  ? ?*/  ? if (0 < example_model_issue_1720319_P.ForIterator_IterationLimit) {  ? ? /* LookupNDDirect: '<S1>/Direct Lookup Table (n-D)'  ? ? ?*  ? ? ?* About '<S1>/Direct Lookup Table (n-D)':  ? ? ?* ?1-dimensional Direct Look-Up returning a Scalar  ? ? ?*/  ? ? if (rtb_ForIterator > 2) {  ? ? ? tmp = 2;  ? ? } else if (rtb_ForIterator < 0) {  ? ? ? tmp = 0;  ? ? } else {  ? ? ? tmp = rtb_ForIterator;  ? ? }  ? }    ? for (s1_iter = 0; s1_iter <  ? ? ? ?example_model_issue_1720319_P.ForIterator_IterationLimit; s1_iter++) {  ? ? rtb_ForIterator = s1_iter;    ? ? /* Sum: '<S1>/Sum' incorporates:  ? ? ?* ?LookupNDDirect: '<S1>/Direct Lookup Table (n-D)'  ? ? ?* ?Memory: '<S1>/Memory'  ? ? ?*  ? ? ?* About '<S1>/Direct Lookup Table (n-D)':  ? ? ?* ?1-dimensional Direct Look-Up returning a Scalar  ? ? ?*/  ? ? rtb_Sum = example_model_issue_1720319_P.DirectLookupTablenD_table[tmp] +  ? ? ? example_model_issue_1720319_DW.Memory_PreviousInput;    ? ? /* Update for Memory: '<S1>/Memory' */  ? ? example_model_issue_1720319_DW.Memory_PreviousInput = rtb_Sum;  ? }   The control flow 'if (rtb_ForIterator > 2) {... ' is incorrectly generated outside of the for  loop 'for (s1_iter = 0;... '.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier takes long time to load existing test cases
Description: Simulink Design Verifier takes long time to load existing test cases when the user specifies Data file ?by selecting the option Extend existing test cases  in the Configuration parameters dialog box

Summary: Simulink Real-Time license is checked out when the Test Manager starts in Simulink Test
Description: On Microsoft Windows platforms, a Simulink Real-Time license is checked out when starting the Test Manager in Simulink Test. The license should be checked out when a Simulink Real-Time API is called, for example, in a Real-Time Test Case, when overriding parameters or running the test. The patch introduces the following change in behavior: In a Real-Time Test Case, in the "System Under Test" test case section, the drop-down list to select a target computer will remain empty until a Simulink Real-Time API is called (?Simulink Real-Time license is checked out) and the Test Case definition is re-opened.?
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it.   Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.  Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Existing?requirements links from Simulink model to external requirements document?are not updated when the external document is imported into a Simulink Requirements Set
Description: When you import read-only requirements from an external document, existing navigation links from MATLAB/Simulink design elements to the imported requirements are not updated. When you click on a requirement link, the external document is loaded and the linked requirement is highlighted in the document's native application (for example, in an Excel worksheet) instead of highlighting the imported?content of the linked requirement in the Requirements Editor/Requirements Perspective.

Summary: Windows 7 computer shuts?down when?simulating a Simulink Desktop Real-Time model
Description: Windows 7 SP1 computer with updates from early January 2018 (KB4056894 and KB4056897) shuts down when you run a model with?Simulink Desktop Real-Time blocks. These updates apply security fixes that are incompatible with the real-time kernel code in Simulink Desktop Real-Time.?
Workaround: Patches are available for releases R2015a to R2017a. These patches contain multiple compatibility fixes that are also applicable to other bug reports that redirect here. If you are running a different version, please contact MathWorks Technical Support.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Restart MATLAB.At the MATLAB command prompt, type sldrtkernel -setup to reinstall the new Simulink Desktop Real-Time kernel.January 19, 2018: attachment_1719571_15a_win64_2018-01-19.zip, attachment_1719571_15b_win64_2018-01-19.zip, attachment_1719571_16a_win64_2018-01-19.zip, attachment_1719571_16b_win64_2018-01-19.zip, attachment_1719571_17a_win64_2018-01-19.zip: updated.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Referenced models with constant value output may produce an incorrect answer
Description: Model hierarchy simulation might produce an incorrect answer when all of the following conditions apply: A model is referenced in Accelerator mode. This referenced model contains one of the following blocks connected to a root Outport block: Probe Width User-written S-Function with a constant sample time output   Code generated from the top model might also produce an incorrect answer.? In both cases, the incorrect output is always the ground value.

Description: Verification for C++ applications can sometimes fail during the compilation phase with an error message similar to this:? ? IL entry write-read difference: region number???1  ?????entry kind =??2 (constant),?  written = 31131,?  read = 31110  ????????missing entry = 184  ????????missing entry = 186  ????????missing entry = 187...? Summary: The?int?function returns incorrect results for definite integrals of rational trigonometric functions over certain intervals
Description: For definite integrals of certain rational combinations of trigonometric terms, over an interval of width pi, 3*pi, 5*pi, ˇ­, symbolic integration (int ) returns incorrect results. >> syms x??  >> int(sin(x)/(sin(x)+3),x,-pi,0)  ans =  -(pi*(3*2^(1/2) - 4))/8  >> double(ans)  ans =  ?? -0.0953  >> integral(@(x) sin(x)./(sin(x)+3),-pi,0)  ans =  ? ?-0.9115
Workaround: Shift the interval to start at 0 by replacing:All instances of?x?with?x?+?a?in the integrandThe right endpoint with b - aThe left endpoint with 0

Summary: Data cursors in the Simulation Data Inspector might show no value or incorrect value when first point is off-screen
Description: The data cursors in the Simulation Data Inspector show no value or an incorrect value when a signal with a nondouble data type is plotted and its first point is off-screen.

Summary: The Image Labeler and Ground TruthLabeler apps become unresponsive when there are thirty (or more) labeldefinitions defined.
Description: The ImageLabeler and Ground Truth Labeler apps become unresponsive to UI interactionsafter approximately thirty label definitions are defined. ?When you attempt to draw an ROI in the image,the apps go into a waiting state, where no interactions are possible forseveral minutes.

Summary: Simulink may hang when compiling a model with a Model block whose referenced model has Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Functions and the referenced model inherits its sample time from the parent model.
Description: Given a model?with?a Model block,?if the referenced model satisfies the following conditions:? 1. It has Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Functions 2. It has inherited its sample time from the parent model Simulink may hang when compiling this model.
Workaround: This issue can be fixed by modifying the Periodic sample time constraint for the referenced model.?In the referenced model, open the?Configuration Parameters. In?Solver details >> Tasking and sample time options, change the?Periodic sample time constraint?parameter from "Ensure sample time independent" to "Unconstrained".

Summary: Proof Objective, Proof Assumption, Test objective and Test condition blocks produce incorrect answers for enumeration values derived from built-in integer types
Description: The Values  field of a Proof Objective, Proof Assumption, Test Objective or Test Condition block accepts enumerations. However, when a Proof Objective, Proof Assumption, Test Objective, or Test Condition block uses an enumeration not derived from Simulink.IntEnumType, such as int8 and uint16, the results are incorrect.  In cases where the enumeration is not derived from Simulink.IntEnumType:  The Proof Objective is always proven valid. The Test Objective cannot be satisfied The Proof Assumption and Test Condition have no effect.
Workaround: If you are using R2015aSP1 or later, and want to keep the enumeration as a subclass of a built-in integer type, such as int8 and uint16, insert a Compare To Constant block with the Operator ==, and set the Constant value to the enumeration value. Connect the Compare to constant block to the Proof Objective, Proof Assumption, Test objective or Test condition block, and set its value to True.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible wrong answer between the simulation and generated code for?n-D Lookup Table and Interpolation using Prelookup blocks when?floating and fixed-point data types are specified
Description: n-D Lookup Table and Interpolation using Prelookup blocks might?return different answers?between simulation and code generation?when these?conditions are true: Table data  type is set to fixed point with non-trivial slope and bias Intermediate results  type is set to double  or single  For the n-D Lookup Table?block, Fraction  type is set to double  or single   Differences between simulation and code generation are caused by:  At?simulation, table data is cast to a float (double  or single ) type before?the interpolation is calculated,?causing?the?slope and bias?to be?applied before interpolation computation.  At?code generation, stored integers of table data are used to compute the interpolation, and the slope and bias corrections are applied afterwards.

Summary: Cannot import multiple Excel worksheets from the same Excel file into the same requirements set
Description: You cannot import another worksheet from the same Excel file into the same requirements set.
Workaround: Import each additional worksheet into a separate?requirements set.

Summary: Repeating a call to?startForeground returns stale data?for a session?with X-series PCI/PXI channels
Description: Sessions that contain?a certain?combination of channels for X-series PCI and PXI devices (for example, NI PCIe 6363 or NI PCIe 6321) will return stale data. This occurs after the first call to startForeground , when the session contains at least one analog input channel along with an analog or digital output channel.
Workaround: To work around this issue, call release() between calls to startForeground. For example:s = daq.createSession('ni');addAnalogInputChannel(s,'Dev1,'ai0','Voltage');addAnalogOutputChannel(s, 'Dev1','ao0','Voltage');% 1st rundataout = zeros(1000,1);queueOutputData(s, dataout); % Queue the samples[datain1,timestamps1] = startForeground(s);release(s); % Call release between calls to startForeground% 2nd runqueueOutputData(s,dataout); % Queue the samples[datain2,timestamps2] = startForeground(s);

Summary: Invoking the Update functionality on a Requirement Set that uses custom attributes may lead to the corruption of the Requirement Set file
Description: After invoking the update functionality on a Requirement Set that contains external requirements that use custom attributes, the update process can corrupt the Requirement Set (.slreqx) file.?  The file corruption might not be apparent until a user restarts MATLAB and tries to reopen the Requirement Set?(.slreqx)?file. Opening the Requirement Set file can result in the following error in the MATLAB command window:  Warning: MF0 parse error in <<fileName.slreqx>>:?  Cannot establish association to a dangling reference to xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx.?   where x is some digit or lower-case letter.  This corruption is known to happen under the following conditions:  The external requirements are imported from an external data source and use custom attributes. For example, when importing from Microsoft Excel, users typically map some columns to custom attributes in the Requirement Set, or when using the command-line utilities to import from DOORS, users may also create custom attributes. Before Update, some rows were deleted in the external document. As a result, Update deleted from the Requirement Set some of the previously imported external requirements.

Summary: Not all required DLLs are packaged with packNGO for model with MIDI Controls block
Description: When you generate a standalone executable from a Simulink model that includes a MIDI Controls block,?not all dependencies are included in the .zip that is created with packNGo. Attempting to run the standalone executable fails with an error like    Could not open library: midi.dll.

Summary: Incrementally increasing test coverage workflow throws an error if the coverage data was collected on a nested subsystem
Description: Suppose a model contains a subsystem named ss1 , and an sltest-harness named harness1  for this subsystem. Subsystem ss1  contains a nested subsystem, ss2 , and an sltest-harness named harness2  for ss2 . If you collect coverage data by simulating harness2,??save the coverage data to a file, and then use this file to incrementally increase test coverage for subsystem ss2 , the test generation fails.

Summary: Internal error during HDL code generation from Lookup Table(n-D) block when data types of Breakpoints?parameter and Input?port have different signs
Description: HDL code generation produces an internal error if both  these conditions are true: Subsystem contains Lookup Table(n-D)  block with Fixed-Point  inputs. Input data type of Lookup Table(n-D)  block and data type of Breakpoints  parameter have different signs (Ex: Input  port type is ufix32_En23 and? Breakpoints ?type is sfix32_En23).

Summary: MATLAB crashes if Simulink Code Inspector compatibility checks run on a Stateflow chart with unstructured control flow within a loop
Description: Simulink Code Inspector does not support loops in the flow graph of a Stateflow chart that have unstructured control flow. The compatibility checker crashes MATLAB when it tries to detect such unstructured control flow within Stateflow loops.

Summary: SLDV Test Generation may incorrectly report some objectives as Unsatisfiable when a subset of inputs on min/max block are driven by common signal data
Description: SLDV Test Generation may incorrectly report some objectives as Unsatisfiable  when a subset of inputs on min/max  block are driven by common signal data?

Summary: Assertion error when output with same name as input is elided
Description: ?MATLAB might throw an assertion error when all of the following conditions are true: You generate code for an entry-point function?with the ?pattern [A, X] = foo(X).   Output X? is elided because you specified fewer outputs than the number of outputs defined in the function (using -nargout  with codegen  or Number of outputs  in the MATLAB Coder app)  The assertion looks like:   ? Assertion at b:\matlab\src\eml\dom_eml\eml_upper_bounds_analysis.cpp line 1397  For example, consider the following function: ?function [A, B, X] = foo(X)? Generating code with -nargout 1  or -nargout 2  causes the assertion because the output X  is elided.
Workaround: To make sure that an output with the same name as an input is not elided, use one of the following workarounds:Specify the number of outputs so that it includes the output variable that has the same name as the input variable.Rename the output variable so that it does not have the same name as the input variable.

Summary: Error when merging coverage results from software-in-the-loop (SIL) tests
Description: Merging coverage results from software-in-the-loop (SIL) tests produces this error: <file name> is not a valid model name because it is not a valid MATLAB function name.

Summary: Some custom library blocks don't appear in Library Browser
Description: Subsystems containing MATLAB Function blocks do not appear in the Library Browser tree. This issue occurs when these subsystems belong to libraries that are created or saved in R2017b or R2018a or libraries exported from a different release to R2017b or R2018a. ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results generated by Simulink Design Verifier in case of Simulink.Breakpoint/Simulink.LookupTable objects
Description: Incorrect results are generated by Simulink Design Verifier when the model uses Simulink.LookupTable/Simulink.Breakpoint object and the storage class of Simulink.LookupTable/Simulink.Breakpoint object is not ?Auto.   When the? Data Specification ?is?Lookup table object  and? the factory default block replacement rule is specified on the 1-D Lookup Table or 2-D Lookup Table blocks, Simulink Design Verifier will incorrectly include the Test Objective/Test Condition/Proof Assumption for break-point and intervals specified by the block parameters related to? Data Specification ?of?Table and breakpoints.

Summary: HDL Coder can use incorrect base in RAM initial value
Description: HDL Coder can generate incorrect code for the initial value of a RAM block under certain circumstances. The design uses a RAM System block (Single Port RAM System, Simple Dual Port RAM System, or Dual Port RAM System block) The RAM System block has a non-default initial value The width of the RAM data is not an exact multiple of 4  If all these conditions are met, the RAM initial value will be emitted in binary, but the base specifier will indicate that the value is in hexadecimal. This may cause a warning or error when compiling the code in a 3rd party simulation or synthesis tool.
Workaround: Change the data width of the RAM to be an exact multiple of 4. This will cause the initial value string and the numeric base specification to match, with both using hexadecimal.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subsystem outputs are?incorrectly cached in local variables across subsystem calls in generated code
Description: Incorrect code may be generated when the?Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to Structure reference  and Optimize global data access  parameter is set to Minimize global data access .  When these two parameters are enabled, global variables corresponding to subsystem outputs may be?incorrectly cached in local variables across calls to subsystems. As a result, a global variable in the function caller (for example, the model_step  function) may not get the changes that the function callee makes to cached variable, producing silent wrong answers.    For example, in this incorrect code, the model_B.subystem1.subsystem2.UnitDelay variable is incorrectly cached to the local variable UnitDelay  in the model_step  function. As a result, the variable model_Y.OutData  gets an older,incorrect value from the UnitDelay  variable.    /* Blocksignals (default storage) */    B_model_Tmodel_B;    /* setslocalB->UnitDelay to 18*/   void model_subsystem2(B_subsystem2_modelType_T *localB)    {    ? localB->UnitDelay = 18;    }    /* setslocalB->subsystem2.UnitDelay to 18 */   void model_subsystem1(B_subsystem1_modelType_T *localB)    {    ? model_subsystem2(&localB->subsystem2);    }    /* Modelstep function */   void model_step(void)    {    ? // cachesthe? value of model_B.subsystem1.subsystem2.UnitDelay    ? // in a localvariable    ? UnitDelay =model_B.subsystem1.subsystem2.UnitDelay;    ? // setsmodel_B.subsystem1.subsystem2.UnitDelay = 18   ? model_subsystem1(&model_B.subsystem1);    ? // makes use ofthe old value of model_B.subsystem1.subsystem2.UnitDelay   ? // rather thanthe value from model_subsystem1    ? model_Y.OutData = UnitDelay;    }

Summary: Custom FFTW callback with C++ code generation causes compiler error
Description: Compilation of the generated code?fails when you specify an FFTW?callback class and?the code generation target language is C++. An FFTW callback class specifies a custom library to use for fast Fourier transform algorithms.

Summary: The Quadcopter Project example does not support PARROT Mambo
Description: The Quadcopter Project example does not support PARROT Mambo.

Summary: Crash when opening test harness for a Stateflow block with requirement links
Description: MATLAB can crash when all of the following conditions are true: There is a test harness on a Stateflow chart, State Transition Table, Truth Table, MATLAB Function block, or Test Sequence block There is a Simulink requirement set with at least one requirement that is linked to an element (e.g., block) inside the above test harness The requirements set and the test harness are opened in sequence

Summary: Code errors in Create a Simulink Report Generator Report Programmatically example
Description: The code for the Create a Simulink Report Generator Report Programmatically example in the Getting Started of the documentation produces errors. The errors are caused by reusing the finder  and section  variable names. Also, the?StateflowDiagramElementFinder  is missing the  chart  input .

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier design error detection analysis may report incorrect objectives count in the Results Summary and Results Inspector window?
Description: During design error detection analysis on contradictory models, Simulink Design Verifier may miscalculate the objectives count? and incorrectly report them in the Results Summary  and Results Inspector  window. ?

Summary: Sporadic error messages are displayed when Diagnostic Viewer attempts to add more than five tabs
Description: If the diagnostic viewer contains five tabs and a new tab is being added (due to any operation on the Simulink model),the oldest tab on the diagnostic viewer is deleted and a new tab is created on the Diagnostic viewer. While doing so, Diagnostic Viewer might not behave as expected and report errors in the MATLAB desktop. An example of the error message is as follows:  Index exceeds matrix dimensions?  Error in slmsgviewer/getTabInfo  Error in slmsgviewer/processStageEndRecord  Error in slmsgviewer/processStageRecord  Error in slmsgviewer.processRecord  This behavior can cause performance issue in model operations and any future diagnostics may not be reported.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible wrong answer when your code passes data by reference in multiple positions that is linearly indexed
Description: When a function call passes array data by reference in multiple positions, and the array data is indirectly defined in at least one place as a result of one-dimensional indexing, the code generator may incorrectly replace different references with the same computation. This replacement can cause the generated code to produce a wrong answer. The most common ways of passing arrays by reference to a function are by using coder.ceval  in your MATLAB code or by calling external code from Stateflow. For example, suppose that you carefully write your code to introduce the variable tmp : tmp = A(:);?  coder.ceval('foo',coder.rref(tmp),coder.rref(A(1)); The code generator may incorrectly eliminate the variable tmp , causing the generated code to pass the address of A for both arguments.

Summary: Slow performance when adding many blocks in Simulink
Description: Adding many blocks to a Simulink model can be slow, such as when you use add_block ?in a MATLAB script,? or copy and paste many blocks. To diagnose this problem, use the MATLAB profiler to profile the MATLAB script or the copy-paste operation. In the profiler report, this problem manifests as a disproportionately large number of calls to the function getHarnessList .
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Change log?February 9, 2018:? attachment_g1707278_16b_2018-02-09.zip: initial version for R2016b.

Summary: Errors and inconsistencies in public command-line API for Simulink Requirements
Description: 1) After creating a new link object via link =  slreq.createLink(source, destination) , or?when using an existing link object, for example via link = requirement.inLinks() ,?you cannot delete a link: >> link.delete???% variable deleted in workspace but the link persists in Simulink Requirements session   >> link.remove?? % throws an error:??   No appropriate method, property, or field 'remove' for class 'slreq.Link'.? ? 2)??slreq.new('ReqSetName')  method does not create a file, and does not check whether a Requirements Set file with the given name already exists. This may cause loss of data when the new Requirements Set is saved. 3)??close()  method for the?slreq.ReqSet  class errors out?if you provide an optional argument to save or discard changes: >> reqSet.close()?? % works as expected?   >> reqSet.close(false)?? % DISCARD CHANGES use case - throws an error:??Too many input arguments    >> reqSet.close(true)?? %?SAVE CHANGES use case - throws an error:??Too many input arguments    4) ?reqSet.find()  method cannot?match results?by SID field value, for example: >> reqSet.find('type', 'Reference', 'SID', 3) ??? % returns empty, even though an slreq.Reference object with SID 3 exists? >> reqSet.find('type', 'Reference', 'SID', '3')? ??? % returns empty, even though an slreq.Reference object with SID 3 exists?

Summary: Diagram reporter does not hyperlink inactive variants
Description: The  Diagram  reporter does not create hyperlinks for inactive variant subsystem blocks in the top-level diagram. The hyperlinks should navigate to the corresponding subsystem diagrams.

Summary: Error when highlighting links to Excel and destination Range is missing or invalid
Description: MATLAB might throw the following error when you highlight requirement links to Excel, if the destination range is missing or invalid: In C:\?Program Files\MATLAB\R2016b\toolbox\? slrequirements\? +rmiref\? @ExcelUtil\? selectCell.m (selectCell) at 31 ?????hRange = hName.RefersToRange;?
Products: Simulink Design Verifier, Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Check

Summary: During the Update operation, whitespace differences between documents are treated as real changes
Description: Whenyou edit the Microsoft Word or Excel document and import it into SimulinkRequirements as rich text content (HTML), the content may differ by the numberor position of various whitespace characters in the HTML. Some of thewhitespace characters include:? - Carriage Return (CR);? - LineFeed (LF) or Newline;? - Non-Breaking Space. In HTML, such whitespace is mostlyinsignificant and can be safely ignored, i.e., the HTML will render mostly asbefore, when viewed in an HTML browser. By not ignoring whitespace, theUpdate operation may indicate many more changes to the requirement descriptionsthan there really are in the documents, when some changes are caused onlybecause of whitespace characters inserted by Microsoft Word or Excel. There isno way, however, to distinguish between changes due to whitespace charactersfrom more meaningful changes. As a result, the change information is triggeredfor all requirements that the Update operation flags as having changed.?
Workaround: Use plain text rather than rich text when importing content from Microsoft Word or Excel. By using plain text, however, the user loses the ability to display tables and images in the requirement descriptions.

Summary: Copying to the clipboard stops working in the Live Editor in Windows 10
Description: Whencopying data to the clipboard from the Live Editor (for example, if you select code or text and type CTRL +C ),MATLAB might throw this warning: WARNING: Error in subscription handler java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: C:\?Users\?<username>\?AppData\?Local\Temp\ConnectorClipboard ... does not exist? After the warning occurs, copying and pasting no longer works in the Live Editor.? The problem occurs only in Windows 10, versions 1709 and above. In these versions, Windows 10 runs a SilentCleanup task?when the system enters an idle state.?SilentCleanup?tries to? free up disk space by removing empty temporary folders, which causes the problem.?
Workaround: To avoid this issue, stopSilentCleanup:1. Open a Command Prompt window as anadministrator.2. Runthis command to disable the?SilentCleanup task:schtasks.exe /change /TN "\Microsoft\Windows\DiskCleanup\SilentCleanup"/Disable3. Restart MATLAB.Note: When you disable the SilentCleanup task,unnecessary files such as temporary files are not automatically deleted. If you disabled the SilentCleanup task, make sure to regulary check for unnecessary files and delete them manually to avoid storing unused temporary files and running out of disk space.?To restart?SilentCleanup:1. Open a Command Prompt window as an administrator.2. Run this command to enable the?SilentCleanup?task:schtasks.exe /change /TN "\Microsoft\Windows\DiskCleanup\SilentCleanup"/Enable

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier report generation may fail with unexpected error
Description: For some models, Simulink Design Verifier may fail to generate a detailed analysis report and displays the following error message: Expected one output from a curly brace or dot indexing expression, ?but there were 7 results.?  Error in Sldv.ReportUtils.getSidFromModelObjectData (line 16)?????????????replacementRuleName = replacementInfoOfModel.RepRuleInfo.RuleName;?Error in Sldv.ReportUtils/ prepareObjectives>buildObjectives (line 88)?????????[sid, ?modelname] = Sldv.ReportUtils.getSidFromModelObjectData(data, ?mo);Error in Sldv.ReportUtils/ prepareObjectives (line 9)?????objectives = buildObjectives(data, ?titles, ?createLink);?Error in Sldv.ReportUtils.generate?Error in sldvreport (line 53)????????[status, ?ActualreportFilePath] = Sldv.ReportUtils.generate(sldvData, options, ?showUI, ?reportFilePath);?Error in urlcall (line 168)?????????[~, ?reportFilePath] = sldvreport(sldvData, ?[], resultPaths.ReportFileName, ?true);

Summary: Inner dimensions must agree?error when generating code for??multiObjectTracker?and?trackingKF
Description: When you generate code for multiObjectTracker  and trackingKF , MATLAB might throw the following error: Inner dimensions must agree. Generated code for a general matrix multiplication at this call site. If this should have been a scalar times a variable-size matrix, the scalar input must be fixed-size.
Workaround: Instead?of using?trackingKF as the tracking filter initialized by the multiObjectTracker, use?trackingEKF?or?trackingUKF?to achieve similar results.To do that, write a state transition function and a measurement function and pass their function handles to?trackingEKF?or?trackingUKF.For example, if KF is a trackingKF initialized with?KF = trackingKF(A,H, ...):1. Write the function myStateTransition:function x_pred = myStateTransition(x, varargin)% Define A here.x_pred = A*x;?end?2. Write the function myMeasurement:?function z = myMeasurement(x, varargin)?% Define H herez = H*x;?end?3. Initialize a UKF with these functions by using:UKF = trackingUKF(@myStateTransition, @myMeasurement, ...)

Summary: Unable to generate HDL code for subsystems with no input ports in a design with clock-rate pipelining enabled
Description: When a Simulink? model has clock-rate pipelining enabled, and contains a Subsystem with no input ports, HDL code generation for the Subsystem can crash MATLAB? with a stack similar to this:? [? 0] 0x0000000352ef0613? ? ? ?bin\win64\pir_core.dll+00263699 pir::AbstractPortManager::reorderPorts+00000099?

Summary: Created link to IBM Rational DOORS Next Generation artifact points to initial stream version of artifact,not the one that was linked
Description: Artifacts link created with IBM Rational DOORS Next Generation in a Project Area under Configuration Management points to the base version of?artifact, instead of the current configured?version.Navigation from Simulink?displays the base version of artifact in the Initial Stream?configuration, instead of the expected linked configuration

Summary: When you import rich text requirements and select the option toignore section numbers, the descriptions of imported requirements still includesection numbers
Description: When you import rich text requirements and select the option toignore section numbers, the descriptions of imported requirements still includesection numbers. Each imported requirement is tied to a specific sectionnumber. When you try to synchronize your requirements set with the externaldocument by using the Update functionality, you will notice that manyrequirement descriptions have changed, including the requirements that werereordered in the external document.? The recommended practice is to ignore section?numbers, in order to handle changes to the external document such as having new requirements inserted before or after previously imported requirements, or having existing requirements moved around within the document, which may change the section numbering, but not change the descriptions of previously imported requirements.
Workaround: Import content from external documents as plain text instead of rich text, if the requirement descriptions do not have tables or images.

Summary: VideoReader?fails to read certain video files on Windows
Description: On Windows platform, reading certain video files using?VideoReader ?may cause MATLAB to crash or to return distorted data.? This has been observed with certain AVI and AVC encoded MPEG-2 TS video files.
Workaround: Turn the?Hardware Acceleration?option off using:?matlab.video.read.UseHardwareAcceleration('off')

Summary: Incorrect red?Non-initialized local variable check?on array of structures field
Description: Polyspace incorrectly detects a red?Non-initialized local variable  (NIVL) check on the field of an array of structures. See for instance this example code snippet. Polyspace detects an incorrect red NIVL check on anarray of structures containing only double  fields, and passed by reference to afunction that initializes all the fields.? typedef struct? _struct_aa  {??? double? dbl_1;??? double? dbl_2;...  }? struct_aa;  void foo(void){??? struct_aa array_aa[MAX];???? init_func(&array_aa[0]); /* initialize all the fields of array in init_func() */??? for(index = 0; index? < MAX; index++)??? {? ? ? ? var_1 = array_aa[index].dbl_1; /* red NIVL on dbl_1?*/????????...??? }}

Summary: Signal object used in referenced model causes error when updating diagram or simulating
Description: If you have a referenced model that uses a signal object (such as Simulink.Signal )?in a Simulink data dictionary (sldd  file), simulating or updating the block diagram might generate an error like this: The Simulink signal object 'ABC' for output port 1 of block 'myModel/myBlock' was changed during simulation.
Workaround: Set the simulation mode of the Model block to Accelerator before updating the diagram or simulating.

To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. Summary: Diagram reporter fails to report on?Stateflow.Box subchart
Description: The  Diagram  reporter fails to produce a snapshot of a Stateflow.Box  subchart and issues an error indicating that the object is an invalid source.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when using the?| operator in if or while condition with empty first argument and scalar second argument
Description: The conditions ? ?if?a | b? ?...? ?end?   and?   ???while a | b? ?...? ?end   are interpreted incorrectly in the generated?code when all of these conditions are true: a  is an empty matrix. b  is a scalar and of?fixed size. logical(b) ?is?true .  In?such cases, the generated code treats the overall value of the condition as true . On the other hand, MATLAB treats?the condition?as  false.  This?bug occurs only?when the operator |  is used in an if  or while  condition.?This bug does not?affect other?usages of the |  operator. This bug affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks and MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Rewrite the if or while condition using the operators || and &&.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier takes longer than expected to display results in the Results Summary window after processing all objectives
Description: Simulink Design Verifier takes longer than expected to display results in the?Results Summary  window after processing all objectives

Summary: Calls to the power builtin with real gpuArray inputs sometimes silently returns wrong answers
Description: A call to the power  builtin function with a fractional double scalar exponent on negative inputs is expected to error out with the message: Error using .^POWER: needs to return a complex result, but this is not supported for real input X and Y on the GPU. Use POWER(COMPLEX(X),COMPLEX(Y,0)) instead.  However, if this call to power  is preceded by another call to power  with a integer scalar exponent, then instead of erroring the second call returns wrong results: >> % The following call returns correct answers and should not error.>> x = gpuArray(-1:10);>> x.^5ans =  Columns 1 through 6          -1           0           1          32         243        1024  Columns 7 through 12        3125        7776       16807       32768       59049      100000>> % The following call should error but does not, and silently returns a wrong answer.>> x.^(0.5)ans =  Columns 1 through 6       NaN         0    1.0000    1.4142    1.7321    2.0000  Columns 7 through 12    2.2361    2.4495    2.6458    2.8284    3.0000    3.1623  As shown in these reproduction steps, the second call to power  returns NaN results in the cases where the imaginary part is not zero.

Summary: Target computer sporadically crashes when model uses serial communication blocks
Description: A target computer running a model that uses serial communication blocks might crash with a bus error in fiforeadwrite.c. You can find out where a crash occurred in your model by running the host command SimulinkRealTime.crashInfo.display after the target reboots.

Summary: Error opening Model Configuration Parameters for models using Polyspace and TargetLink
Description: Opening the Configuration Parameters dialog box for a Simulink model results in an error when all of the following conditions are met:? dSPACE TargetLink is installed. The model is configured to use either Polyspace Bug Finder or Polyspace Code Prover. The model is not configured to use an ERT target.  The error message displayed in the dialog box is: An unexpected error occurred while creating the Configuration Parameters Dialog. This might be caused by functions in your MATLAB path that shadow build-in MATLAB functions.  Undefined function 'getparam' for input arguments of type 'double'. ?  In Simulink versions prior to R2017b, this error occurred only on the All Parameters tab of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer might occur for a reusable export function model that includes a reusable subsystem
Description: The code generator can generate incorrect code for a model?when all of the following conditions are true: The model is an export-function model. If the model is a referenced model, the model configuration parameter?Total number of instances allowed per top model  ?parameter is set to?Multiple .  If the model is a top model, the model configuration parameter? Code interface packaging?  is set to ?Reusable function .  The model includes at least one atomic subsystem with block parameter?Function packaging? set to?Auto ,  Nonreusable function ?or ?Reusable function .  In the generated function interface for the subsystem, the second argument is a pointer to the?real-time model (RT_MODEL ) data structure .  The subsystem function calls a function that belongs to the same subsystem (for example, the subsystem's reset function).   When these conditions exist, the pointer to RT_MODEL  can be incorrectly passed to the?calling function where a pointer to a different?struct  type is expected. Use of the incorrect pointer to a structure type can result in wrong answers.

Summary: Custom Storage Class Designer?supports only?char and logical scalar custom attributes
Description: If you use the Custom Storage Class Designer to?define an advanced custom storage class with your own custom attributes class (derived from?Simulink.CustomStorageClassAttributes ), in the?class definition,?you can set the property type?(PropertyType ) of each attribute only?to?char  or logical scalar . You cannot use other types such as double  or int32 .?

Summary: Simulation Data Inspector might not plot all checked channels for subsequent simulations
Description: With Overwrite Run mode enabled for a run from a simulation that logs non-scalar signals, the Simulation Data Inspector plots only the first channel of a non-scalar signal for subsequent simulations when other channels are checked for plotting.
Workaround: To refresh plots in the Simulation Data Inspector, run Simulink.sdi.refresh in the MATLAB command window.

Summary: Diagram reporter accepts non-subchart?Stateflow.State objects?
Description: The Diagram  reporter accepts?Stateflow.State ?object inputs regardless of whether or not they are subcharts. The Diagram  reporter should accept Stateflow.State  objects only if they are subcharts.

Summary: Invalid timer input args?error when generating PLC code for Stateflow Moore charts that use temporal logic
Description: When you try to generate PLC code for a model that has a Stateflow Moore chart?with temporal logic and a?Simulink.Parameter  argument with non-Auto  storage class, Simulink throws the following assertion error: ?CGIR assertion 'Error: invalid timer input args' failed

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may not decide or give a wrong status to some objectives in a model with Enabled Subsystems or Triggered Subsystems
Description: In a model with an Enabled Subsystem  or a Triggered Subsystem, Simulink Design Verifier may fail to decide or give a wrong status to some objectives that depend on the initial value of the enable or trigger signal. This affect Test Generation, Design Error Detection and Proper Proving analysis.

Summary: webread, webwrite, and matlab.net.http.RequestMessage fail for https connections in an environment behind a firewall that requires web access through a proxy
Description: RESTful and HTTP web access functions work for http connections, but fail for https connections, to a server through a proxy behind a firewall, if the internal DNS cannot resolve the domain name of the server.? The error is similar to: ?? The IP address of "abc.def.com" could not be determined.  where abc.def.com  is the name of the server.?  To confirm that this issue is the reason, all of the following must be true: A proxy is involved. This might be configured in MATLAB preferences or your computer's system settings  There is no error accessing http web sites using the proxy The ping command (issued in a shell or Windows command prompt) cannot resolve the host name
Workaround: In some cases, urlread can be substituted.

Summary: Error loading configuration set from saved MATLAB files
Description: This bug affects configuration sets using one of the following system target files: slrt.tlc tlmgenerator_grt.tlc tlmgenerator_ert.tlc  If a configuration set is exported to a MATLAB (.m) file using the saveAs  API or using the Export menu in Model Explorer, then an error will occur when loading a configuration set from that exported MATLAB file:? Changing property 'ProdLongLongMode' is not allowed
Workaround: Modify the exported MATLAB (.m) file in the MATLAB editor.? Remove the line containing the text:cs.set_param('ProdLongLongMode',?'on');

Summary: Coverage analysis fails for large cascade of Logical Operator blocks
Description: For MCDC analysis, a cascade of Logical Operator blocks is treated as a single, complex logic expression. (This behavior occurs starting with release R2016b.) However, if the expression is too large, the following warning is shown, followed by an error: Warning: MCDC coverage not recorded for "LogicOp_Root", because a logic expression for this model object was too complex.   Depending on the context in which the coverage analysis was initiated, different error messages are shown.?
Workaround: To work around this issue, avoid analyzing a large Logical Operator cascade for MCDC.??One workaround is to simplify the expression represented by the Logical Operator blocks, reducing the size of the cascade (that is, the number of blocks, number of inputs, etc.).If you cannot simplify the expression, consider breaking up the cascade. For example, assume you have two Logical Operator blocks cascaded together. If the output signal of the upstream block is branched and connected to another block (for example, an Outport), then the two logic blocks are not treated as a single expression. Instead, they are analyzed individually for MCDC. See the Logical Operator Cascade Patterns?example for?the criteria of how these cascades are identified.Another workaround is to force Simulink Coverage to analyze all Logical Operator blocks individually for MCDC. The tool does not analyze logic cascades when logic blocks are treated as non-short-circuiting and the Unique-Cause definition of MCDC is being used. (Note that prior to R2017a, all MCDC analyses used Unique-Cause.) Use the following commands to change these settings:set_param(model,'CovLogicBlockShortCircuit','off');set_param(model,'CovMcdcMode','UniqueCause');? ?% Not applicable before R2017aThese settings may affect the MCDC results for other elements in your model. To understand the effects of short-circuiting, see the example?Analyzing MCDC for Cascaded Logic Blocks. For a description of the effects of setting CovMcdcMode, see?Modified Condition and Decision Coverage (MCDC) Definitions in Simulink Coverage.

Summary: Report API cannot report on a model that cannot be compiled
Description: The Report API by default attempts to compile a model before reporting on it. If the model cannot be compiled, report generation terminates.?
Workaround: The Report API's Reporter object has a hidden property that disables this default behavior, thereby?allowing reporting on uncompilable models. To generate a report without compiling the model, set the hidden?CompileModelBeforeReporting property to false. For example,rpt = slreportgen.report.Report("Sample","pdf");?rpt.CompileModelBeforeReporting = false;?model_name = "sf_car";?ch =? mlreportgen.report.Chapter("Title","sf_car Model");load_system(model_name);?diagram = slreportgen.report.Diagram(model_name);?add(ch,diagram);?add(rpt,ch);?close(rpt);?close_system(model_name);rptview(rpt);?
