Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for multitasking mode
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a?model that meets these conditions: In Solver  pane, under Additional parameters , the?Treat each discrete rate as a separate task  parameter is on. In the same pane, the?Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  is off. In the?Diagnostics >Sample Time  pane, the?Multitask rate transition  parameter is set to warning . In the model, at least one root outport block has a different discrete sample time?than the block output port driving it.
Workaround: In the?Solver?pane,?under?Additional parameters,?select the?Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer parameter.

Summary: Diagram reporter hyperlinks fail for Stateflow subchart blocks
Description: Hyperlinks for Stateflow Atomic subchart blocks in the top-level diagram may not navigate to the corresponding subchart diagram. Hyperlinks may also fail for other Stateflow subchart blocks, such as?Simulink.Function , if the subchart diagram is reported before the top-level diagram.

Summary: Figure reporter does not support?SVG as default snapshot format
Description: The Figure  reporter?auto  snapshot format?uses the ?"HG images" option in the MATLAB Report Generator panel of the MATLAB Preferences dialog box to specify the format of a figure snapshot. However, the "HG images" option does not allow you to specify SVG as a format.
Workaround: Explicitly set the?SnapshotFormat property of the Figure reporter to svg.

Summary: Diagram reporter does not support?SVG as a default snapshot format
Description: The?Diagram  reporter's auto  snapshot format?uses the image file format specified in the MATLAB Report Generator panel of MATLAB Preferences. However, this behavior is not consistent with the MATLAB Figure  reporter, which uses SVG as the?auto?snapshot format.
Workaround: Explicitly set the?SnapshotFormat?property of the?Diagram?reporter to?svg.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using units from a disallowed unit system in a Simulink.Parameter object
Description: MATLAB crashes when you run a model or update diagram?when these?conditions are true: The Unit  parameter of a Simulink.Parameter  object is set to a unit system that is not allowed. The Simulink.Parameter  object is passed as a parameter to an S-Function, Stateflow chart, or MATLAB Function block.   Allowed unit systems are specified by the AllowedUnitSystems ?configuration parameter or by a Unit System Configuration block.

Summary: Incorrect handling of matrix concatenation in a MATLAB Function block under strict conditions
Description: Incorrect code generation might occur when using matrix concatenation in a MATLAB Function Block. For this to occur, all of the folowing conditions must be true: ?1.? Matrix concatenation is used on the right-hand side of an assignment. ?2. The matrix concatenation uses a variable that is the same as or depends on the variable used on the left-hand side of the assignment. ?3. The use of the variable from condition 2 appears in an expression such that a new value is produced. For example, if the variable from condition 2 is A, then A + A would be an expression that produces a new value.? ?4. The expression from condition 3 is indexed, either directly or indirectly through a new variable, such that a single element is extracted.? ?For example, consider the following MATLAB code:? function [y1,y2,y3] = foo(in)    y2 = 44;    y3 = 45;        y1 = in(6:3:33, 1:1:1);    P7 = (y1) + (y1);    y1(2:1:10, 1:1:1) = [in(3:6:39, 1:1:1); y1(4, 1); P7(5, 1)];end? Condition 1 is satisfied by: y1(2:1:10, 1:1:1) = [in(3:6:39, 1:1:1); y1(4, 1); P7(5, 1)]; Condition 2 is satisfied by both? y1(4, 1) and P7(5, 1). The latter satisfies condition 2 because P7 depends on y1. Condition 3 is satisfied by? P7 = (y1) + (y1); Condition 4 is satisfied by? P7(5, 1). ?The generated code for this example might be:? ? ? ?--SNIP--? ? ? rtb_P3 = 44.0;? rtb_P5 = 45.0;    for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) {    rtb_P1[i] = xg1716065_model_P.in_IA1_1[3 * i + 5];  }  tmp = rtb_P1[3];  for (i = 0; i < 7; i++) {    rtb_P1[1 + i] = xg1716065_model_P.in_IA1_1[6 * i + 2];  }  rtb_P1[8] = tmp;  rtb_P1[9] = rtb_P1[4] + rtb_P1[4]; ? ? ? --SNIP--?? The addition rtb_P1[4] + rtb_P1[4]  is incorrectly performed after the second loop. In the correct code, the addition is computed and stored in a temporary variable tmp_0  before the second loop:? ? ? ? --SNIP--? ?   rtb_P3 = 44.0;  rtb_P5 = 45.0;  for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) {    rtb_P1[i] = xg1716065_model_P.in_IA1_1[3 * i + 5];  }  tmp = rtb_P1[3];  tmp_0 = rtb_P1[4] + rtb_P1[4];  for (i = 0; i < 7; i++) {    rtb_P1[1 + i] = xg1716065_model_P.in_IA1_1[6 * i + 2];  }  rtb_P1[8] = tmp;  rtb_P1[9] = tmp_0;? ? ? ? --SNIP--? ?

Summary: Job Monitor updates overwrite the UserData property of a parallel.Job
Description: While the Job Monitor is open, any changes to a parallel.Job in your workspace will cause the Job Monitor to automatically update. This updating process overwrites the UserData  property of the changed parallel.Job.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: ModelAdvisor exclusion is not filtering Stateflow library blocks
Description: ModelAdvisor incorrectly reports Stateflow library blocks that should?befiltered by?the user-defined exclusion.?

Summary: Unable to close MATLAB file linked to currently selected Simulink Requirements entity.
Description: When a code range in a MATLAB file is linked to a currently selected requirement in the Requirements Editor, it is not possible to close the file. When you close the file, it is reopened again after a brief moment.

Summary: "Illegal memory access" error when calling functions on gpuArrays with Fermi cards (compute capability of 2.x) using CUDA 9 drivers
Description: When calling some functions on gpuArrays, you can sometimes see the following error message. For example:  >> x = log(gpuArray(1:10))Error using gpuArray/ logAn unexpected error occurred during CUDA execution. The CUDA error was:an illegal memory access was encountered  This has occurred on Fermi cards (compute capability of 2.x) using CUDA 9 drivers in R2017b.The affected list of functions is: logacosacoshatanhacothasinacscasecasech
Workaround: Downgrade to a CUDA 8 driver in the 37x range. Go to?NVIDIA's driver download page, select the link "Beta and Older Drivers", select your GPU, OS, and CUDA 8.0 as the Toolkit. Then look for a driver in the 37x range such as 376.33.Alternatively, precede the call that errors with a superfluous call to mod with your data:>> x = gpuArray(1:10); % Your data.>> mod(x,0);?????????? % Bring in correct memory location.>> y = log(x);???????? % Original erroring function.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Discrete FIR Filter might generate incorrect HDL Code when configured with control ports and certain optimizations enabled
Description: The generated HDL Code for a Discrete FIR Filter might be incorrect when certain optimizations such as adaptive pipelining is enabled, and the Show enable port  is selected and/or External reset  is set to a value other than None .

Summary: polyspace-configure does not catch all header files using mingw32 compiler on Windows 10
Description: An options file created with?polyspace-configure ?does not contain paths to some compiler headers. You see this issue when running?polyspace-configure ?on a build system that uses the compiler g++ 4.9.3 in the mingw32 development environment on Windows 10.? As a result, running Polyspace Bug Finder with the options file causes compilation errors.
Workaround: Installation instructions using R2016b on a Windows system only  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it.   Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.  Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: MATLAB may crash with Casting modes if?Standards Compliant?and signal of?enumerated type is used to specify the number of bits to shift on Shift Arithmetic block
Description: MATLAB may crash when generating code for a Simulink model that meets all of these conditions:  The?model?contains?a Shift Arithmetic block. The Casting Modes  model configuration parameter is set to Standards Compliant . On the Shift Arithmetic block parameters dialog box, the Bits to shift  parameter is set to Input port . The data type of the signal entering the input port that provides the bit shift is an enumerated type derived from Simulink.IntEnumType.
Workaround: Use an enumerated type that is derived from a MATLAB built-in type and not Simulink.IntEnumType or use a data type conversion block to cast the shift source to a type that is smaller than target signed int.

Summary: Incorrect dimension propagation error for Selector block with 'Starting and ending indices (port)' Index Option
Description: During model compilation, Simulink may issue an incorrect dimension propagation error?about Selector blocks if the following conditions exist: Selector block is configured with the index option of Starting and ending indices (port) . The dimension information of the input signal to the data port "U" of the Selector block is under-specified. The dimension information of the output signal from the port "Y" of the Selector block can be derived from its downstream blocks.?
Workaround: As a workaround, use the Signal Specification block to fully specify the dimension information of the input signal to the data port "U" of the Selector block.

Summary: System Requirements block in Simulink model appears empty
Description: If you have a System Requirements block in your Simulink model?for the purpose of displaying?links associated with the model or a subsystem, the block may appear empty when you open the model/subsystem.???
Workaround: Move the block slightly on the canvas to make its contents visible.

Summary: Cannot create requirements traceability links from more then one character range in MATLAB Function code
Description: When you?create a requirement traceability link from a range of code in MATLAB Function block, the first link is created correctly and linked range is highlighted in MATLAB Editor.? But when you try to link another range of characters in the same MATLAB Function, the new link overwrites the previously created link at the first linked range, and second range is not linked and not highlighted in the Editor.?? As the result, you can only link a single character range in a MATLAB Function.?? Unfortunately, this causes loss of data, because every next failed attempt overwrites the previously created links at the first character range.??

Summary: Saving a Simulink model that uses Tunnel line crossing style causes MATLAB to crash
Description: If you use Tunnel  style for your Simulink line crossing style, MATLAB might crash when a model preview is being generated (for example, when you save the model). The crash log states:? Abnormal termination:  std::terminate()
Workaround: Open the Simulink preferences editor pane and change the line crossing style to Line Hop?or None.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Assertion error ?or?incorrect generated code might occur for a model containing a?ForEach subsystem
Description: Incorrect generated code or an assertion might occur for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a Selector or Demux block The Selector or Demux block output connects to the input of a ForEach Subsystem, or the Selector or Demux block is inside the ForEach subsystem and a subsystem inport connects to the block's input.  The ForEach subsystem partitions the input signal that connects to the Selector or Demux blocks into elements or subarrays.   If the partitioned input signal has more than one dimension, instead of generating incorrect code, MATLAB might throw the following assertion error during the build process: assertion 'aMatSubXVec->size() == 1 && aMatSubXVec->at(0).fStepSizes.size() == 1'

Summary: MATLAB crashes on CTRL+C while loading MATLAB class instance from MAT-File
Description: If a CTRL+C is pressed while?loading a MAT-File that contains an instance of a MATLAB class, MATLAB might crash while cleaning up the resources allocated during the load.

Summary: Error using netcdflib?when attempting to read from some OPeNDAP servers
Description: The ncread  function returns an error?when trying to access certain OPeNDAP servers.??For example, the following returns an error: url = 'https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/thredds/dodsC/namanl/201609/20160929/namanl_218_20160929_0000_000.grb';?  x = ncread(url,'x');?  Error messages:? Error using netcdflib?  Unable to open file. File may be corrupt or filename may have invalid characters. If the data source was an OPeNDAP URL, see the?  OPeNDAP Troubleshooting section in the Users Guide.?    Error in netcdf.open (line 59)?  [varargout{:}] = netcdflib ('open', filename, varargin{1} );?    Error in internal.matlab.imagesci.nc/openToRead (line 1278)?  this.ncRootid = netcdf.open(this.Filename,'NOWRITE');?    Error in internal.matlab.imagesci.nc (line 121)?  this.openToRead();?    Error in ncread (line 53)?  ncObj = internal.matlab.imagesci.nc(ncFile);

Summary: Model containing variant subsystem with multiple rates fails during code generation with TLC error
Description: A multi instance simulink model may fail during code generation when all of the following conditions are true:  Model contains variant subsystem with multiple rates  Option Treat each discrete rate as a separate task? in the configuration parameter is selected   The error message is as follows:  The specified index (2) was out of the range 0 - 1?Main program:? ==> [00] matlab/ rtw/ c/ tlc/ mw/ opaquelib.tlc:SLibCG_SystemFcnArgAccessed(4282)??
Workaround: Configure the multi-rate subsystem variant to be non-inlined by setting the subsystem's parameter 'Function packaging' to 'Nonreusable function' or 'Reusable function'

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code?generator might not honor data alignment setting on?output signal of Data Type Conversion block
Description: The code generator does not honor the data alignment setting on the output signal of a Data Type Conversion block when all of the following?conditions are true: Input and output signals of the Data Type Conversion block?are set to the same data type or?inherit the same data type. Data alignment settings for the input and output signals of the Data Type Conversion block differ.  If the compiled code from your compiler for the target processor would result in different numeric results or crash when using an instruction?that requires specific data alignments but the data is not aligned properly,?the generated code can produce numeric results that differ from simulation results or result in crashes when all of the conditions above are met.

Summary: Compilation error: class has no member "type"
Description: When running Polyspace Code Prover on C++?source code, the compilation phase can fail?with an error message such as this: C:\compiler\?usr\? include\? c++\? 4.8.2\? functional,?  line 1697: error: class "std::result_of<std::_Mem_fn<void (std::__future_base::_State_base::*)(std::function<std::__future_base::_State_base::_Ptr_type ()> &,?  bool &)> (std::__future_base::_State_base *,?  std::reference_wrapper<std::function<std::__future_base::_State_base::_Ptr_type ()>>,?  std::reference_wrapper<bool>)>" has no member "type"?   The issue can happen when analyzing two C++ files that include the <functional>  header file.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you confirm closure of a MATLAB app
Description: MATLAB crashes when you confirm closure of an app which uses questdlg  in its close request function (i.e.,?UIFigureCloseRequest  or EditorFigureCloseRequest ).
Workaround: Use?uiconfirm instead of questdlg in the close request function.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: FPGA-in-the-Loop generates wrong reset logic when set to "active-low"
Description: When the reset asserted level is set to"active-low", FPGA-in-the-Loop generates incorrect control logic which?sets reset value?to "low" during simulation. This causes the HDL design to enter?a reset state and generate the wrong simulation result.

Summary: Code inspection might return "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" for Delay block
Description: Code inspection returns "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" if a Delay block is inside a conditional subsystem and the state of the Delay block is expected to be initialized in the reset and init functions.

Summary: Unable to see aggregated coverage data in Simulink Test Manager after consistency errors
Description: When running tests in Simulink Test Manager, the MATLAB command window displays consistency errors, and aggregated coverage does not appear in the Coverage Results  section.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup Table?(n-D) or Prelookup blocks incorrectly share same prelookup function?for some fixed-point inputs and breakpoints
Description: The Lookup Table?(n-D) or Prelookup blocks might incorrectly share the same prelookup?function when the following condition is?true for two or more Lookup Table?(n-D) or Prelookup blocks in a model: Inputs and breakpoints both specify fixed-point data types with the?same?container word length , positive fixed exponent, and slope adjustment factor, but with different word length.  The incorrectly shared prelookup function might lead to wrong results?from the generated code.

Summary: Compiling the generated code may result in warnings or errors regarding the constness of pointer variables
Description: In rare cases, when you compile the generated C or C++ code, you may encounter one or both of the following issues: 1. The generated code dereferences a pointer-to-const and writes to the result. For example: ? ? ? ?? ? ?const int *a;  ? ? ? ? ?[SNIP]  ? ? ? ? ?a[i] = a[j];  ? ? ?The C or C++ compiler will output an error similar to:? ? ? ?Error: assignment of read-only location ? 2. The generated code assigns a pointer-to-const to a pointer. For example: ? ?? ? ? ? int *a;  ? ? ? ? ? const int *b;  ? ? ? ? ? [SNIP]?  ? ? ? ? ? a = b;  ? ? The C or C++ compiler will output an error or warning similar to: ? ? Error: invalid conversion from 'const int*' to `int*`  ? ? or? ? ? Warning: assignment discards ˇ®constˇŻ qualifier from pointer target type ?

Summary: MATLAB can freeze when calling matlab.addons.installedAddons in certain conditions
Description: MATLAB can freeze when calling matlab.addons.installedAddons  in these conditions:  MATLAB is started with the -r  option ?and the function passed as a parameter in turn directly or indirectly calls matlab.addons.installedAddons . In this case, MATLAB freezes at startup while the Command Window is still initializing. A MATLAB script, function, or class installs an add-on using a?matlab.addons  function? (such as matlab.addons.toolbox.installToolbox ) and then immediately calls matlab.addons.installedAddons .
Workaround: If the freeze occurs when running a MATLAB script, function, or class that installs an add-on using a matlab.addons function, avoid?running the?matlab.addons install function?and?matlab.addons.installedAddons?back to back within the file. Instead call them as two separate commands in the Command Window.

Summary: Modifying a model may unexpectedly disable?zero-crossing detections.
Description: In 2017b, for some models that rely on the heuristic algorithm to compute sample times, modifying the models may change the Inport?blocks' compiled?sample times from Continuous to Fixed-In-Minor, and thus unexpectedly disable zero-crossing detections in the model.
Workaround: Specify Continuous sample time in the Inport block. Open the Block Parameters dialog of the Inport block and set Block Parameters >> Signal Attributes >> Sample time (-1 for inherited) to "0".

Summary: Some source files seem to disappear from module when opening project from a previous product release
Description: When you open a project created in a previous release, for instance, open a pre-R2017b project in the R2017b Polyspace user interface, some source files seem to disappear from a project module. The issue typically happens when you add a new include folder to the project.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results and code generation for handle class instances in embedded MATLAB Function blocks
Description: When two (or more) handle class instance variables appear in a MATLAB Function (that is, k1  and k2  below). function y = problematic(u)??? k1 = handleClass(u);??? k2 = handleClass(2*u);??? y = [k1.getValue k2.getValue];  In simulation and the generated code, optimizations may merge these improperly into a single instance of the handle class (note k2  disappears). function y = problematic(u)??? k1 = handleClass(u);??? k1 = handleClass(2*u);??? y = [k1.getValue k1.getValue];  The result is a wrong answer. For an example of this issue, please see the file 'issue_example/issue_1688298_example.slx'  in the attached zip file 'issue_1688298.zip' .
Workaround: To workaround this issue force the constructor of the handle class to be inlined using the coder.inline('always') directive. For an example of the workaround, please see the file 'workaround_example/handleClass.m' and the corresponding model 'workaround_example/workaround_1688298_example.slx' in the attached zip file??'issue_1688298.zip'.

Summary: Order of type definitions within auto generated files is nondeterministic.
Description: When?Embedded Coder generates a file containing multiple?Simulink.AliasType  definitions, the order in which these type definitions appear in the generated code may vary between builds.? For example, if?a model uses 15 Simulink.AliasType  definitions, whose scope is Exported  and the header file?is myHeaderFile.h , the order in which these type definitions appear in the?myHeaderFile.h  file?may change from one build to another build.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In normal simulation mode,an enabled mask parameter on a linked block with mask-on-mask evaluates to model workspace value if it references a model argument
Description: An enabled mask parameter is incorrectly evaluated to model workspace value ,if all of the following conditions are true:  Normal mode of simulation is chosen  The referenced child model contains a linked library block  The linked library block in the child model has a mask directly on another mask  The mask parameter of the linked block references a model argument  The parent model specifies the model argument values?   The enabled mask parameter evaluates to base workspace value for model with Accelerator and Rapid Accelerator simulation mode.

Summary: Accessing the?NumTicksToNextSampleHit parameter of an M S Function without any controllable rates can crash MATLAB
Description: For an M S function without any controllable rates, accessing the parameter NumTicksToNextSampleHit ,?for example to get or set block.NumTicksToNextSampleHit ,?can lead to a crash of MATLAB with the following crash report: Stack Trace (from fault):[  0] 0x000000000a4398df                bin\win64\libmwslexec_simbridge.dll+00366815 slexecGetNumTicksToNextSampleHitForVariableDiscreteRate+00000015[  1] 0x00000000b5aca712                bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+11577106 SLGlue::DockComponentInfo::getType+00217634[  2] 0x00000000b5e1be6f                bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+15056495 SlBdDomainEventHandler::registerListener+00070943[  3] 0x00000000f2ae0b21                bin\win64\SimulinkBlock.dll+00658209 slSimBlock_tag::getNumTicksToNextHitForVariableDiscreteRate+00000033

Summary: Incorrect code may be generated for a For Iterator Subsystem containing an Assignment block with the input port <Y0> enabled
Description: Incorrect code may be generated for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a For Iterator subsystem. The For Iterator subsystem contains an Assignment block with the input port <Y0>  enabled. The immediate data source feeding into the input port <Y0>  of the Assignment block is located outside the For Iterator subsystem. The aforementioned immediate data source is either a Mux block or a Bus Creator block configured to output a virtual bus signal.  In the incorrect generated C/C++ code, ?the variable corresponding to the output of the Assignment block is initialized by an uninitialized local variable.
Workaround: As a workaround, insert a Signal Conversion block with the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter selected in front of the input port <Y0> of the Assignment block.

Summary: U1052?build error when using the Visual Studio 2017 compiler to build code generated using the TMF approach
Description: If you use the Microsoft Visual Studio 2017 compiler to build code generated from a model configured to use the Template Makefile approach, and either "VSCMD_START_DIR" is defined in the environment or?the "%USERPROFILE%\Source" directory exists, Simulink reports a build failure with the following error message: ?NMAKE : fatal error U1052: file '<model_name>.mk' not found?  See?Bug Report ID 1649009 ?for a similar issue when building code from a model configured to use the Microsoft Visual Studio 2017 toolchain.
Workaround: Run the following command after opening MATLAB:>> setenv('VSCMD_START_DIR',?'%CD%');?For more details, please refer to the following link:?https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/content/problem/26780/vsdevcmdbat-changes-the-current-working-directory.html?

Summary: Changing folders between two SIL coverage simulations makes the coverage data incompatible
Description: If you change your current working folder between two Software-in-the-Loop coverage simulations then you can no longer aggregate the corresponding coverage data.
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Design Verifier, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Check

Summary: Pressing Control+S while editing requirements clears all unapplied changes in the current edit field
Description: In the Requirements Editor, any edits made to requirements content are applied automatically when the active focus is moved from the field being edited to another. If you use the Control+S key combination to save your work, your changes in the current edit field are cleared. However, any changes you made in other fields will be saved in the requirements set file.?
Workaround: Click a different area in the Requirements Editor to move the active focus before using Control+S to save the requirements set.

Summary: Execution of real-time models that contain interrupt triggered function-call subsystems can skip execution steps
Description: Real-time model execution steps can be skipped if the following conditions are all true: The model contains a function-call subsystem that is triggered by hardware interrupts The execution mode for the model is interrupt mode, not polling mode The model configuration enables Allow tasks to execute concurrently on target .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing value for enum type subsystem mask parameter in generated PLC code
Description: If a subsystem uses enum  type mask parameter, the value of the parameter is missing in the generated code.
Workaround: Consider using integer type as alternative.

Summary: intlinprog crashes for some problems
Description: If the internal LP preprocessing removes all problem constraints, intlinprog  can crash.
Workaround: Disable LP preprocessing by setting the?LPPreprocess option to 'none' using optimoptions. For example,options = optimoptions('intlinprog','LPPreprocess','none');x = intlinprog(f,intcon,A,b,Aeq,beq,lb,ub,x0,options)

Summary: Incorrect caching of imported rich text content may lead to stale data being used for Import or Update from Microsoft Word or Excel
Description: Simulink Requirements might use stale data for descriptions of requirements?(text, tables, or images) that do not match the current document on disk, under the following scenario: Import requirements as rich text content from a Microsoft Word or Excel document of some timestamp t1. Replace the previously imported document with another document of timestap t2, for example, by reverting a checked-out document, or by extracting it from an archive.  During Import, if t2 is older than t1, Import might read stale cached data that was produced when importing the document at timestamp t1, instead of getting the rich text content directly from the document at timestamp t2.?  Similarly, the Update operation might read the stale cached data, and indicate that there are no changes, even when there are content changes in the document between timestamps t1 and t2.
Workaround: After making changes to your requirements document(s):Close MATLABDelete the folders <TEMP>\RMI\MSWORD and <TEMP>\RMI\MSEXCEL, where <TEMP> is your Windows temp folderRestart MATLABPerform the Import or Update operationYou can locate your Windows temp folder by running the following command at the Windows command prompt:echo %TEMP%

Summary: MATLAB might crash if you press Ctrl+C when graphics are updating
Description: MATLAB might crash with the following assertion error if you press Ctrl+C to cancel a drawnow  operation when graphics are updating. Assertion in void __cdecl `anonymous-namespace'::mwJavaAbort(void) at b:\matlab\src\jmi\javainit.cpp line 1346: Fatal Java Exception.

Summary: Heading truncated when?importing requirementscontents from IBM Rational DOORS
Description: DOORS Object Heading values that are used to populate the Summary field values of imported requirements are truncated to about 50 characters.

Summary: After importing requirements from some Microsoft Word document, loading a previously saved Requirement Set file produces an error
Description: After importing requirements from some Microsoft Word documents, typically ones that contain special characters such as vertical tabs, the user is able to save the Requirement Set successfully. Attempting to load the Requirement Set file (with the .slreqx extension) later on, either through the User Interface or through the command-line, however, may produce an error and the file may fail to load with an XML parser error.  This is because some of these special characters are not valid inside XML files, which are used internally in the slreqx file. If an XML parse error occurs, Simulink Requirements invokes some error recovery code to detect special characters that may have prevented the loading of the file, and replace each such character with a whitespace. The error recovery code, however, did not handle exhaustively all character occurrences. When the error recovery code is triggered, the user may see the following warnings in the MATLAB command-window: Warning: XML parser errors while loading <<REQSET_FILE.slreqx>>. Attempting to recover... Warning: Recovery successful. Replaced each illegal character with a space while loading?<<REQSET_FILE.slreqx>>. Save the Requirement Set to accept changes.

Summary: Update diagram during simulation can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB can crash under the following conditions: A?model contains a block that converts a parameter with an enumerated data type to an alias of the enumerated data type.  While simulating the model (to update parameter values), you do an update diagram.  This?issue can also occur if you run multiple simulations in fast restart on the same model described above.
Workaround: Use enumerated data types directly in your models instead of?aliases to enumerated data types.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check errors for non-scalar Boolean operations in assignment statements in C Charts
Description: Beginning in R2017b, scalar Boolean operations in assignment statements (such as those found in Stateflow states and transitions) are analyzed for coverage as well as for test generation via Simulink Design Verfier.? Simulink Coverage does not support Boolean operations with one or more non-scalar operands, and assignments containing such operations are not analyzed for coverage. Note that non-scalar Boolean operations are not valid MATLAB syntax but can be found in Stateflow C Charts. Due to a bug in Simulink Design Verifier, the following error will occur when attempting to perform a Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check on a model containing a non-scalar Boolean operation in an assignment statement in a C Chart. Checking compatibility...  Errors occurred during translation:  fromPointerType   ?As such, some models that were compatible with Simulink Design Verifier in previous releases are shown as incompatible in R2017b.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulating referenced model with shared local data store in atomic subsystem gives incorrect result
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy in normal mode might produce incorrect results when all of these conditions apply:? The top model in the model hierarchy contains Model blocks that simulate in normal mode. A referenced model contains Data Store Memory blocks that have the Share across model instances ?parameter selected. The Data Store Memory blocks are inside atomic subsystems.

Summary: Computer can become unresponsive while or after running a Simulink Desktop Real-Time model
Description: When you are executing a real-time application using the Simulink Desktop Real-Time product on Microsoft Windows 10, the computer sometimes starts to respond very slowly or becomes unresponsive.  If you encounter issues similar to this in R2018a, please install the latest?R2018a Update ,?as this contains additional fixes for similar bugs.
Workaround: Please follow the instructions in Bug Report 1719571 to install a workaround for this and other issues related to the real-time kernel.?

Summary: Simultaneous navigation and deletion crashes MATLAB
Description: MATLAB might crash if you navigate inside a block with an open action, such as a Subsystem, and then quickly attempt to delete something using the Delete key or another keyboard shortcut.

Summary: No "backlink" from DOORS Next Generation requirement artifacts to design objects in MATLAB/Simulink when trying to use bi-directional linking from MATLAB/Simulink side
Description: When creating traceability link from MATLAB/Simulink to a requirement in DNG (DOORS Next Generation), the "backlink" from DNG to Simulink is not automatically created when DNG Requirements Project Area has Configuration Management option enabled. There is no error or warning,? one-way link from Simulink to DNG is successfully created, as if the 2-way linking option is disabled.

Summary: Incorrect terminate event logging in accelerate mode when a test harness model containing IRT?uses dataset logging
Description: If you simulate in Accelerator mode a test harness model containing Initialize Reset Terminate subsystems, dataset logging will incorrectly log terminate events.

Summary: Improper PWM Generator configuration for example?BoostConverterFRSPExample
Description: In the Control Design of a Boost Converter Using Frequency Response Data ?example, the block parameter settings for the PWM Generator block are not properly configured. As a result, users?cannot replicate the?example.
Workaround: Before running the example, in the PWM Generator block, set the Timer period (s) parameter?to 5e-6.

Summary: DMR client's dictionary function threw std::exception 'Invalid access'?error during simulation
Description: When you simulate a model with a dashboard Display block bound to a signal with dimensions 1x1, Simulink throws the following error and then MATLAB becomes unresponsive. DMR client's dictionary function threw std::exception 'Invalid access'?
Workaround: Change the signal dimensions bound to the dashboard Display block to scalar 1 using the Reshape block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when custom storage class is on a signal with a constant sample time
Description: Signals with custom storage classes that have constant sample time?are not correctly initialized in?these cases: Signal is the output of a Merge block Signal is the output of a Concatenate block?and?has one?dimension or concatenates columns Custom storage class is the output of a block (excluding Model Reference or Atomic Subsystem blocks)?that has constant sample time and non-constant sample time outputs.  When the bug occurs, the?generated code does not initialize the custom storage class resulting in the wrong value?for the signal with the custom?storage class, as well as incorrect values for signals downstream of the custom storage class. For example, a model contains a custom storage class SC  that is the output of a?Concatenate block and all inputs?are constant. The output of the?Concatenate block?is an input to an Add block. The code for this model might look as follows, where SC is   in the step function but never written to in either the model_step  or the model_initialize  functions: void model_step(void) {...?? /* Add: '<Root>/Add' */???rtb_add = SC + rtb_product;... } The?model_initialize  function should have the following code, but does not when the bug occurs void model_initialize(void) {?...? /* ?custom signals */? SC = 3;...?}
Workaround: These are possible workarounds:Remove the custom storage class.Insert another block before the signal with the custom storage class, such as a Signal Conversion block?in which?the Exclude Block Reduction parameter?is set.

Summary: writetable displays a?popup window asking for permission to overwrite Excel file
Description: When writing a table?to an?XLSM  file (a spreadsheet file that contains macros) using writetable, ?Excel? displays a popup window?asking for permission to overwrite the file.  For instance, if you have an XLSM  file called test.xlsm  and you use writetable  to write a sample table to this file, Excel? displays a popup?asking for permission to replace the file.? >> T = table({'M';'M';'F';'F';'F'},[38;43;38;40;49],[71;69;64;67;64],[176;163;131;133;119]);?  >> writetable(T,'test.xlsm');

Summary: Connecting?Function-Call Feedback Latch blocks to multiple function-call subsystems with the same block name causes Simulink to crash during simulation
Description: Simulink may crash during model simulation when the following conditions are both true: Two function-call subsystems, situated within separate subsystems, have the same block name. For some value of N , the N th input ports of both function-call subsystems are connected to Function-Call Feedback Latch blocks.
Workaround: Change the name of one of the function-call subsystems so that the names no longer match.

Summary: Calling cellfun with character vector as only input in try block crashes MATLAB
Description: There is a cellfun  syntax where the first argument is one of a limited set of character vectors ?('isempty' , 'islogical' , 'isreal' , 'length' , 'ndims' , 'prodofsize' , 'size' , and 'isclass' ), provided for backwards-compatibility. If cellfun  is called with a character vector as the first and only argument, and the cellfun  call is in a try  block, then MATLAB crashes when the code is executed several times in a row. The input to cellfun ?can be any character vector, even one that does not match any of the valid function names listed above. MATLAB should error in this situation, indicating that the function has too few inputs. This situation is most likely to arise in a script where cellfun  is being called inside of a?try ?block, where the second input argument to cellfun  is a cell array, and colon-expanding that array happens to result in an empty array: try  out = cellfun('islogical', c{:})?   catch ME  ...?   end?  ?If c  happens to be c = {} , then MATLAB crashes.?
Workaround: Avoid calling cellfun with too few inputs when the user function is specified by a character vector rather than function handle.In the try/catch example above, the crash condition can be avoided by first checking that c is not empty, and then calling cellfun only if?c is not empty. ??if ~isempty(c)tryout = cellfun('islogical', c{:})?catch ME...?endend?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code for modeling pattern?containing?a function within a?for?loop that only reads global variables
Description: The code generator contains an optimization that identifies invariant code in a?for ?loop and moves this code before the?for ?loop if the move preserves the program semantics. For some modeling patterns,?the code generator may incorrectly?apply?this optimization to a function that reads a global variable. For an example, please see the attached model 'issue_1678624' . The MATLAB Function block in this model?example_1678624 contains  a ? function?readG1 that?  is called?within a loop .?readG1 ?reads the global variable?g1? in its function definition . In this situation, it is not safe for the code generator to move the call to?readG1 ?before the?for ?loop due to the other statement in the loop?g1(k) = u1(k) + 1; . ?The incorrectly optimized generated code can be seen below.  ? issue_1678624_readG1(locData);  ? for (k = 0; k < 10; k++) {  ? ? issue_1678624_DW.g1[k] = issue_1678624_U.In1 + 1.0;  ? }
Workaround: To prevent the optimization from incorrectly moving the function call, make it appear that the function reading the global calls custom C code. Please see the attached model 'workaround_1678624'. In the MATLAB Function block?example_1678624, note the extra function definition fakeSideEffect and the call to this function within the definition?of readG1.

Summary: Test harness created with a Test Sequence block source may error out when updating the model
Description: If you create a test harness for a subsystem or model that has multidimensional inputs, and choose Test Sequence as the source, the test harness will error out with the following error while updating the model: ??Expression or statement is incorrect--possibly unbalanced (, {, or [.

Summary: Toolchain definition in?Build 32-bit DLL on 64-bit Windows? Platform Using MSVC Toolchain example contains an error
Description: In the example?Build 32-bit DLL on 64-bit Windows? Platform Using MSVC Toolchain , the toolchain definition?contains an error. This error can cause generated code to crash. The?toolchain directs the?MSVC compiler to be invoked with preprocessor definitions, which should only be used when compiling 64-bit objects and not?32-bit objects.?This?results in?unexpected runtime behavior, such as wrong answers or access violations.?In addition, if you use the toolchain to link an executable, the toolchain directs the MSVC linker to link against Windows 64-bit system libraries instead of 32-bit system libraries. This linking?results in run-time access violations. A previous version of this example?appeared in the Simulink Coder documentation entitled Add MSVC Toolchain on 64-bit Windows? to Build 32-bit DLL .

Summary: MATLAB might?crash when custom storage classes are used in transition labels
Description: MATLAB might crash during code generation for a Stateflow chart that meets all of the following conditions: Two or more transitions leave a junction or state. Those transitions compare the same expression to different constants. At least one of the constants is a custom storage class. At least one of the transitions does not contain condition-actions.
Workaround: There are two possible work arounds:?Provide a condition-action (such as a comment) instead of leaving the condition-action empty.Avoid using custom storage classes in transitions whose conditions share expressions with other transitions.

Summary: Model reference coverage not collected with fast restart and test iterations
Description: Coverage is not collected for model references if?Run test iterations in fast restart  isenabled when running test iterations in Simulink Test Manager.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Within a nested function inside a method, properties might be resolved incorrectly
Description: A nested function accessing a property of a containing class finds a subclass' property of the same name, rather than the containing class's property.? For example: classdef Super    properties (Access = private)        property    end    methods        function property = getSuper(this)            function result = nested                result = this.property;            end            property = nested;        end    endend and classdef Sub < Super    properties        property    endend A call to getSuper from an instance of Sub will find Sub's property, rather than Super's property. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?

Summary: Invalid or deleted object?error message for Model Advisor check "Check Stateflow charts for strong data typing"
Description: Model Advisor check "Check     Stateflow charts for strong data typing" (check IDs mathworks.do178.hisf_0015      and mathworks.iec61508.hisf_0015 ) errors with message Abnormal exit:     Invalid or deleted object .

Summary: Errors or warnings about system time zone thrown on macOS High Sierra (version 10.13) or later
Description: If you run MATLAB on macOS High Sierra (version 10.13) or later, and you create a datetime array and specify its time zone as 'local' , then it might throw errors or warnings that refer to the system time zone setting. These errors or warnings can occur even when the system time zone is set correctly. You might see an error message similar to: Error using datetime (line 522)  The system time zone setting, 'local', is not valid. See the datetime.TimeZone property for details about specifying time zones.   Or a warning message similar to: Warning: The system time zone setting, 'US/Eastern', does not specify a single time zone unambiguously. It will be treated as 'America/New_York'. See the datetime.TimeZone property for details about specifying time zones.   when your system's time zone is not set to?US/Eastern . Also, if you use the?parpool ? command, it might throw an error or warning such as those shown above. The Parallel Computing Toolbox uses the datetime class during initialization.
Workaround: To work around the bug described in this report, set the?TZ?environment variable to your desired time zone before starting MATLAB. For example, to set TZ to the America/Detroit time zone, add?the following?command to your?MATLAB startup file:setenv('TZ','America/Detroit')To?find the correct time zone name for your location, use the timezones?function in MATLAB.If you run MATLAB from a terminal window, you can also use a command similar to this (using csh):% setenv TZ America/Detroit; matlabor this (using?bash):$ export TZ=America/Detroit; matlab?

Summary: fread?and binblockread are slow when reading large data sizes
Description: Using fread  or binblockread? with interface objects (serial, tcpip, udp, bluetooth, gpib  and  visa ) to read a large amount of data is slow if the Terminator   is not manually set to empty (''). For example, reading a 5 MB data block can take up to 7 seconds.??
Workaround: Set the Terminator?property to '' (null) before using fread or binblockread to ensure efficient throughput of binary data. For example:t = tcpip('localhost',4020);t.Terminator = '';

Summary: Figure reporter produces image too big to fit on report page
Description: A Figure reporter can produce an image of a MATLAB figure too big to report on a page, depending on the size of the figure.

Summary: Diagram reporter produces images too big to fit on a page
Description: The Diagram reporter can produce images of block diagrams and charts that are too big to fit on a report page, depending on the size of the block diagram or chart. The resulting image is cut off in Word reports and flows onto the next page in PDF reports.

Summary: MATLAB tools might fail to open when using a proxy server
Description: Some MATLAB tools might fail to open when MATLAB is using an authenticated proxy server. These tools include: App Designer Simulink Data Inspector Root Inport Mapping Simulink Template Gallery Export Model to Template Add-Ons Explorer Simulink Start Page

Summary: Generated CUDA code for imerode and imdilate functions?might produce results that mismatch with MATLAB
Description: Simulation results of the generated CUDA code might mismatch with that of the MATLAB code if the following conditions are met: The MATLAB code uses Image Processing Toolbox functions imerode  and imdilate . The input to the functions is 1D. The size of the structuring element (strel  object)? is 1x17  or greater.

Summary: MATLAB fails to launch with Windows user profile containing Unicode characters
Description: If?you have?Chinese, Japanese, or Korean (CJK) characters in your user profile, then MATLAB cannot be launched on a Windows machine. Nothing happens when you double click the MATLAB.exe file in Windows Explorer

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using?delete_block with an open model window
Description: MATLAB might crash if you use the?delete_block ?command when a model that includes the block is visible in the Simulink Editor.
Workaround: Delete any signals associated with the block by using the?delete_line?command before deleting the block itself; or,Close any open Editor windows before using?delete_block.

Summary: Dragging a signal from a Bus Selector can cause?Simulink to respond very slowly
Description: While dragging a signal from a Bus Selector block, Simulink can respond very slowly and appear to stop responding. This condition occurs when the bus is fed by custom S-functions.
Workaround: Install and extract the attached file, as described in the instructions. Then run the command disable_automatic_port_creation at the beginning of each MATLAB session (you can automate this by adding it to your startup.m script).Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Variant preprocessor conditionals might not error check all usages of a Simulink.Parameter
Description: For nested variant subsystems, the preprocessor conditionals might not?contain error checks for all uses of a Simulink.Parameter in the generated code. The preprocessor conditionals contain the error check for only the first use of the Simulink.Parameter .?The Simulink.Parameters  specify variant controls. The error?checks?that these parameters are defined.?

Summary: Code generation for mldivide and mrdivide might error when?the coefficient?matrix is not square and contains nonfinite values
Description: Code generation for?mldivide  and mrdivide ,?and?functions that?depend on them such as detrend , might error?if?any one of the following conditions?is true: The coefficient matrix is variable-size, is not square, and contains nonfinite values. The coefficient matrix is fixed-size, is not square, has at least 64 elements, and contains nonfinite values.  MATLAB returns an array of all NaN values in such situations.? This issue?affects generated MEX files and MATLAB Function Blocks in Simulink model simulations.?It does not?affect the code generated for LIB, DLL, HDL, or EXE targets.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Function input arguments of a character vector type are incorrectly padded in a MATLAB Discrete-Event System in Simulate using Code Generation mode
Description: In a MATLAB Discrete-Event System in Simulate using Code Generation  mode, the input arguments (or structure fields of the arguments)?of a character vector type?for the following methods are incorrectly padded to a fixed length with  char(0). This might result in incorrect simulation results?for the?methods using these arguments and?you might get different results compared to using?Simulate using Interpreted execution ?mode.  [entity,events] = blocked(obj,storage,entity,destination):?destination.type ;  [entity,events] = entry(obj,storage,entity,source):?source.type ;  [events] = exit(obj,storage ,entity,destination):?destination.type ;  [entity,events] = generate(obj,storage,entity,tag): tag ;  [entity,events,next] = iterate(obj,storage,entity,tag,cur): tag ;  [entity,events] = timer(obj,storage,entity,tag) : tag .  For example, ?if an entity generation event is already scheduled with a tag 'new'   , ?in the generate  method specifying the event action, ?the comparison of the tag with 'new'  returns false  in Simulate using Code Generation  mode:? function [entity, events] = generate(obj, storage, entity, tag)?????events = obj.initEventArray;?????disp(strcmp(tag, 'new'));?end?
Workaround: For a MATLAB Discrete-Event System?in Simulate using Code Generation mode, if a function input argument of a character vector type is used, remove?all?char(0) characters?from the argument strArg as the?following:str =?strArg?(strArg?~= char(0));and use str to replace the argument?strArg in your subsequent code.

Summary: Incorrect preprocessor conditionals generated when Simulink.LookupTable object?containing fixed-point data?uses dimension variants
Description: When you use dimension variants with a Simulink.LookupTable   object containing fixed-point data, Embedded Coder can generate?incorrect preprocessor conditional statements. The incorrect generation occurs under?these two?scenarios. Scenario 1 ? If?a dimension symbol used in a lookup table object is a Simulink.Parameter  object named?symbolicDim, t  he incorrect generation occurs when all of these conditions are met:  A breakpoint set uses symbolicDim  as a dimension length.  The table data uses symbolicDim  as the length of the dimension corresponding to the breakpoint set. The table data uses a fixed-point data type while the breakpoint set uses a floating-point data type, or vice versa.  For the parameter object, suppose the  Min  property is set to 2  and Value  is set to 4 .  Under these conditions, the  expected preprocessor conditional is: #if symbolicDim <= 1  # error "The preprocessor definition?'symbolicDim' must be greater than '1'"?  #endif  However, the code generator produces:? #if symbolicDim !=?4  # error "The preprocessor definition 'symbolicDim' must be equal to '4'"?  #endif  Scenario 2   Suppose a breakpoint set and the table data both?use fixed-point data types. If the breakpoint set uses a dimension symbol breakpointDim  and the corresponding dimension of the table data uses a different?dimension symbol tableDim , the code generator omits an expected preprocessor conditional statement that confirms the equivalence of breakpointDim  and tableDim . The expected preprocessor conditional is: #if breakpointDim != tableDim?  # error?"The preprocessor definitions?'breakpointDim' and 'tableDim' must be equal"?  #endif?

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier might report the objective status as "Undecided due to runtime error" even though the status is "Satisfied" in some cases
Description: If the top model satisfies both the following conditions,Simulink Design Verifier might set the status of some of the objectives to ˇ°Undecided due to runtime errorˇ±  even if the objective is actually  ˇ°Satisfiedˇ±  The top model has more than one referenced models. At least one (but not all) of the referenced models     have?Configuration Parameters > Data Import/Export >Format ?set to "Dataset".
Workaround: Either all the referenced models should have?Configuration Parameters > Data Import/Export >Format set to?"Dataset"?or none of the referenced models should have?Configuration Parameters > Data Import/Export >Format?set to?"Dataset".

Summary: Saving project throws "file not found on disk" error
Description: Saving a project might present an error dialog that prevents the project from being saved. The error dialog contains the following text: Cannot save project file. File <filepath>.view was not found on disk. ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect variable reuse when a Unit Delay input or output is connected to a subystem's output signal and the?Optimize global data access parameter is set to a value other than none
Description: In the code, the state variable for a Unit Delay block might be incorrectly reused, leading to wrong answers, if all of the following conditions are true:? Optimize global data access ?configuration parameter is set to?Use global to hold temporary results ?or?Minimize global data access . Reuse local block outputs ?configuration parameter is enabled. A model contains a subsystem in which the?Function packaging ?parameter is set to?Auto ?or?Reusable, or is set to  NonReusable?and? Function interface  ?is set?to  Allow Arguments.   A Unit Delay block's input or output is connected to that subsystem's output signal.    In releases R2015b to R2017b, Delay blocks configured with Length  = 1 are also affected by this bug. Errors might be thrown during the build process.? See the attached?BugReport1668727Details.zip for more information, including models that demonstrate the issue.
Workaround: To work around this issue, as well as the those described in Bug Report IDs?1284809, 1286055, 1287804, 1301976, 1373141, 1649889, 1658667, 1771523, 1773229, and 1773232, do one of the following:?Unzip the contents of attachment_1668727_15a_thru_17b_2018-04-10.zip to a folder on the MATLAB path. Each time you start MATLAB, enter the following in the MATLAB Command Window:>>? workaroundForBugReport1668727Set the?Optimize global data access?configuration parameter to?None. Note that changing this configuration parameter setting disables more optimizations than the above workaround and can result in less efficient code.?

Summary: Error when using arrayfun and some element-wise functions withgpuArray input arguments and GPUs withcompute capability 2.0 or 2.1
Description: If you call arrayfun  with a gpuArray  as input argument and have an NVIDIA? Fermi GPU  with computecapability 2.0 or 2.1 selected, then arrayfun  returns the following error: Error using gpuArray/ arrayfun?  Requested code-table for unsupported compute-capability: 2.1  or the equivalent for GPUs with compute capability 2.0. Also,a significant number of element-wise functions, including?sqrt , power , log10,   and eps   cause the following error whenused with a gpuArray   i nput argument: An unexpected error occurred during CUDA execution. The CUDA error was:  CUDA_ERROR_UNKNOWN  To check the computecapability of your GPU, enter gpuDevice   at the command line.  Examinethe?ComputeCapability f  ield of theoutput. Summary: Error when trying to generate code for deep learning networks on Windows
Description: On Windows, MATLAB throws an error when you try to generate code for networks that contain layers such as leaky ReLU or batch normalization. For example, using the codegen  function to generate code for a YOLO network results in the following error: ?? >> codegen -config coder.gpuConfig('mex')? yolo_predict.m? -args {ones(448,448,3)}  ------------------------------------------------------------------------  gmake: *** No rule to make target '$codegen_dir\MWLeakyReLULayerImpl.obj', needed by 'mnisteg_mex.mexw64'.? Stop.  ------------------------------------------------------------------------  ??? Build error: C++ compiler produced errors. See the Build Log for further details.  Code generation failed.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect result of increment (++) and decrement (--) operations on fixed-point values in C action language charts
Description: If you perform an increment or decrement operation on a fixed-point value in a C Action Language chart,?the value is increased (or decreased) by an incorrect amount.  The expected behavior of x++  before saturation, if any, is: ?x = x + (1 << offset_of_binary_point)   And similarly, a subtraction is expected for the decrement operator x-- .? Compound operators such as += and -= are also affected by this issue.?
Workaround: In the chart, replace each instance of x++ with x = x + 1 and each instance of x-- with x = x - 1.

Summary: When clicking on a Simulink model and using quick-insert to insert an Inport block, it is possible to choose the RF Blockset Inport block even if you don't have an RF Blockset license.
Description: Without an RF Blockset license, you are unable to delete the block within Simulink.
Workaround: ?Select the block in the model, and use the delete_block(gcb) command at the MATLAB command line.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when running SIL/PIL simulation of AUTOSAR model that contains QueuedExplicitReceive ports
Description: If an AUTOSAR?model contains?a mix of QueuedExplicitReceive input ports and other data ports, MATLAB might crash when you run a SIL or PIL simulation of the model.

Summary: Divide block can produce unexpected results or cause MATLAB crash when configured for complex signed integer division
Description: Divide block can produce unexpected results or cause MATLAB crash when configured for complex signed integer division. Improper integer overflow happens when the Divide block attempts to divide the minimum value of a signed integer type by the function?complex(-1)  or a complex number in which the real part is -1 .?When in1 = int32(-2^31); in2=int32([i, -1]) , this overflow can cause MATLAB?to crash.?  Another example?of an integer overflow can?be caused?when the Divide block Saturate on integer overflow  parameter is selected and?in1 = int8(-128); in2=int8([i, -1])  produces out(1) = -128  instead of 127.   The Divide block produces unexpected results when dividing the maximum value of a signed integer type by complex(-1)  or a complex number in which the real part is -1 .  For example,?in1 = int8(127); in2 = int8([i, -1]) ?produces?out(1) = -128 ?instead of?-127 . Similarly,?in1 = Int16(2^15-1); in2 = int16([i, -1]) ?produces?out(1) = -2^15 ?instead of?-2^15+1 .?
