Summary: Simulink DesignVerifier design error detection analysis fails to produce results with an Unexpected Internal Error message in the Results Summary window
Description: During design errordetection for models that contain Stateflow charts containing chart variable ofbus datatype, Simulink Design Verifier analysis may fail to produce resultswith an Unexpected Internal Error  message in the Results Summary window.?

Summary: Deleting a figure with many?Area objects is slower in R2017a than in previous releases
Description: Deleting a Figure ?or?Axes ?object containing a large number of Area  objects is slower in R2017a than in previous releases. In R2017a, the time required to delete the axes or figure scales proportional to the number of Area  objects squared.
Workaround: If you are usingR2017a, follow the instructions below to install the attachment to work aroundthis issue:  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Change log:?November 28, 2017: attachment_1663795_17a_2017-11-28.zip: initial version

Summary: Non-ASCII characters are not supported for Solver Configuration, Simulink-PS Converter and PS-Simulink Converter blocks
Description: If a block name for Solver Configuration, Simulink-PS Converter, or PS-Simulink Converter contains non-ASCII characters, then the model simulation fails with an error.
Workaround: Rename the Solver Configuration, Simulink-PS Converter, and PS-Simulink Converter blocks in your model so that they use ASCII characters only.

Summary: Deployed apps using Text Analytics Toolbox might not work
Description: When using MATLAB Compiler with Text Analytics Toolbox functions, the resulting apps might not work. Trying to run them results in error messages such as the following:? Undefined variable "textanalytics" or class "textanalytics.internal.erasePunctuation".?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constants blocks in Simulink Functions that are called from Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function blocks do not execute correctly
Description: This issue can occur when your model meets all the following conditions: A Constant block exists in a Simulink Function.? The Sample time  parameter for the Constant block is set to Inf . The Simulink Function is called by a Function Caller block inside an Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block.  Under these conditions, the Constant block outputs a zero, especially when the Simulink Function is called from the Initialize Function block. This is because the initialization phase of Simulink starts before the Constant block parameters are evaluated.
Workaround: For all Constant blocks inside Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function blocks, set their Sample time parameter value to -1.

Summary: Crash during Accelerator simulation of referenced model that uses MATLAB structure
Description: When you simulate a model, MATLAB crashes if all of these conditions are true:? The model contains a Model block that simulates in Accelerator mode. The referenced model uses?a MATLAB structure to set?block parameter values. The structure contains a field whose name is a?reserved keyword?for code generation, such as int8_T .
Workaround: Add a non-numeric field to the structure. For example, add a field whose value is an empty cell array. At the command prompt:>> ?myStructuredVar.dummyField = {};

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using certain formats with the linuxvideo adaptor
Description: When using the linuxvideo  adaptor with videoinput  or imaqtool , MATLAB might crash with the following crash log: Stack Trace (from fault): [  0] 0x00007f499071877f /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+00219007 gsignal+00000159  [  1] 0x00007f499071a37a /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6+00226170 abort+00000362  [  2] 0x00007f4942af8135 /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libglib-2.0.so.0+00463157 g_assertion_message+00000325 [  3] 0x00007f4942af81ca /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libglib-2.0.so.0+00463306 g_assertion_message_expr+00000074[  4] 0x00007f4834fe1ba9 toolbox/imaq/supportpackages/genericvideo/adaptor/glnxa64/mwlinuxvideoimaq.so+00060329

Summary: Callback registered for a numeric rtwoptions?parameter?in System Target File is never invoked
Description: When an rtwoptions parameter defined in a System Target File file is a numeric parameter, the callback function attached to that parameter cannot be invoked. Thus, changes in the associated UI element in the Model Configuration Parameters have no effect.

Summary: Cannot convert parameter InitialCondition from data type 'double' error when creating a test harness
Description: If you create a test harness for a subsystem or model that has enumeration data output, and choose?Test Sequence?as the source, compiling the test harness fails with this error: 'BLOCK' cannot convert parameter InitialCondition from data type 'double' to data type 'ENUMTYPE' because conversion of parameters with enumerated data types is not supported
Workaround: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code?generator does not honor data alignment?requirement when model uses continuous time
Description: The code generator does not honor a data alignment requirement when all of the following conditions?are true: Model contains blocks that use or generate continuous time, such as an integrator block. Model configuration parameter Support continuous time  is selected. Model has a?data alignment requirement specified by a Simulink signal property?or a custom code replacement library (CRL).  If the compiled code from your compiler for the target processor would result in different numeric results or crash when using an instruction?that requires specific data alignments but the data is not aligned properly,?the generated code can produce numeric results that differ from simulation results or result in crashes when all of the conditions above are met.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for model with Ignore custom storage classes parameter set to on
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a model that meets these conditions: A signal with a custom storage class other than auto drives a root output port. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Ignore custom storage classes  parameter is selected.  The incorrect generated code does not contain a write/update to the variable associated with the root output port.
Workaround: Clear the Ignore custom storage classes parameter.

Summary: After updating a Requirement Set from an external source, some users may encounter an error while using the application; or links may get resolved to incorrect requirements
Description: After invoking the Update feature on a Requirement Set, some users may encounter an error while continuing to use the application.This situation may occur if some requirements were deleted in the external source, and there are links to these now deleted requirements. The following errors (an error dialog as well as errors displayed in the MATLAB command window) may be encountered while continuing to use the application, in particular, when switching from the Requirements View to the Links View, or when saving the updated LinkSets either directly (File > Save All ) or by saving the model. The error dialog may say:? Error:slreq.gui.Toolbar.viewChangeCallback  Insufficient number of outputs from right hand side of equal sign to satisfy assignment.  If the user restarts MATLAB, and re-loads the updated Requirement Set and the Link Sets, previous links to the now deleted requirements may get resolved to refer to incorrect requirements instead of appearing as invalid or dangling links Those links will have to be deleted and re-created to refer to the correct requirements.

Summary: Error when building a model with a lookup table custom parameter of ?Simulink.AliasType
Description: Simulink throws an error when you try to build a model containing Lookup Table blocks when all of the following conditions are true:? A Lookup Table block uses a custom parameter or Simulink.Parameter  that has Data Type set as?Simulink.AliasType . Configuration parameter?Code Generation > Interface > Shared code placement ?is set to Shared location  or the model generates utility functions that go to the 'slprj'  folder.
Workaround: To work around the issue, do either of the following:For non-model-reference target builds, set Code Generation > Interface > Shared code placement to Auto.Set the Data Type field of Simulink.Parameter to the base type of the?Simulink.AliasType.

Summary: Documentation implies that function prototype control supports reentrant code
Description: Some documentation pages imply that when you generate reentrant code by setting Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Interface > Code interface packaging  to Reusable function, you can control the characteristics of the arguments of the model entry-point functions step  and initialize? by applying function prototype control to the model. However, to apply function prototype control, you must?set Code interface packaging  to Nonreusable function or  C++ class.     The issue affects these pages:? Control the Data Interface in the Generated Code  Exchange Data Between External C/C++ Code and Simulink Model?or Generated Code ?
Workaround: See the MathWorks website for the updated documentation:Control the Data Interface in the Generated CodeExchange Data Between External C/C++ Code and Simulink Model?or Generated Code?

Summary: Signal Logging and Dashboard block settings may not be retained for some Simulink Test operations
Description: If you perform certain Simulink Test operations, Signal Logging and Dashboard block settings may not be retained inside a test harness. Operations include:  Copying and pasting a block Cloning a test harness Importing a test harness Updating a test harness

Summary: robotics.OccupancyGrid does not support a variable-size input matrix in code generation
Description: The call robotics.OccupancyGrid(p)  creates an occupancy grid object from the values in matrix p . If the input matrix?p ?is variable-size and?used in code generation with MATLAB Coder or Simulink Coder, an error is displayed. For example, if you try to generate code for the following:?? ? ?coder.varsize('mapdata', ?[30, ?30], ?[1 1]);? ?mapdata = eye(10);? ?map = robotics.OccupancyGrid(mapdata,? 2);?  an error message similar to the following is displayed: ? ???? Expected a scalar value.? This expression has size [:? x :?].? ?Error in ==> OccupancyGrid Line: 1104 Column: 29? ?Error in ==> OccupancyGrid Line: 1166 Column: 29?
Workaround: Ensure the data is no longer variable-size before being passed as input to?robotics.OccupancyGrid.?For example, using the code sample from above, introduce an intermediate variable to select the size of the actual?mapdata?variable:? ?coder.varsize('mapdata',?[30,?30],?[1 1]);?? mapdata = eye(10);? ?mapdatafixed = mapdata(1:10, 1:10); % re-define the size of the variable? ?map = robotics.OccupancyGrid(mapdatafixed,?2);?

Summary: C/C++ code generation from the System objects?dsp.UDPSender or dsp.Receiver might fail due to compiler warnings or errors
Description: C/C++ code generation from the System objects?dsp.UDPSender  or dsp.Receiver  might fail due to compiler warnings or errors on some C/C++ compilers. This failure happens due to a missing? #ifndef (header file guard) code in a .hpp file used in the code generation process for these System objects. The header file is used during host machine code generation for these specific System objects. An example of this compilation failure occurs in a Simulink model using MATLAB Function blocks that contain code using dsp.UDPSender  or dsp.Receiver  System objects.  Certain C/C++ compilers on Linux platforms using recent Ubuntu?installations might fail when building the generated C/C++ code, in particular. One such Simulink build error looks similar to the following:? ? ??HostLib_rtw.o: In function `DestroyHostLibrary':?????HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x15): undefined reference to `dlclose'?????HostLib_rtw.o: In function `CreateHostLibrary':?????HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x120): undefined reference to `dlopen'?????HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x15b): undefined reference to `dlsym'?????HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x16e): undefined reference to `dlsym'?????HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x181): undefined reference to `dlsym'?????HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x194): undefined reference to `dlsym'?????HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x1a7): undefined reference to `dlsym'?????HostLib_rtw.o:HostLib_rtw.c:(.text+0x1ba): more undefined references to `dlsym' follow?????collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status??????????gmake: *** [testUDPsend_rec_sfun.mexa64] Error 255? ? ??Error:Unable to create mex function 'testUDPsend_rec_sfun.mexa64' required for simulation.

Summary: Input arguments of MATLAB Discrete-Event System methods?are?zero-based instead of one-based in Simulate using Code generation mode
Description: Input arguments of MATLAB Discrete-Event System methods?are?zero-based instead of one-based in Simulate using Code generation  mode. As a result, in Simulate using Code generation  mode, the following input arguments of MATLAB Dis crete-Event System methods?have incorrect values: source   in method? [entity,events]= entry(obj,storage,entity,source)  destination   in method ?[events]= exit(obj,storage,entity,destination)  destination   in method  [entity,events]= blocked(obj,storage,entity,destination)   These arguments commonly have the same location type, i.e., struct('type', place, 'index', index) , and the  index  field is what becomes zero-based. ?Thus, you might get different results?when?using?Simulate using Interpreted execution  mode?compared to?Simulate using Code generation  mode.
Workaround: Create a?fixLocation function on path?and call it before using source or destination?arguments in entry, exit or blocked functions. ?For example:function [events]= exit(obj,storage,entity,destination)?events = initEventArray();destination = fixLocation(destination);if??destination.index == 1? ? ?events = obj.eventIterate(storage, '', 200) ;end??endAnd in a separate file:function location = fixLocation(location)if coder.target('Sfun')? ? ?location.index = location.index + 1;?end?

Summary: Sample time?error for Relay block in a periodic Function-Call Subsystem
Description: When a Relay block is used inside a periodic Function-Call Subsystem, updating the block diagram throws an incorrect error message similar to: Output port 1 of the block 'repro/? Function-Call Subsystem/? Relay' has an improperly defined sample time when placed in the function call subsystem 'model/? Function-Call Subsystem'. Only constant (inf) or inherited (-1) sample times are allowed.?

Summary: Previous import statements are not recognized when running individual sections
Description: When a section is run in the Editor using the Run Section ?buttons,?import? statements from previous sections are not recognized. For example, if a class is imported in the first section and then used in later sections, MATLAB throws an Unrecognized function or variable  error.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible?when an Assignment block?connects to a Unit Delay block
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a model that meets these conditions An Assignment block that has a Y0  port?connects to the output of a Unit Delay block (or a Delay Block with a Delay Length  value of 1 ) The output of the Assignment block connects directly to the input of the same Unit Delay block. Both blocks are inside a While Iterator, For Iterator, or For Each subsystem. The?Optimize Global Data Access? parameter is set to Use global to hold temporary results  or Minimize global data access.   When?a model?meets the above conditions, the state for the Unit Delay block may be a local variable in the generated code. This causes the state to be uninitialized every time the function gets called causing incorrect answers. For the attached example, the code generator?generates the following incorrect code:? void Model_1658667_step(void){? int32_T s1_iter;?  real_T UnitDelay_DSTATE[4];  ?? for (s1_iter = 0; s1_iter + 1 <= rtP.ForIterator_IterationLimit; s1_iter++) {?? ? UnitDelay_DSTATE[s1_iter] += rtU.In1;?? ? rtY.Out1[0] = rtP.Gain_Gain * UnitDelay_DSTATE[0];?? ? rtY.Out1[1] = rtP.Gain_Gain * UnitDelay_DSTATE[1];?? ? rtY.Out1[2] = rtP.Gain_Gain * UnitDelay_DSTATE[2];?? ? rtY.Out1[3] = rtP.Gain_Gain * UnitDelay_DSTATE[3];??}}?
Workaround: See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727. That report describes a different but related issue, and the same workaround applies.

Summary: Apps that previously shared callbacks with deleted components do not open
Description: App Designer apps created in R2016b might not open in App Designer using R2017b. The problem occurs when the R2016b app has a shared callback, and one of the components sharing the callback is deleted. When you try to open the app in R2017b, a dialog box displays the following message:Error loading filename .mlapp.

Summary: MATLAB crashes during simulation or update diagram of a model hierarchy containing Variant Source blocks connected to global Data Store Read/Write blocks
Description: MATLAB may crash during simulation or update diagram of a model hierarchy if all of the following are true: A referenced model in the hierarchy has a global data store read block connected to an inactive input port of a Variant Source block The top model of the hierarchy has a global data store write block fed by an inactive Variant Source block. The Data Store Read/Write blocks specify the same Simulink.Signal object for the "Data store name" block parameter.??
Workaround: Turn off 'Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate pre-processor conditionals' for the Variant Source/Sink blocks in the model?Add Data Store Memory blocks to top and reference models referring to the commonly named data stores

Summary: Metric Engine may fail to collect compile-time metric data
Description: When collecting metric data for a model containing a Unit System Configuration block inside of a?library-linked subsystem, the model may fail to compile even though it compiles fine?when you are not collecting metric data. You will get the?following warning:? The model <model name> has changed between the time when two instances of this model were compiled.??This is not allowed for models referenced in Normal Mode.?  Compile time metrics like mathworks.metrics.CyclomaticComplexity  will fail to report metric data.?

Summary: Cannot?update imported references to DOORS requirements
Description: The Update  button in the?Requirements Editor does not update?referenced requirements imported from IBM Rational DOORS. After the original import from the DOORS module, the structure and contents?displayed in the Requirements Editor match the?original content in DOORS. But if you modify the DOORS module?(add, remove, or edit objects?or adjust parent-child relationships), using the update functionality in the Requirements Editor fails.?Previously imported references do not refresh to match the modified contents in DOORS.?

Summary: Table entries might overflow table boundaries in Report Explorer PDF reports
Description: For?Acrobat (PDF)  file format reports, table components, such as?Simulink Automatic Table , might fail to hyphenate the table contents. This can cause long, unbreakable content to overflow the table entry boundaries.

Summary: Invalid Cidentifiers generated for enumerations not derived from int32
Description: For?code generation, enumerations in MATLAB can be derived from these integer types:? int8,?uint8,?int16,?uint16, int32.    In R2017b, when using enumerations that?do?not derive from int32 , it is possible to generate invalid C identifiers. As a result, the generated C code does not compile.  This problem?can occur when?the same?matrix or structure of enumeration constants appears in two different functions, and the matrix or structure becomes a single constant. The attached example demonstrates such a ?matrix of enumeration constants.
Workaround: There are two workarounds:Use enumerations that derive from int32.Use integer casts around enums if they do not derive from int32. See attached example:For workaround 1, use codegen sample1 -args {enum_int32(0)}For workaround 2, use?codegen sample2 -args {enum_uint16(0)}

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: IncorrectVariant subsystem output value when source is unconnected
Description: A non-driven outport block in a VariantSubsystem does not consider Specify output when source is unconnected ?value during Simulation and Code generation when all thebelow conditions are true:  Variant Subsystem contains     flexible interfaces. Variant Subsystem contains a Model     block as an active choice. Outport block inside Variant     subsystem has Specify output when source is unconnected? and non-zero     value is specified.  Variant subsystem has Analyze all     choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  unchecked.
Workaround: Right click on the Model block within the Variant Subsystem and select?Subsystem & Model Reference ->?Create Subsystem from Selection to create a Subsystem around the Model block.Rename the Inports and Outports of this Subsystem respectively to ensure consistent interfaces.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model step function with reusable packaging may lead to incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code may be generated if all of these conditions are met: The configuration parameter Code interface packing  is set as Reusable function  for the model step function. The model step function is configured to be passed by reference using Configure Model Functions  on the the Code Generation > Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. In the generated code for model step function, a model output is accessed through a data copy where the variable read is only conditionally written.

Summary: Error when using GPUs with compute capability 7.0 or above with convolutional neural network functions such as?trainNetwork,?classify?and?activations
Description: Neural Network Toolbox uses the cuDNN Deep Learning library to train and execute convolutional neural networks on the GPU. The version of this library used by MATLAB does not work on GPUs with a compute capability of 7.0 or above, including the latest Volta architecture cards. If you try to train or run a network using one of these GPUs, you see an error. This occurs with the function?trainNetwork  and?the?classify ,?predict ?and?activations ?methods of?SeriesNetwork . It also occurs with functions in Computer Vision System Toolbox that use convolutional neural networks. The error usually has the ID 'nnet:cnngpu:CuDNNInitFailed'  and the message 'Failed to initialize the cuDNN handle. Return code was CUDNN_STATUS_INTERNAL_ERROR.'  Other error messages are possible, depending on the exact way the error was triggered. To see if your GPU is affected, in MATLAB, enter:   gpuDevice Examine the field?ComputeCapability . If this value is 7.0 ?or higher, your card will not work with convolutional neural networks.

Summary: Incorrect coding rule violations by file in Polyspace report
Description: A report generated from Polyspace results with the?CodingRules? template sometimes has incorrect information in the Violations by File  graphs.? The correct number of violations by file can be found later in the report in the table?Summary for all Files .?
Workaround: Installation instructions for R2016b  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Merging output?from an?Initialize, Reset,?or Terminate Function block to a root Outport block does not work as expected in generated code
Description: Generated code from your model does not work as expected when the following conditions are met: The model contains an Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block with an output port. The output of?the Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block is combined with an output of another block using a Merge block. The Merge block drives a root Outport block.  The intent of the above modeling pattern is to use an Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block to update the output of?a model during an initialize, reset, or terminate event. However, Simulink creates separate buffers for the Merge block and the root Outport block, causing the model output to remain unchanged during a call to?a model?initialize, reset, or terminate function. This causes a wrong?answer when the generated code is integrated with the target run-time environment.?A wrong answer can also?occurs when using a code generation based target such as rapid accelerator for top model simulation or model reference simulation in software-in-the-loop (SIL) mode with top model code interface.

Summary: Assertion error when combining?min or max function and custom code in C action language charts
Description: Simulink might display an assertion error when you attempt to update diagram or run a model?containing a Stateflow chart that meets all of the following conditions: The Stateflow chart uses C as the action language. The chart uses the?min  or max  function. At least one of the arguments to the?min  or max  function contains a custom code symbol. The output of the?min ?or?max ?function?is not assigned directly to a Stateflow data.?  The assertion error contains the following text: failed assertion in?const/cg_const_function.cpp at line ###:   can_create_instance(type)?
Workaround: Assign the output of the min or max function with custom code arguments to a Stateflow data object. For example, if?getCustomCodeValue?is a symbol from custom code, this transition produces an error:?[max(getCustomCodeValue(), x) > 0]{out = 1;}To apply the workaround, divide the original transition into two new transitions. The first transition stores the value of?max(getCustomCodeValue(), x)?in a Stateflow data:[]{temp_var = max(getCustomCodeValue(), x);}The second transition uses the stored data in place of the?max?function in the original transition:?[temp_var > 0]{out = 1;}

Summary: "Runs cannot be compared" error in Fixed-Point Tool
Description: In the Fixed-Point Tool, after completing the workflow to convert a model to fixed point and clicking the Compare Results  button to examine the system behavior in Simulation Data Inspector, the Fixed-Point Tool throws the error message,? "Runs cannot be compared. Ensure that signals have been logged in more? than 1 runs." The tool throws this error even when more than one run is available if the 'SignalLogging ' parameter of the model is set to 'off' .
Workaround: To compare results in the Fixed-Point Tool, turn on the signal logging parameter of the model.For example, in MATLAB, enterset_param(model_name, 'SignalLogging', 'on');

Summary: Opening MATLABWindow on Ubuntu 17.10 generates error: Unable to launch the MATLABWindow application
Description: You might encounter a warning or error message  on Ubuntu version 17.10 when launching certain tools in MATLAB. Some of these tools include: App Designer Simulink Data Inspector Root Inport Mapping Simulink Template Gallery Export Model to Template Add-Ons Explorer Simulink Start Page  For example: >> simulinkWarning: MATLABWindow application failed to launch. Unable to launch the MATLABWindow application >In sltemplate.ui.StartPage/showWithFallback? In sltemplate.ui.StartPage.show
Workaround: To ensure that this workaround applies, execute these lines of code in MATLAB:cd(matlabroot)!./bin/glnxa64/MATLABWindowIf MATLAB displayssymbol lookup error: /?usr/?lib/?x86_64-linux-gnu/?libatk-1.0.so.0: undefined symbol: g_log_structuredthen use the following steps to fix the issue.In a command shell, navigate to the MATLAB installation folder and enter the following at the command prompt. The first line uses an example of where MATLAB might be installed; modify it for your installation. You will need root privileges.    # cd /usr/local/MATLAB/R2017b    # cd cefclient/sys/os/glnxa64    # mkdir exclude    # mv libglib-2.0.so* exclude/    # exitStart MATLAB and execute the following commands to verify the fix:    w = matlab.internal.webwindow('http://mathworks.com');    w.showThe mini-browser window opens, showing the mathworks.com website.

Summary: dsp.HighpassFilter or?dsp.LowpassFilter?objects generate IIR filter representations with incorrectly scaled magnitude responses
Description: In rare cases,?dsp.HighpassFilter? and?dsp.LowpassFilter  might generate IIR filter representations with incorrectly scaled magnitude responses.

Summary: Enumerator class conflicts with another entity
Description: When running Polyspace Code Prover on C++ code, you get this error:? C:\? Polyspace\?? sources\my_file.cpp,?  line Y: error: declaration of constant "std::future_status::timeout" is incompatible with a declaration in another translation unit (enumerator conflicts with another entity) |????????????the other declaration is at line?Z of "C:\? Polyspace\?? sources\second_file.cpp" |??????timeout,?  |??????^ |??????????detected during compilation of secondary translation unit "second_file.cpp"   The error occurs sometimes when two enumerators have the same name. For instance, in the above message, timeout  is an enumerator (or enumeration constant).?
Workaround: Rename one enumerator when possible.

Summary: Solver Profiler error when encountering Simscape Multibody blocks with multidimensional states
Description: The Solver Profiler does not count integration failures correctly when encountering Simscape Multibody blocks with multidimensional states, and throws the following error: Error while evaluating TimerFcn for timer 'timer-1'?  Subscript indices must either be real positive integers or logicals.

Summary: Mix of sample times in an algebraic loop can cause wrong simulation answers
Description: When an algebraic loop in a model contains blocks with a mixture of constant sample times and other types of sample times, simulating the model might produce wrong answers. To detect the problem, run the?detectConstantSampleTimeInAlgebraicLoop() ?function?with the model name as the argument. The attached script test_script.m  and model?Example_R2015b.slx ?serve as?an example.?

Summary: Cannot open reference applications from Simulink Start Page Examples tab
Description: You cannot open the Powertrain Blockset reference applications from the Simulink Start Page Examples tab.?
Workaround: To open the reference applications, at the MATLAB command line, you can type:autoblkConVehStart (opens?Conventional Vehicle Reference Application)autoblkHevVehStart (opens?Hybrid Electric Vehicle Multimode Reference Application)autoblkHevIPSVehStart?(opens Hybrid Electric Vehicle Input Power-Split Reference Application)autoblkEvVehStart (opens Electric Vehicle Reference Application)autoblkCIDynamometerStart (opens CI Engine Dynamometer Reference Application)autoblkSIDynamometerStart?(opens SI Engine Dynamometer Reference Application)

Summary: MATLAB crashes when generating code for certain cases of structure array indexing with a colon operator
Description: When you generate code for ?a colon operator that accesses elements of a structure array, under certain conditions,?a crash can occur. The stack trace contains: EML::DEF_BEFORE_USE::DuStridedFact::isDBitDefined(int) const  This crash can occur when all of the following conditions are true:? A colon indexing expression assigns an element of a structure array  The structure array is inside of an object The?class of the object is the same as the class of?an input to the function  For example, the following code causes the crash: function out = foo(~)x = MyClass();x.prop = struct('field', {ones(1,10), ones(1,10)});bar(x);out = x;endfunction x = bar(x)x.prop(1).field(1:2) = 10;end  This?issue can occur for code generation with MATLAB Coder and for MATLAB code in a MATLAB Function block in a Simulink model.
Workaround: Locate the MATLAB source line that triggers the crash. If you?are unable to locate the source line, contact MathWorks technical support for assistance.Rewrite the code so that the colon indexing operates on a temporary variable. For example:t = x.prop(1).field;t(1:2) = 10;x.prop(1).field = t;

Summary: Simple Heat Exchanger (G-TL) block ignores changes to initial conditions of gas
Description: The initial conditions of the gas volume in the Simple Heat Exchanger (G-TL) component are internally set to their default values at the start of each simulation. Changes to these conditions, made through the Gas 1 initial temperature  and Gas 1 initial pressure  block parameters in the Effects and Initial Conditions  tab, are ignored.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Use of?Simulink.defineIntEnumType in a MATLAB Function block may lead to wrong answers
Description: When you use?Simulink.defineIntEnumType ?in the MATLAB environment to create in-memory Simulink enumeration types, use of these enumerations inside a MATLAB Function block may incorrectly continue to use a stale definition, leading to wrong answers. For example,? define a Simulink enumeration type as:? >> Simulink.defineIntEnumType('MyColor', {'Red','Yellow'}, [0;1])  Use the enumeration inside a MATLAB Function Block:? function y = myFunction  y =? int32(MyColor.Red);  The model simulation outputs 0, as expected. However, if you redefine the enumeration as: >> Simulink.defineIntEnumType('MyColor', {'Red','Yellow'}, [1;0])  Then the simulation output does not change.
Workaround: After each change to the in-memory enumeration, you can force the model to rebuild in the following ways:1. Make a change (for example, by adding a space or a comment) to every MATLAB Function block using the in-memory enumeration. This will force all data to reload.2. Change the build mode for the model:>> set_param(bdroot,'SimBuildMode','sf_nonincremental_build');You can also find this option in the?Configuration Parameters?dialog under?Simulink Target > Advanced parameters. See this documentation for details:?https://www.mathworks.com/help/simulink/gui/simulation-target-build-mode.html.?Then, update the diagram (Ctrl-D). After the diagram updates, you can revert back to the default build mode:>> set_param(bdroot,'SimBuildMode','sf_incremental_build');?

Summary: Copy and paste of Model block in AUTOSAR composition model might generate access violation
Description: In a Simulink model configured as an AUTOSAR composition, under certain conditions, copying and pasting a Model block generates an access violation error message.
Workaround: To work around this bug, instead of copying and pasting a Model block, add a new Model block to the AUTOSAR composition model.? You can use quick insert or drag a Model block from the Simulink Library browser.

Summary: Spectrum Analyzer crashes MATLAB in filter bank mode
Description: When you use the Spectrum Analyzer block or the?dsp.SpectrumAnalyzer   System object, MATLAB crashes if you use the following settings together:? Method ?property set to 'Filter bank'  FrequencySpan ?property set to either?'Start and stop frequency'  or  'Span and center frequency'

Summary: Logic Analyzer missing data in some expandedsignals.
Description: For expanded (matrix or bus) signals, data could be missing in the Logic Analyzer display. Data loss can occur when the following steps are performed: Scroll down the Logic Analyzer display with two or more expanded signals displayed, such that the last waves are visible.  Simulate the model again Scroll up to the top of the Logic Analyzer. All waves of the top expanded signal are erased and not replotted.
Workaround: If the top signal remains expanded between simulations, zoom into the display. The Logic Analyzer repaints the erased signals.?

Summary: Replacing a table or content placeholder corrupts PowerPoint presentation
Description: Replacing a table or content placeholder might corrupt a PowerPoint presentation, causing PowerPoint to display an error when it tries to open the file.

Summary: Compiler error when FIR Interpolation block is configured to use CSD or Factored-CSD and has multiplier input or output pipeline registers
Description: When you configure FIR Interpolation block to implement multipliers as CSD or Factored CSD, and the multiplier input or output pipeline settings are not zero, the generated HDL code is missing a declaration for a vector signal. This results in errors during compilation in an HDL compiler and will result in a synthesis error as well.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for muxed, asynchronous signals to Rate Transition block
Description: Incorrect generated code might occur for these two modeling scenarios: 1. If your model contains a Rate Transition block:? Two signals from different asynchronously executed tasks (for example, using Asynchronous Task Specification blocks) feed through a Mux block to a Rate Transition block.? The Rate Transition block parameter Ensure data integrity during data transfer  is selected.  2. If your model employs automatic rate transition: Two signals from different asynchronously executed tasks (for example, using Asynchronous Task Specification blocks)?feed through a Mux block to another task.? The Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  model configuration parameter is selected. The Deterministic data transfer  model configuration parameter option is set to Whenever possible  or Never (minimum delay) . A hidden Rate Transition Block is inserted, and as a result, a badge with the text dbbuf ?is on the port of the block that connects to the output of the Mux block.  When the above conditions are met, in the generated code for the Rate Transition block, the update method in one of the asynchronously executed tasks might be incorrect because of the ambiguity of the muxed asynchronous signals as to whether to transfer bit-wise data or all the data as a whole.

Summary: designMultirateFIR designs filters of the wrong length for certain interpolation and decimation factor combinations
Description: designMultirateFIR returns the wrong number of coefficients when both of the following conditions are true: The decimation factor is greater than 1 The interpolation factor is greater than the decimation factor

Summary: For Model Reference block, disable function interface may contain extra, unused?parameters causing uncompilable code
Description: In the generated code for a Model Reference block, the disable function interface may contain extra, unused parameters for a model that meets all of these conditions: The Model Reference block contains an enable port at the root level. The Model Reference block contains an Enabled subsystem.  The extra parameters may cause uncompilable code when other models?use the Model Reference block.

Summary: No data received in MATLAB when subscribing to ROS topic published by Simulink
Description: You can publish ROS message data through a Simulink ROS?Publish block. If you use rostopic echo or the receive ?function in MATLAB, you will not receive any of the data published by the block. The function call in MATLAB pauses execution of the model until you cancel the function execution or a timeout occurs. This issue only occurs when all of the following conditions apply: The Simulink model is running (not stopped or paused). You call rostopic echo ?or the receive? functions in the same MATLAB instance.?
Workaround: In general, we suggest that you don't execute MATLAB code on the command-line while a Simulink model is running.If you still want to display the message data that is being published in Simulink, we suggest one of the following workarounds:?Use the non-blocking subscriber functionality?to receive data in MATLAB. For example, if the Simulink Publish block publishes data on the /cmd_vel topic, you can check the last data that has been received by calling:sub = rossubscriber('/cmd_vel')? ?sub.LatestMessage You can also specify a MATLAB callback function. See?rossubscriber?for additional information on different ways to receive data.Open a second instance of MATLAB?and connect to the same ROS Master. If the ROS Master has been created in the first instance of MATLAB, call?rosinit. If the ROS Master is on a different machine, call?rosinit?with additional arguments. Once the ROS node is initialized, you can call?rostopic echo?or?receive?without any problems.?Create a second Simulink model with a ROS?Subscribe?block. Run this second model at the same time as the first model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code may be generated?for Rate Transition block in multitasking environment
Description: The Rate Transition block may generate incorrect code for the use in multitasking environment, when all of the following conditions are met:-The input signal of the Rate Transition block is of aggregate type (matrix or structure) and the output of the block feeds into a Selector block in which?only an element or structure field is used downstream. -The Rate Transition block has these?conditions:? The input sample time of the Rate Transition block is?faster than the output sample time. The output sample time?is a multiple of the input sample time. The output of the Rate Transition block must connect to an output port directly. In the Rate Transition block parameters dialog box, the?Ensure data integrity during data transfer? parameter is selected. In the Rate Transition block?parameters dialog box, the?Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)? parameter is not selected. The Rate Transition block displays?buf ?on the block icon.

Summary: Reading MDF data might generate Numerical value returned as physical value unsensible error
Description: Some MDF files generated by Vector CANape?might have?VTAB channels with hidden linear scaling applied. Using the Vector MDF Validator tool shows a note specifically indicating this. This condition is outside of the formal ASAM MDF standard, and results in the following error when reading such channels:   Numerical value returned as physical value unsensible.

Summary: Clicking the?Analysis menu with more than?1,000 model elements selected can cause?Simulink to appear to stop responding.
Description: If you select thousands of?model elements in the Simulink Editor and then click the Analysis menu, Simulink appears to stop responding. However, the system will eventually respond.
Workaround: Clear your selection?before?clicking the Analysis menu.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Prelookup block might incorrectly cast intermediate results when input and breakpoints have different floating-point data types
Description: The generated code for the Prelookup block might incorrectly cast some intermediate results when all of these conditions are true: Index search method  is Evenly spaced points  or Linear search . The input and breakpoints have different floating-point data types.?For example,?the data type of the?input is double and the data type of the breakpoint is single. The data type of Fraction  is double.  The incorrect cast leads to a?wrong?answer for the?fraction output, and impacts stand-alone code as well as code generated for Accelerator and Rapid Accelerator simulations.

Summary: State variable for Unit Delay incorrectly overwritten for a subsystem whose function interface includes arguments
Description: See?Bug Report ID 1668727 ?for full details on related issues.? Incorrect code generation might occur when all of the following conditions are true: Optimize global data access ?configuration parameter is set to?Use global to hold temporary results ?or?Minimize global data access  Reuse local block outputs ?configuration parameter is enabled. The model contains a subsystem in which the?Function packaging ?parameter set to?NonReusable?and Function interface ?is set to  Allow Arguments.   A Unit Delay block's input or output is connected to that subsystem's output signal.  Errors might be thrown during the build process.?
Workaround: See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727.
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 7, R2017b Update 4, R2018a

Summary: signalbuilder function unnecessarily opens Simulink model when deleting signals from Signal Builder block
Description: Using the?signalbuilder  function to delete signals from a?Signal Builder block in a loaded Simulink model unnecessarily forces the Simulink model to?open.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code for coding pattern?containing?a function within a for loop that only reads global variables
Description: The code generator contains an optimization that identifies invariant code in a for  loop and moves this code before the for  loop if the move preserves the program semantics. For some MATLAB coding patterns,?the code generator may incorrectly?apply?this optimization to a function that reads a global variable. For example, in the below MATLAB function?example_1649162 , the function readG1  is called?within a loop . readG1  reads the global variable g1  in its function definition . In this situation it is not safe for the code generator to move the call to?readG1 ?before the?for ?loop due to the other statement in the loop?g1(k) = u1(k) + 1; . ?The incorrectly optimized generated code can be seen below.  ?Input MATLAB Code:? ?function y1 = example_1649162(u1)  ? ? global g1;  ? ? for k = 1:10  ? ? ? ? g1(k) = u1(k) + 1;  ? ? ? ? y1 = readG1();  ? ? end  end  function y = readG1()  ? ? coder.inline('never');  ? ? global g1;  ? ? y = 2*g1(1:10);  end?   Incorrect generated C code:? static void readG1(double y[10])  {  ? int i;  ? for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) {  ? ? y[i] = 2.0 * g1[i];  ? }  }  void?example_1649162(const double u1[100], double b_y1[10])  {  ? int k;  ? readG1(b_y1);  ? for (k = 0; k < 10; k++) {  ? ? g1[k] = u1[k] + 1.0;  ? }  }?
Workaround: To prevent the optimization from incorrectly moving the function call, make it appear that the function reading the global calls custom C code. Create the following function on the MATLAB path:?function fakeSideEffect? ? coder.inline('always');?? ? coder.ceval('(void)',1);?end?Then, add a call to this function in the definition of the function only reading global variables. See below for the application of the workaround to the? example in the above Description section.function y1 = example_1649162(u1)? ? global g1;? ? for k = 1:10? ? ? ? g1(k) = u1(k) + 1;? ? ? ? y1 = readG1();? ? endendfunction y = readG1()? ? coder.inline('never');? ? global g1;? ? fakeSideEffect();?? ? y = 2*g1(1:10);end ?

Summary: U1052?build error when using Visual Studio 2017 toolchain
Description: When you try to build the generated code from a model using the Microsoft Visual Studio 2017 toolchain, and either "VSCMD_START_DIR" is defined in the environment or?the "%USERPROFILE%\Source" directory exists, Simulink reports a build failure with the following error message: ?NMAKE : fatal error U1052: file '<model_name>.mk' not found?  See?Bug Report ID 1686741 ?for a similar issue when building generated code from a System Target File that uses the template makefile approach, such as Simuilink Real-Time.?
Workaround: Run the following command after opening MATLAB:>> setenv('VSCMD_START_DIR',?'%CD%');For more details, please refer to the following link:?https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/content/problem/26780/vsdevcmdbat-changes-the-current-working-directory.html?

Summary: MATLAB might hang when simulating models containing the Battery block in Specialized Technology
Description: MATLAB might hang when simulating models containing the Battery  block under the?Simscape Power Systems > Specialized Technology > Electric Drives > Extra Sources  sublibrary.?

Summary: Model block with periodic event ports does not update its states when simulating in accelerator mode
Description: Your model is subject to this bug when it meets the following conditions: Your model contains a Model block which references another model containing blocks with states such as a Unit Delay block. The Model block has the?'Show model periodic event ports' parameter selected. The Model block or its parent model?has simulation mode set to 'Accelerator'.  When all of the above conditions are met, the states in the referenced model remain fixed at their initial values, leading to an incorrect simulation result.

Summary: Expected output may not be generated post SLDV analysis with DVSaveExpectedOutput
Description: Simulink model with?Configuration Parameters >? Simulink Design Verifier Design Verifier > Results > Data file options > Include expected output values? (i.e?DVSaveExpectedOutput )?  enabled may not generate the expected output values? post Simulink Design Verifier ana lysis. Incorrect warning message is displayed as below even though none of the outports in the model execute conditionally:  Simulink Design Verifier is unable to record the expected output values on sldvData because some Outport blocks of the model 'model_name' execute conditionally. Expected output values of Outport blocks can be regenerated by invoking sldvruntest after Simulink Design Verifier completes.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results when multiple blocks driving a Merge block are executing?during the same time step
Description: Incorrect?generated code might occur for a model that meets all of the following conditions:? Multiple blocks driving a Merge block are executing?during the same time step. Block?priority?or data dependency do not determine the block execution order. The Optimize block operation order  parameter has a value other than off .  The Optimize block?operator order? parameter can change which block?is the most recently computed?block in the generated code?as compared to simulation. As a result, the simulation and code generation results do not match at the Merge block output. This?bug does not occur?if you follow the?Guidelines for Using the Merge block in the documentation. See?https://www.mathworks.com/help/simulink/slref/merge.html .

Summary: dcmeci2ecef, lla2eci, eci2lla, and eci2aer transformations for IAU-76/FK-5 reduction may return inaccurate results with adjustment to longitude and obliquity

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code for a subsystem containing an HDL State Control block with the State Control parameter?set to Synchronous
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a model that meets all of these conditions:  The Optimize global data access? parameter is set to?Use global to hold temporary results ?or?Minimize global data access.  A Subsystem block contains an Enabled Subsystem block and an HDL State Control block.   In the HDL State Control block parameters dialog box, the State Control parameter is set to Synchronous . The enabled subsystem contains a Unit Delay block.  In the generated C/C++ code, there is an illegal merging of the Unit Delay block?state and its input or output signal. In the incorrect generated code, the state changes even when the subsystem is not enabled.
Workaround: Choose one of these workarounds to avoid generating incorrect code:Change?the State control parameter setting to Classic.Remove the State Control block.Replace the Unit Delay block with a Delay block. In the Delay block parameters dialog box, set the?Delay Length parameter to 1.?Set the Optimize Global Data Access parameter to None.

Summary: Some AUTOSAR examples fail to open
Description: MATLAB is unable to?open some examples from the Embedded Coder Support Package for AUTOSAR Standard, and displays an error in the command window about missing supporting files.

Summary: Invalid source block specification?error when creating a SIL/PIL test harness
Description: If you create a?SIL/PIL test harness for a?block in a model for which?Configuration Parameters > Code Generation >?Generate Code Only ?is set to on , the test harness creation fails with this error message: ? ??Invalid source block specification.
Workaround: R2017a: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.R2016b: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1671801?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2016b.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Numerical distortions in Sine HDL Optimized and Cosine HDL Optimized blocks
Description: The Sine HDL Optimized and Cosine HDL Optimized blocks produce large numerical errors if the Number of Data Points ?block parameter is a power of two.

Summary: mldivide operator A\b can be very slow when A is a sparse symmetric indefinite matrix
Description: The function call?mldivide(A,b) ,where A  is a sparse symmetric indefinite matrix, does not use an approximate minimum degree permutation (see function amd ) as it should. Furthermore, if the function call spparms('autoamd',tf)  is used, then the value of tf  has the opposite of the documented effect for a sparse symmetric indefinite matrix A .
Workaround: To avoid the slowdown caused by this bug, you can change a global option with spparms prior to solving A\b. This ensures that a sparse non-symmetric solver (provided by UMFPACK) is used for sparse symmetric indefinite matrices, instead of MA57.spparms('usema57',0);A\b

Summary: Using a protected model that has GenerateMakefile == 'off' errors out when building the top model
Description: Protecting a model with the configuration parameter GenerateMakefile == 'off' and the protected model output format set to 'CompiledBinaries' results in a protected model that does not have a makefile or compiled binaries. As a result, when code generation is initiated from a model referencing this protected model, the following error is thrown.? "The library file <modelName>_rtwlib.a does not exist."

Summary: Failed validation status for time zones different than EST
Description: The IEC Certification Kit performs an auto-comparison of Simulink Design Verifier reports that are generated in the userˇŻs environment against baseline reports that are shipped with the product (expected results). The time stamps in the reports generated in the userˇŻs environment represent the local time zone. Since the time stamps in the expected results represent the Eastern Time Zone, the discrepancy in these values affects the auto-comparison outcome and results in failed validation status.
Workaround: Visit?Bug Report ID?1644448?to download a patch that addresses this bug in R2017a.

Summary: Cannot generate web view with Requirements option turned on
Description: When trying to generate a?web view with the?Requirements  option turned on: The error No matching files were found  appears. The web view report is not generated.  The?issue?occurs when there are requirements traceability links?from Simulink objects?to requirements managed in the Simulink Requirements Editor.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect output from Discrete Zero-Pole block when changing zero and pole parameters from empty array to nonempty values

Summary: Possible size difference between MATLAB and generated code for multiplication of two empty matrices
Description: In MATLAB, multiplication of an m-by-0 matrix?and?a 0-by-n matrix produces an m-by-n array of zeros.? In certain cases, generated code incorrectly produces a 0-by-0?empty matrix. The issue can occur for code generated by MATLAB Coder or for a MATLAB Function block. The issue occurs only when both matrices are variable-size (values?m?and?n are not constant) and at least one of the following conditions is true: One matrix is complex and the other is not complex. One matrix is single and the other is double. One of the matrices has more than two dimensions and the extra dimension is 1 at run time.  For example, consider the function test1 .? function out = test1(n,m)a = ones(n,0);b = ones(0,m);out = a*single(b);end   Generate a?mex function.? codegen test1 -args {5 6}?   R un the original MATLAB function. test1(5,6)   The result is a 5-by-6 matrix of zeros. Run?the generated mex. test1_mex(5,6)?   The result ?is a 0-by-0 empty matrix.
Workaround: Modify the source so that?the matrices do?not meet the conditions that cause the issue. Make sure that ?the matrices:Have the same complexityHave the same base type (both single or double)Do not have trailing singleton dimensions

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for AUTOSAR target when an S-function sets?ssSetOutputPortOutputExprInRTW?to?TRUE

The system target file is set to AUTOSAR.tlc, AND The system has an S-function with the ssSetOutputPortOutputExprInRTW(SimStruct *S, int idx, bool val)  function having the bool  argument val set to 1 (TRUE)? for at least one Outport block, AND The S-function must be connected to a root input port.  In the generated code, the input variable for the S-Function may be incorrectly reused.

Enable the parameter?Excludethis block from ˇ®Block reductionˇŻ optimization on the Block Parameters?dialog box?for each insertedSignal Conversion block. Regenerate and inspect the code to confirm that theproblem is averted.?

Summary: Memory leak when calling cellfun on string array
Description: Starting in R2017b, the cellfun  function accepts string arrays as input arguments. For compatibility, cellfun  treats any input string array as though it were a cell array of character vectors. However, calling cellfun  on a string array causes a memory leak. cellfun(@isempty, ["hello", "world"])  The memory leak is most noticeable when the input is a large string array or when cellfun  is called repeatedly in a loop.?
Workaround: There are two workarounds. Both avoid calling cellfun on a string array.Convert the string array to a cell array of character vectors?before calling cellfun:?s = ["Hello", "World"];?if isstring(s)?s = cellstr(s);end?out = cellfun(@isempty, s);?Use arrayfun on a string array. To access the underlying character vectors for each string element, use curly-brace indexing:s = ["Hello", "World"];?out = arrayfun(@(x) isempty(x{:}), s);

Summary: MATLAB crashes while simulating a model with large data store memory in rapid accelerator mode

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Delay block with ?enable port and 'External reset' set to 'Level' generates HDL code with incorrect reset behavior
Description: When a Delay block has 'Show enable port' set to 'on'??and external reset set to 'Level' is placed in a Simulink system, the external enable signal always has priority over the external reset signal. However, the generated HDL code will conversely give the external reset signal priority over the external enable signal. This issue is unrelated to global synchronous/asynchronous resets and clock enable signals which appear in the generated HDL code. The mismatch only pertains to a delay block with external enable and reset signals with 'Classic' state control.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds both involving changes to the Simulink model. Workaround 1** results in a significant change to the simulation behavior. Workaround 2 maintains simulation behavior with a minor difference.?Place a State Control block with 'State control' set to 'Synchronous' in the same subsystem as the Delay block with external enable and reset signals. The 'External reset' type on the Delay block(s) must also be changed to 'Level hold'. The State Control block enforces the Simulink subsystem to use semantics with enable and reset, among other constructs, which closely match that of digital hardware. The Simulink behavior of the delay block will instead give the external reset signal priority over the enable signal, which matches the generated HDL code. Additionally, enforcing these semantics in Simulink will generate HDL code which better maps to hardware constructs, yielding better hardware performance and lower resource consumption than the same subsystem with 'Classic' state control. For more information, see?State Control?documentation.Use a Logical Operator block to 'AND' the external enable and external reset signals of the Delay block. Connect the output of the Logical Operator block to the external reset port on the Delay block. Note that modeling a Delay block with an external enable and 'External reset' type of 'Level' in this manner yields behavior as if the Delay block had a 'Level hold' reset type. Simulation may differ slightly. Please see the attached image 'workaround_2_sim_diff' as an example. For more details on the difference between the reset modes, review the section 'External reset' in the?Delay?documentation. An example of this workaround is attached as 'workaround_2_example.slx'.** Only applicable to MATLAB R2016a and later.

Summary: MATLAB Function block output signal with?storage class?might not be in generated code

Summary: 'Executable not found' error occrus?when running parallel simulations using parsim with?RapidAcceleratorUpToDateCheck set to 'off'
Description: Parallel simulations can be?run by creating an array of Simulink.SimulationInput  objects and passing them as an input argument to the parsim  command. However, if ?the model is set to run in rapid accelerator mode and any of the Simulink.SimulationInput  objects have RapidAcceleratorUpToDateCheck? parameter set to 'off' , those simulations error out with an 'Executable not found ' error as can be seen on the ErrorMessage ?property of the returned Simulink.SimulationOutput  object. The error will not  be encountered if each of the parallel workers builds the rapid accelerator target as shown in the following example? ?https://www.mathworks.com/help/simulink/examples/rapid-accelerator-simulations-using-parsim.html ??

Summary: Nested subchart triggers "Unreachable State" diagnostic despite being reachable by a boundary crossing super transition
Description: A nested subchart will incorrectly trigger the "Unreachable State" diagnostic if the subchart is only reachable by a boundary crossing super transition.
Workaround: To allow your chart to compile and execute, configure the "Unreachable execution path" diagnostic to be either a warning or disabled. To configure the "Unreachable execution path" diagnostic,?open the Model Configuration Parameters. In the Diagnostics drop-down menu, select Stateflow.?The "Unreachable execution path" diagnostic can be set to none, warning, or error. If set to warning, the "Unreachable State" diagnostic will still appear but the chart will compile and execute. If set to none, the diagnostic will no longer display, but you will no longer see other legitimate instances of this issue.

Summary: Saving SimState of a model?results in a memory leak?if the model contains a model reference block that is referenced in accelerator mode
Description: When a model is referenced using model reference block, the following situation results in a memory leak: The referenced model is referenced in accelerator mode (either as a setting on the Model block or because the top model is configured for accelerator mode) SimState  of the simulation of the top model is saved. You can save the?SimState ?by using the model parameter SaveCompleteFinalSimState  or by using the parameter CurrentSimState .  The size of the memory leak grows with the number of continuous and discrete states insides of the accelerator-referenced model block.

Summary: Running linmodv5 without loading Simulink can result in an error
Description: Running linmodv5 when Simulink is not yet loaded may result in the error: Error using slsvTestingHook  Unknown hook 'DoNotBusExpandUnitDelay'. Either no such hook is registered or the module registering the hook is currently not loaded.  Error in busUtils>do_not_expand_unit_delay_for_buses (line 130)  ? ? ret = slsvTestingHook('DoNotBusExpandUnitDelay', val);  Error in busUtils (line 27)  ? ? ? ? ret = do_not_expand_unit_delay_for_buses(varargin{1});  Error in sl (line 15)  [varargout{1:nargout}]=feval(varargin{:});  Error in linmodv5 (line 40)  fUDBusVal = sl('busUtils', 'handleunitdelaybus', 0);?
Workaround: Explicitly load Simuilink before running linmodv5. You can load Simulink by running?load_simulink at the MATLAB command line.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code?generator?does not?honor data alignment?requirements?for?nonreusable subsystem
Description: The code generator does not honor data alignment?if a model?meets?all of the following conditions: Model contains at least one subsystem for which the code generator produces nonreusable function code. Subsystem has at least one inport or outport signal with a data alignment requirement, which is specified?with a?signal property or results from code replacement specified with a code replacement library (CRL) entry that includes the AlignmentBoundary ?property.  If the compiled code from your compiler for the target processor would result in different numeric results or crash when using an instruction?that requires specific data alignments but the data is not aligned properly,?the generated code can produce numeric results that differ from simulation results or result in crashes when all of the conditions above are met.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: sum, prod, mean return incorrect results in code generation?with NaN inputs and mismatched dimensions
Description: Code generated for sum , prod , or mean  might produce incorrect values when all of?the following conditions are true: The input array?contains a NaN. The specified working dimension is larger than the number of dimensions in the input.  For example, the following incorrectly returns all zero values:    out = sum(nan(2),3);  ans =       0     0       0     0

Summary: Statistics columns are blank in the Workspace browser
Description: The Workspace browser does not display any values in the statistics columns. This includes columns such as?Min  and Max .

Summary: Accelerator mode results might be different on subsequent runs if mask initialization code unconditionally sets data types or sizes

Workaround: Surround calls to set_param in your mask initialization code with an if statement so that the call only takes place if the value has changed. For example:if ~strcmp(get_param(block_handle, 'OutDataTypeStr'),'single')??? set_param(block_handle, 'OutDataTypeStr', 'single');end
