To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Simulating a model in Accelerator mode may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB?may crash for a model that meets these conditions: From the Simulation > Mode  menu, Accelerator  is selected. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Solver  pane, the Type  parameter is set to Variable-step . The model contains a Backlash block with the?Enable zero-crossing detection  parameter selected.
Workaround: From the Simulation > Mode menu, select Normal

Summary: Moving Simulink lines can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Moving lines that connect to branched signals?or connecting a port to a signal can cause?MATLAB to crash. The crash sometimes occurs during the next operation after?moving or creating?the segment.

Summary: ?Inefficient code produced for structure initialization?
Description: For MATLAB code containing nested structure initialization code that meets all  of these criteria, an optimization inadvertently duplicates the function call for every field in the structure: A function returns a substructure. Typically, such a function is written to initialize a structure. The function has no visible effect other than its return value. The resulting structure is assigned as a field into another structure.  This situation typically results in many calls to the initialization function, and many temporary variables. This bug?can cause inefficient generated code. It does not lead to wrong answers.
Workaround: ?To prevent the optimization from duplicating calls, make it appear that the function calls custom C code. Create the following function on the path:?function fakeSideEffect    coder.ceval('(void)',1);?end?Then, add a call to this function from the bottom-most structure initialization function.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you click Solver information
Description: MATLAB crashes when you click the solver name at the lower right corner of the Simulink editor window. This happens when all of the following conditions are true: The model uses configuration reference for the active configuration The referenced configuration set is not found?

Summary: Analysis fails with error?'Internal error: Lack of cast between incompatible result type and first operand type'
Description: Polyspace analysis can fail with the following error message in the?Run Log ?pane:? ?**** GNU C 4.9 to intermediate language translation - 3 (P_SIA)?   Internal error: Lack of cast between incompatible result type and first operand type (case X) in PROC myproc?   instr : mul t:gaa (i.yy) myfunction/xx eyyyy, exxxx?   | ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ...?   | ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?["source coordinates": myfile.c YY XXXX]?   The error occurs when analyzing source?myfile.c  ?and procedure?myproc around line XXXX.? This error appears only if the code being verified has a very specific situation.
Workaround: If you see this error, try to relaunch analysis with option -c-extra-flags -no-suif-checker?(to add in Advanced Settings>Other).If the issue persists, contact Technical Support and if possible, use Polyspace Obfuscator?to obfuscate details of your code:?download the tool on MATLAB Central.?Then provide the obfuscation.zip and log file.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Rate Transition block may generate incorrect code for the use in multitasking environment
Description: The Rate Transition block may generate incorrect code for the use in multitasking environment, when all of the following conditions are met:-The generated code for the Rate Transition block and/or the immediate surrounding logics?is in one fused?for  loop rather than in separate for  loops. -The loop fusion optimization is not disabled in effect as a part of certain code optimization configuration. -The Rate Transition block has these?conditions:? The input sample time of the Rate Transition block is?faster than the output sample time. The output sample time?is a multiple of the input sample time. The output of the Rate Transition block must connect to an output port directly. In the Rate Transition block parameters dialog box, the?Ensure data integrity during data transfer? parameter is selected. In the Rate Transition block?parameters dialog box, the?Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)? parameter is not selected. The Rate Transition block displays?buf ?on the block icon.  -If you select?Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer? and?Whenever Possible? or?Never (minimum delay) ?for?Deterministic data transfer? on the?Solver ?pane in?the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Rate Transition badge displays ?buf ?on the badge. When these conditions are met, the Rate Transition block input and output code may be incorrectly moved out of their semaphore protection context in the generated code. For?example this is the?incorrect code?pattern:? ??? RateTransition_semaphoreTaken = 1;??? RateTransition_semaphoreTaken = 0;??? for (i = 0; i < 25; i++) {????? Y.Out[i] = RateTransition_Buffer0[i];??? } This is the correct code pattern:? ??? RateTransition_semaphoreTaken = 1;??? for (i = 0; i < 25; i++) {????? rtb_RateTransition[i] = RateTransition_Buffer0[i];??? }??? RateTransition_semaphoreTaken = 0;??? for (i = 0; i < 25; i++) {????? Y.Out[i] = rtb_RateTransition[i];??? } In the correct code pattern, the read access of? RateTransition_Buffer0 is protected.
Workaround: Create an atomic subsystem and put the Rate Transition block in it.?Set the?subsystem Function packaging parameter to Nonreusable.

Summary: Crash MATLAB and Target Computer when Parameter work space window opens in Simulink Real-Time Explorer for Referenced Model
Description: It is possible to cause a segmentation fault (crash) of MATLAB and the Target Computer when both conditions are true: The real-time application is loaded on the target computer. The Parameters work space window?in Simulink Real-Time Explorer is opened from the UI or command line to display run-time parameters for a reference model in the real-time application.
Workaround: To request a patch to resolve this issue in R2017a, please?contact MathWorks technical support.

Summary: DOM API does not honor both height and width dimensions for an image in PDF output
Description: If you specify both Height  and Width  dimensions for an Image , these dimensions are not honored in the PDF output. For example, the image generated with the below script will not have the specified dimensions. import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('MyReport', 'pdf');open(d);append(d, Paragraph('Image should have 1in width and 5in height'));image = append(d, Image(which('ngc6543a.jpg')));% specifying Image Width to be 1inimage.Width = '1in';% specifying Image Height to be 5inimage.Height = '5in';close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);
Workaround: Specify the scaling to be nonuniform for the Image, for exampleimport mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('MyReport', 'pdf');open(d);append(d, Paragraph('Image should have 1in width and 5in height'));image = append(d, Image(which('ngc6543a.jpg')));% specifying Image Width to be 1inimage.Width = '1in';% specifying Image Height to be 5inimage.Height = '5in';% specifying the scaling to be non-uniformimage.Style = [image.Style {FOProperties(FOProperty('scaling', 'non-uniform'))}];close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);

Summary: ASAP2 file generation error for model that uses Simulink dual-scaled parameters
Description: If you generate ASAP2 files for a model that uses Simulink dual-scaled parameters, code generation might fail with a message similar to one of the following: Error: File: <MATLAB>\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2lib.tlc Line: 3127 Column: 78Attempt to divide by zero Error: File: <MATLAB>\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2lib.tlc Line: 1767 Column: 37The left-hand side of a . operator must be a valid scope identifier
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: cd command fails?to change to a One Drive directory
Description: When running MATLAB on a PC with the Windows 10 Fall Creators Update, the cd  command might?fail?to change the directory to your One Drive directory: Error using cd    Cannot CD to C:\Users\<user_name>\OneDrive (Name is nonexistent or not a directory).?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a MATLAB function block in which a variable is written to in and after a for loop
Description: Incorrect code may occur for the following code pattern in a MATLAB function block: A variable A  is written to in a for  loop.? The variable A  is copied from variable B  right after the same for  loop. The variable B  is written before the for  loop.  For the above code pattern, the copy to variable A  after the for  loop may be incorrectly optimized away in the generated C code. For example, this C code is correct for the above code pattern ? B = ...  ? ...  ? for (P1 = 0; P1 < 9; P1++) {  ? ? for (P5 = 0; P5 < 5; P5++) {  ? ? ? A = rtb_mpswitch[35] * 5.0;  ? ? }  ? }  ?A = B;  This C code contains the bug for the above code pattern. A = ...  ...  for (P1 = 0; P1 < 9; P1++) {  ? for (P5 = 0; P5 < 5; P5++) {  ? ? A = rtb_mpswitch[35] * 5.0;  ? }  }?
Workaround: Unzip the attachment and save the workaroundForBugReport1609506.p file to a folder on your MATLAB path. Run this script in each MATLAB session before opening your model.

Summary: Simulating a model in Accelerator mode will crash MATLAB when simulation artifacts from a previous release are present
Description: Simulating a model in Accelerator mode in R2017b or later releases will crash MATLAB if the slprj/accel folder generated in R2016b or R2017a is present. The expected behavior is to prompt the user to delete the slprj folder generated in another release. This only affects top model simulation in accelerator mode. Referenced models simulated in Accelerator mode are unaffected.
Workaround: Delete the slprj folder or slprj/accel folder generated in R2016b or R2017a.

Summary: Cloning a Git repository fails with "authentication not supported" error
Description: When you try to retrieve some Git repositories in MATLAB, you can see the error: authentication not supported . This problem can only occur with repositories that require a user name and password to clone. In some cases, you see an error instead of a prompt for your credentials.

Summary: Custom EDA script commands specified through the CLI not reflected in ModelSim script
Description: When the  SimulationTool ? property is set to 'Mentor Graphics ModelSim' , the simulation and compilation scripts do not reflect custom EDA script commands specified using the CLI.
Workaround: Set the SimulationTool property to 'Custom'.

Summary: Error during slice generation when the model contains masked variant subsystem
Description: Simulink throws an error during slice generation if the model being sliced contains masked variant subsystems: Only Inport, Outport, Connection Port, Subsystem and Model blocks can be added to Variant Subsystem block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code may be generated in classic initialization mode for a subsystem Outport block that?is driven by a Constant block
Description: The output of a subsystem is not initialized when all these conditions are true: The Underspecified initialization detection  parameter?is set to Classic . The subsystem is a conditionally executed subsystem. The Outport block has an initial output value of 0 or [] The source of the Outport is a Constant block with its Sample time  parameter set to inf .
Workaround: Set the model parameter Underspecified initialization detection to Simplified.? Simplified is the recommended setting for Simulink models and avoids the above issue.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when a value of a different enumeration is assigned via a subscripted assignment
Description: MATLAB issues an error when?you try to?change a value of a matrix element from one enumeration type to another enumeration type by using subscripted assignment. In the generated code, the matrix element is assigned a value of the original enumeration type.? For example, suppose that you have?an enumeration class E1  that contains values Red  and Green  and an enumeration class E2  that contains values Low  and High.? Suppose that  array?a  contains enumerated values of class E1 , and value?b  has?enumeration class E2. MATLAB throws an error when you try to execute the following code. In the generated code, however, a(index) is assigned a value of?E1 that has the same ordinal as?b .? a = [E1.Red, E1.Green];b = E2.Low;a(index) = b;  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function Blocks and MATLAB System Blocks in Simulink, and Stateflow charts.

Summary: After importing an existing test result, signals cannot be plotted in Test Manager
Description: If you import an existing test result into Test Manager and then try to plot a signal, the signal does not get plotted.
Workaround: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.

Summary: Slice generation with inline masks option disabled removes contents of masked subsystem
Description: In some cases, generating a slice with the option to inline masks disabled still removes virtual blocks within masked subsystems in the sliced model.

Summary: Exporting a clock signal from certain National Instruments devices might generate an error
Description: When using NI-DAQ devices, exporting a clock using addClockConnection ?and then starting a clocked operation with either startForeground  or startBackground , might generate the following error message: NI Error -89120: Source terminal to be routed could not be found on the device. Make sure the terminal name is valid for the specified device. Refer to Measurement & Automation Explorer for valid terminal names. Property: DAQmx_SampClk_Src Property: DAQmx_SampClk_ActiveEdge Source Device: Dev1 Source Terminal: Default Task Name: _unnamedTask<149> Status Code: -89120

Summary: Compatibility check fails with usage of sizeof?function
Description: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails for models with C Functions in Chart containing? sizeof? function.  The error message reported in the summary window is as follows:? Unexpected internal error.  And the diagnostic viewer displays the following error:? Model is incompatible with Simulink Design Verifier.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Initializing a Ground block signal as a bus might produce a silent wrong answer
Description: Simulation might produce a silent wrong answer when all of these conditions apply: A Ground block connects to the first input data port of a Switch or Multiport Switch block. A nonvirtual bus connects to an input data port of the same Switch or Multiport Switch block. The nonvirtual bus output of the block connects to a conditionally executed subsystem. The conditionally executed subsystem has an initial condition structure specified by an Outport block.  Code generation produces an error?when all of these conditions apply.
Workaround: Replace the Ground block with a Constant block that specifies?0?as its constant value.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data Store Memory block or?block input or output?accessed across different sample times may lead to data integrity issue in the generated code
Description: ?A model that meets all of these conditions may?result incorrect generated code: The model contains a Data Store Memory block?or block?input and output?accessed across different rates. The model configuration parameter Optimize Global Data Access  is set to Use global to store temporary results  value.  In the generated code, the the global variable corresponding to the DSM or block IO may be incorrectly reused and cause the access from the slower rate to receive incorrect values.

Summary: Compatibility check fails with enumerations derived from built-in integer types
Description: If your model contains an enumeration derived from a built-in integer type such as int8  or uint16 , but not from the Simulink.IntEnumType  class, or created from Simulink.defineIntEnumType  with the StorageType  set to a built-in integer type, Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check may fail with the following error:Checking compatibility...Unexpected internal error:Assert? aSrcValue->type()->equals(this->type(),CG::Type::IGNORE_ENUMS)

Summary: MATLAB does not check the TMPDIR or TMP environment variable to determine where to store log files
Description: On Linux systems, MATLAB always uses the /tmp directory to store log files instead of using the TMPDIR or TMP environment variable to determine the temporary file path.

Summary: IEC Certification Kit for Simulink Test mayreport a failed validation status if the User ID is numeric
Description: IEC Certification Kit for Simulink Testperforms an auto-comparison of the Simulink Test reports that are generated inthe userˇŻs environment against the expected results. When the User ID is composed of numeric characters and similar to a timestamp format, the auto-comparison feature will treat it asvolatile content. This can affect the comparison outcome and result in failedvalidation status.

Summary: Wrong answer when a subsystem containing an unconnected output is placed between a Variant Source and an Outport block
Description: Wrong simulation and code generation results are seen when all  the following conditions are true: An atomic or virtual subsystem block is driven by a Variant Source block. The subsystem contains an Inport block directly connected to an Outport block. The subsystem also contains an unconnected Outport block. The output port of the subsystem corresponding to the direct connection between the Inport block and the Outport block is connected to an Outport block of a conditionally executed subsystem.

Summary: CAN Pack block precision of packed signal values less than expected
Description: When using the CAN Pack block to pack CAN signals into a message, numeric rounding might cause values to be calculated with less than expected precision. This might be more noticeable when using large signal offset definitions.

Summary: Appending an empty table to a PDF document causes an error
Description: Appending an empty table to a PDF document causes a Java error at the MATLAB command line, for example import mlreportgen.dom.*?d = Document('bug', 'pdf');append?(d, Table(1));close(d);? ?
Workaround: Do not append empty tables to a PDF document.

Summary: Cannot specify both PDF?table width and column specification group
Description: Specifying both a table width and a column specification group, without specifying the group's Span  property, in a PDF report causes a Java error to appear at the MATLAB command line. For example, this script causes a Java error. import mlreportgen.dom.*rpt = Document('MyReport','pdf');% Span property is not specified for the groupgroups(1) = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpecGroup;groups(1).Style = {Color('red'), Border('solid') };tbl = append(rpt, Table(magic(5)));tbl.ColSpecGroups = groups;tbl.Width = '5in';close(rpt);
Workaround: Specify the Span property for the TableColSpecGroup, for exampleimport mlreportgen.dom.*rpt = Document('MyReport','pdf');groups(1) = mlreportgen.dom.TableColSpecGroup;% Specifying the Span property for the groupgroups(1).Span = 1;groups(1).Style = {Color('red'), Border('solid') };tbl = append(rpt, Table(magic(5)));tbl.ColSpecGroups = groups;tbl.Width = '5in';close(rpt);

Summary: When array of buses or structures uses?GetSet,?generated code does not expect your?functions to accept index argument
Description: When you apply the custom storage class GetSet  to a piece of array data, you must provide get  and set  functions that accept an index argument. For example, you must provide a get  function like this one: double get_myData(index);?  Then, the generated algorithmic?code?reads?an element of the array data by c alling get_myData , passing an integer value (for example, 3)  ?for the index argument: block_output = ?get_myData(3) * 5.13;  However, when you apply GetSet  to an array of?buses or an array of structures,?the generated code does not expect your functions to accept an index argument. For example, you cannot provide a get ?function like this one: myStructType get_myStruct(index);?  Instead, the generated code expects your function to return a pointer to?the entire array of structures, like this function: myStructType* get_myStruct(void);  The generated algorithmic code?reads?an individual field?by calling get_myStruct  without any arguments, indexing into the array of structures, and dereferencing the target field:? block_output = get_myStruct()[3].targetField * 5.13;

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Switch block and Manual Switch block do not generate expected error?at simulation
Description: Switch block and Manual Switch block do not generate?expected error for simulation at first time step. Instead, they?return 0 .? When a model has Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Data Validity > Signals > Wrap on overflow  set to Error , a Switch or Manual Switch block does not?generate?a wrapped value error at the first time step.?Instead, the blocks?return?0 . For?subsequent time steps, the blocks behave as expected. This?issue happens?only during simulation. Generated code works correctly.?

Summary: Codegeneration?may fail?when blocks that model control flow have a non-scalarconditional expression with input signal data that does not reside incontiguous memory.
Description: Code generation mayfail with blocks such as Switch, Multiport Switch, or Dead Zone when both of theseconditions exist:  The conditional logic is     non-scalar, implying an element by element evaluation. The non-scalar evaluation can come from     the control port signal or a threshold parameter or both.  Multiple upstream blocks     contribute to a data input signal causing the input data to reside in     non-contiguous memory.
Workaround: You can workaround this issue by implementing these steps:For each data Inport to a     block modeling control flow that originates from multiple upstream blocks,     add a Signal Conversion block between the upstream blocks and the block     that models the?control flow.For each added Signal     Conversion block, right click on the block to open the Block Parameters dialog box and select the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter.

Summary: Model Advisory check?Check signal line labels does not flag Constant block values that are numerical.
Description: Model Advisor check Check signal line labels (check ID: mathworks.maab.na_0008 ) should flag the output of a Constant block when the signal label is missing and the constant value is a numerical vs. workspace variable.? The check does not flag Constant block values that are numerical.

Summary: MATLAB may crash during code generation for a model containing structure arrays?or matrices and with inlined parameters
Description: MATLAB may crash during code generation for a model?that meets?these conditions: The model contains parameters?that are structure arrays or matrices. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Default parameter behavior?  parameter is set to Inlined .?  In the crash log, the stack trace may contain this text: [ ?9] 0x00000000fb30bdfb C:\MATLAB\R2014b_x64\toolbox\rtw\rtw\tlc_new.mexw64+00179707 [ 10] 0x00000000fb327b60 C:\MATLAB\R2014b_x64\toolbox\rtw\rtw\tlc_new.mexw64+00293728 [ 11] 0x00000000fb3324bb C:\MATLAB\R2014b_x64\toolbox\rtw\rtw\tlc_new.mexw64+00337083 [ 12] 0x00000000fb333b21 C:\MATLAB\R2014b_x64\toolbox\rtw\rtw\tlc_new.mexw64+00342817 mexFunction+00000401 [ 13] 0x00000000fc5f3701 ? ? ? ? ?C:\MATLAB\R2014b_x64\bin\win64\libmex.dll+00079617 mexRunMexFile+00000129 [ 14] 0x00000000fc5f2762 ? ? ? ? ?C:\MATLAB\R2014b_x64\bin\win64\libmex.dll+00075618 inSwapMexfileReader+00000690 [ 15] 0x00000000fc5f2248 ? ? ? ? ?C:\MATLAB\R2014b_x64\bin\win64\libmex.dll+00074312 mexUnlock+00006008
Workaround: You can workaround this issue by turning on TLC debugging for code generation.?At the MATLAB?Command?Prompt, turn on TLC debugging by entering this command:??set_param(model,'TLCDebug','on')For large models, this workaround may cause MATLAB to use all available memory, which will cause code generation to fail. If this occurs, the only solution is to use a machine with more memory.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code may be generated when Discrete-Time Integrator's State Port is used and Single output/update function parameter is selected
Description: Incorrect code may occur for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a Discrete-Time Integrator block. On the?Discrete-Time Integrator block parameters dialog box, the State Port  parameter is?selected. The State Port output connects to a Goto block. The From block that corresponds with the Goto block is in another atomic subsystem. In the Configuration parameters dialog box, the Single output/update function  parameter is selected. The?Discrete-Time Integrator block and state port destination execute in the same time step.  The demo model sldemo_clutch  contains activated state ports and corresponding Goto and From blocks that cross atomic subsystem boundaries. This model demonstrates?how to correctly?use state ports that cross atomic subsystem boundaries -- the locked and unlocked subsystems are never enabled in the same time step. While the model contains the continuous time version of the Integrator block, you could use the Discrete-Time version, and the model would still generate correct code because of the exclusive execution contexts. However if a model?executes both the Discrete-Time Integrator?block and its state port destination in the same time step, the code is incorrect? because the generated code uses the newly calculated state, rather than the state from the previous time step as simulation would do.
Workaround: Clear the?Single output/update function parameter for the model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in answer with MATLAB?when the input to isreal?is an object, a structure, or a function handle
Description: If?x  is an object, a structure, or a function handle,? isreal(x)  returns true  in the generated code, but false  in MATLAB. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, ?and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Modify the source code to avoid dependency of the return value of isreal when the input is an?object, a structure, or a ?function handle.

Summary: Magnitude-Angle to Complex block generates incorrect fixed-point HDL code
Description: The Magnitude-Angle to Complex Simulink block generates HDL code that does not produce the correct result when fixed-point operands are used.
Workaround: Use the Native Floating Point mode of  HDL Coder to generate the HDL code for this block.? If this is undesirable, the behavior of the "Magnitude-Angle To Complex" block can be replicated by using the "Trigonometric Function" block in sincos mode. The cos output is the real portion of the complex result and the sin output is the imaginary portion. To include the magnitude in the calculation, multiply the complex output with the magnitude input by using a "Product" block.

Summary: When a group of Inport blocks are pasted in a subsystem, if not all Inport blocks with the same port number are copied, Simulink can crash
Description: Simulink can crash when a group of blocks are copied and pasted in a subsystem, when both of these conditions exist: The group contains one or more Inport blocks. Not all of the Inport blocks with the same port number (duplicate Inport blocks) have been copied.
Workaround: Before pasting the blocks, make sure that all of the Inport blocks with the same port number have been copied.

Summary: Unable to log in to the MATLAB Production Server dashboard after setup
Description: After setting up the MATLAB Production Server dashboard and starting it, you might not be able to log in using the default admin account and password. The error message might look like this: error fetching client from pool { [Error: getaddrinfo ENOENT localhost:9080]???code: 'ENOENT',???errno: 'ENOENT',???syscall: 'getaddrinfo',???hostname: 'localhost',??host: 'localhost',? port: 9080 }?
Workaround: If you are using the dashboard in R2017a, follow the steps below to workaround the issue:1. Navigate to the folder where the dashboard is installed. ?? ? The default location on Windows is C:\Program Files\MATLAB\MATLAB Production Server\R2017a\dashboard.? ? The default location on Linux is /usr/local/MATLAB/MATLAB_Production_Server/R2017a/dashboard.2. Navigate to the config folder and open the config.txt file in a text editor.?3. Change the databaseHost value from localhost to 127.0.0.1.4. Locate the file cnPool.js under <MPS_installed_directory>\dashboard\main\server\, and replace it with the file cnPool.js from inside the attached .zip file.5. Restart the dashboard. ?If you are able to log in but do not see any content on the page, try logging out and logging back in again.

Summary: Control System Designer generates incorrect responses for control architecture configurations 2 and 3
Description: Control System Designer generates incorrect responses when using control architecture configuration 2 or 3. Both architectures contain a plant (G), sensor dynamics (H), prefilter (F), and a compensator (C). Control System Designer generates incorrect plots and transfer functions for any response that includes both C and F in the path from the input to the output.?
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. Summary: Creating axes that contain both a legend and a text object can lead to an increase in CPU usage
Description: If you create axes that contain both a legend and a text object and do either of the following, then the CPU usage might increase. Change the axes position. Set the ActivePositionProperty  to 'position' .  For example: plot(1:4)ax = gca;?legend(ax,'My Legend')text(ax,2,2,'My Text');?ax.ActivePositionProperty = 'position';

Summary: MATLAB Live Editor does not work on Ubuntu versions 17.04 and newer
Description: The Live Editor does not work on Ubuntu versions 17.04 and newer. When running MATLAB in one of these versions, there is no Live Script ?option in the New ?menu. ?In addition, attempting to open a live code (*.mlx) file by double clicking on the file in the Current Folder Browser, using the edit  command, or using the Open  menu produces no action. This occurs because Ubuntu versions 17.04 and newer do not ship with one of libraries required to run the Live Editor.
Workaround: Install the libgconf-2.4 library:apt-get install libgconf-2.4Restart MATLAB.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when displaying a MAT-file in the Current Folder browser
Description: If the current folder contains a corrupted v4 MAT-file, MATLAB might crash when the Current Folder browser attempts to display a preview of the file content. The preview is triggered when the?file is selected in the Current Folder Browser and the Details Panel is?expanded.
Workaround: Collapse the detail panel in the Current Folder browser prior to selecting the offending MAT-file.

Summary: Incorrect simulation results in CUDA code generated for ifft
Description: Inverse fast Fourier transform of an input array Y is given by: X = ifft(Y, n),? where n is the inverse transform length.? When n > numel(Y) , simulation of the generated CUDA code might mismatch with MATLAB.?

Summary: The "Unnecessary type conversions" configuration parameter in "All Parameters" tab can be set to unsupported?"error"
Description: The Unnecessary type conversions  configuration parameter in the Commonly Used Parameters  tab provides the two supported options:?none  and warning .?? However,?the All Parameters  tab provides a third, but unsupported option: error . This parameter should only accept none  and warning .

Summary: MATLAB? crashes when a nested panel becomes visible after being reparented
Description: If you create an app that contains nested panels, MATLAB might crash when the following changes occur in this order:A visible nested panel becomes invisible. The parent container of the invisible panel moves to a different figure. The invisible panel becomes visible again.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when generating code for a Stateflow chart with bit shifts using enumerated types
Description: MATLAB crashes when generating code for a Stateflow chart (with C action language) that performs bit shift operations using data of an enumerated type, as shown by the following code: {y |= (1 << enumVar);}?  where enumVar is an instance of an enumeration.?
Workaround: If it is necessary     to use an enumeration value in a bit shift operation, explicitly cast the     enumeration value to ˇ°unitˇ± types (uint8, uint16 or unit32). e.g. {y |= (1 << uint8 (enumVar));}

Summary: Stateflow charts with runtime errors might crash MATLAB while debugging
Description: When a Stateflow chart encounters a runtime error while debugging, an error message is shown in the Diagnostic Viewer and MATLAB immediately crashes.?

Summary: Newly added rows in the source Excel spreadsheet are missed when updating previously imported requirements
Description: If you have imported requirements from a Microsoft Excel spreadsheet, the Update operation in the Requirements Editor captures changes you make to existing requirements but does not update the requirements set to reflect new rows that you added to the source spreadsheet.
Workaround: Re-import?the updated Excel spreadsheet into a different requirements set, so that the new result contains all rows to match the current version of Excel document.?? Discard the results of previous import.?This has a disadvantage if?there are existing?traceability?links?TO or FROM?the previously imported items - some of the links may need to be re-created.?The Summary field value malfunction can be avoided if the column selected for Summary has more text, for example, complete sentences rather than 2-3 word titles.?

Summary: Custom colors in Simulink editors do not?persist across MATLAB sessions
Description: When you set custom colors using the color picker for a Simulink block foreground or?background or the background color of the canvas, these?colors are lost after you end the MATLAB session.?

Summary: Code inspection can incorrectly return "Failed to verify" for some components
Description: Code inspection returns "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" if both of these code patterns are present in the generated code: memcpy  is used in the generated code. The third argument of memcpy  has an extra uint32  cast.  These code patterns are created when you select the model configuration parameter Use memcpy for vector assignment  .
Workaround: Clear the model configuration parameter Use memcpy for vector assignment.Regenerate code.

Summary: Error?when duplicate Inport blocks in a referenced model?resolve to the same signal object
Description: Simulink generates an error if all of these conditions are true: A?model contains duplicate Inport blocks. The output signals of the Inport blocks resolve to the same signal object (such as Simulink.Signal ), for example, when you select the property Signal name must resolve to Simulink signal object . You reference the model through a Model block in a different model.?  The error occurs when you update the block diagram?of the parent model. The error looks like this: Can not resolve Simulink signal object 'sigName' for output port?1 of 'myModel/In1'.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong detection of zero crossings involving duration of continuous data
Description: In a Stateflow chart that uses a "Continuous" update method, Stateflow produces a wrong answer in both simulation and generated code when you use the duration  operator on an expression involving Stateflow data with continuous sample time (either input or local). For example, a transition condition of the form: ?[duration(u>0)>=1]   where u  is a continuous-time input will not evaluate to true after?exactly?1 second.

Summary: A referenced model with an initialize port on does not support signal logging when simulated in rapid accelerator mode
Description: Signal logging (logsout ), i.e, the logging of intermediate signals, is not supported within a referenced model when both of these conditions are true: The top model is being simulated in rapid accelerator mode. The referenced model has the Initialize port enabled.
Workaround: Use a simulation mode other than rapid accelerator (either normal or accelerator).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?Find Nonzero Elements?block
Description: When a scalar signal is the input to a?Find Nonzero Elements block, incorrect code?might be?generated. Here is an example of the incorrect generated code:? void mtest_RW_step(void)?{???int_T subscript0;???int_T dim0;???int32_T rtb_ComplextoRealImag;???subscript0 = 1;???while (1 < dim0) {???...???}?}? This?code is incorrect because dim0  is not initialized before it is used, so?it contains an undefined value.? Many compilers will catch this error.
Workaround: No workaround.? Compile generated code with a high warning level, so?as to warn?of use of an uninitialized variable.

Summary: Analysis fails with error Internal error: assertion failed at: "edg/src/lower_name.c", line 10924
Description: Polyspace analysis of C++ code can fail with this error message:   Verifying __polyspace__stdstubscpp.cpp  Pre-linking GNU C++ 4.9 sources ...  Internal error: assertion failed at: "edg/src/lower_name.c", line 10924
Workaround: If you see this error, try to restart the analysis after removing??__abi_tag__(x) from the preprocessed code using an analysis option.Command line: Use the flag?-D??__abi_tag__(x)=?.User interface: Add __abi_tag__(x)= in Target & Compilers?> Macros > Preprocessor Definitions.If the issue persists, contact Technical Support. If possible, use Polyspace Obfuscator?to obfuscate details of your code:?download the tool on MATLAB Central.?Then provide the obfuscation.zip and log file.

Summary: Slow Simulink startup when default printer is offline
Description: On Windows, MATLAB appears to stop responding when starting Simulink if either: The Windows default printer is set to a physical printer and the printer is offline or not available. The driver for the Windows default printer is outdated.  Simulink?starts up?after some time.
Workaround: Set the Windows default printer to a local printer before starting Simulink.Try updating the driver of the printer to the latest version.

Summary: Nested masked Subsystems generate Unconnected block error during update diagram
Description: When blocks from certain Simscape libraries (For example, SimRF library) are placed inside a masked Subsystem of a model,  Unconnected block  error might be displayed during model update diagram.  An example of the error form one such model with SimRF library is:  Error compiling Simscape network for model.  Caused by:  [block]: Physical port is not connected to any other Simscape blocks. Connect this port to another Simscape block.
Workaround: Look under the mask of the parent subsystem before trying to run your model or updating the diagram.

Summary: Sample time independent model in a Function-Call Subsystem?may cause a strong error message
Description: When a model block referencing a sample time independent model is placed in a Function-Call Subsystem whose Trigger Port's sample time type is set to be periodic  and sample time to be -1 ,?updating?the model diagram may cause the following error: The S-function 'XX_Model/XX_Function/XX_REF' has improperly set the sample time of input port?YY when placed in the function call subsystem 'XX_Model/XX_Function'.
Workaround: To work around the error message,?change the Trigger Port's sample time to?be equal to the triggering signal's sample time. For example, if the Function-Call Subsystem is controlled by a signal with sample time 0.2, set the Trigger Port's sample time to 0.2?by opening the block parameter dialog of the Trigger Port block in the Function-Call Subsystem and change the Sample time?in the tab Main to 0.2.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible with Casting modes set to Standards Compliant and Replace multiplication by powers of two with signed bitwise shifts set to on
Description: Incorrect generated code may occur for a model that meets all of these conditions:In the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Casting Modes  parameter is set to Standards Compliant . In the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Replace multiplications by powers of two with signed bitwise shifts  is set to on.   The model must also meet one of these conditions: 1. The model contains a Rate Transition block that meets these conditions:  The Rate Transition block displays Db_buf . The input signal to the Rate Transition block is at a slower rate than the output signal. For the Rate Transition block, in the Block Parameters dialog box  the Ensure data integrity during data transfer  parameter is selected. For the Rate Transition block, in the Block Parameters dialog box, the Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)  parameter is not selected. The Rate Transition block input is not a scalar.        2. *The model contains a MATLAB Function Block in which a logical expression is an operand of the multiply operator, and the other operand is a power of 2. For example:?a = (b > c) * 8 3. The model contains a Numerical Oscillator block (DSP Toolbox) and the Add internal dither  checkbox is selected, with Number of dither bits  with a value?greater than 1       4. The model uses fixed point binary point conversion in which the input and output have the same Real World Value.  *This bug may also occur for MATLAB coder if a logical expression is an operand of the multiply operator, and the other operand is a power of 2. (For example:?a = (b > c) * 8)?and the Casting Modes  parameter is set to Standards Compliant and the  Use signed shift left for fixed-point operations and multiplication by powers of 2   is set to  on.    Note that the combination of parameter settings leading to incorrect code generation is not recommended by the Code Generation Advisor for the  MISRA objective.  The recommendation is   The Casting Modes  parameter set to Standards Compliant  The  Replace multiplications by powers of two with signed bitwise shifts  or Use signed shift left for fixed-point operations and multiplication by powers of 2  is  set to off.

Summary: On Linux, changing the monitor resolution can result in black patches on the screen and error messages displayed in the desktop
Description: On Linux, if you change the monitor resolution, either directly on your local display or through a VNC viewer that allows resolution changes, MATLAB shows display artifacts such as black patches on the screen. The following error message might also be displayed in the Command Window: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0"java.lang.ClassCastException: sun.awt.image.BufImgSurfaceData cannot be cast to sun.java2d.xr.XRSurfaceData
Workaround: Create a java.opts file with the following line:-Dsun.java2d.xrender=falseFor more information on java.opts, see?Specifying Java Startup Options.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly report an objective status as Unsatisfiable for logical operator blocks which have more than 100 inputs?
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly report?an?objective status as Unsatisfiable . This happens for?some objectives?associated?with?the logical operator block?that has more than 100 inputs.
Workaround: Replace the logical operator block with a cascade combination of the same type?block?that has?less than 100 inputs. This?preserves the functionality and ensures?that the?test generation?works correctly.

Summary: Output signal err is incorrect when CRC Detector receives non-contiguous validIn?
Description: When you provide the?General CRC Syndrome Detector HDL Optimized block with a non-contiguous validIn signal during the checksum, the resulting err output signal is incorrect.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in simulation and code generation results when?multiword fixed-point data with large word lengths are input?to the Sqrt?block configured for the rSqrt function
Description: Mismatch occurs in simulation and code generation results when?multiword fixed-point data with large word lengths are input to a Sqrt block under these conditions: Function  parameter is set to rSqrt .  Method  parameteris set to  Newton-Raphson .  Intermediate results  datatype parameter is set to  Inherit: Inherit from input .  Output  datatype parameter is set to  single .

Summary: MinGW compilation issue when building model reference simulation target with mixed C and C++ code
Description: When building a model reference simulation target that uses a mix of C and C++ code and the MinGW toolchain, the following compilation errors might occur: ?error: 'slmrAddResetEvents' was not declared in this scope ?error: 'slmrSetDataDictionarySet' was not declared in this scope? ?error: 'slmrSetHasSignalLogging' was not declared in this scope ?...

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Breakpoint data type not propagated to input signal for n-D Lookup Table block
Description: Breakpoint data type not propagated to input signal for n-D Lookup Table block. In some cases, no error is returned. Default propagation of input signals for the n-D Lookup Table block does not produce the same data type as the corresponding breakpoint in the case of breakpoint data with fixed-point data types. In these cases: No error is returned if the slope adjustment factors of the table and breakpoint are the same and the?breakpoint data type does not?correctly backpropagate to the?input signal for the block.?? This error is returned during model update?if the?slope adjustment factor of the table data does not match?the slope adjustment factor of any?breakpoints:  The input and breakpoint data types of block '<n-D Lookup Table block>' do not have the same SlopeAdjustmentFactor. The breakpoint data type has a SlopeAdjustmentFactor of?N  and the input data type has a SlopeAdjustmentFactor of M .
Workaround: As a workaround,??if n-D Lookup Table does not produce the same data type as the input source, Add a Data Type Conversion block between the source of the input and the input port of the n-D Lookup Table block.For the Data Type Conversion block,?set Output data type to the same data type as the corresponding breakpoint of the connected n-D Lookup Table block.

Summary: Code generation may fail for a?model containing an?n-D Lookup Table in which the Intermediate results parameter?has a?value smaller than int32
Description: Code generation may fail for a model that meets these conditions: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Casting modes  parameter is set to Standards Compliant . The model contains an n-D Lookup Table block. In the n-D Lookup Table block parameters dialog box, the Intermediate results  parameter?has a?value smaller?than int32 .??

Summary: When using MATLAB Compiler SDK, figures sometimes fail to display
Description: When using MATLAB Compiler SDK, and executing MATLAB code which displays a figure, the figure may not be displayed under certain circumstances.?For instance, when calling the following MATLAB code:  function y=myfun??? spy;??? y = 3;    If the driver code (the code written in another language), does not call back into MATLAB, but rather blocks (for example, on console input), the figure is not displayed. This c++ code is an example of driver code which manifests the problem:  #include <libmy.h>int run_main(void){?????? libmyInitialize();?????? mwArray output;?????? myfun(1, output);?????? std::cout << output << std::endl;?????? getchar();?????? libmyTerminate();?????? mclTerminateApplication();?????? return(0);}int main(void){?????? mclInitializeApplication(NULL, 0);?????? return(run_main());}
Workaround: The workaround is to? add a drawnow function all in the? MATLAB code:function y=myfun??? spy;??? drawnow;??? y = 3;?

Summary: Names of formal arguments do not match between Simulink Function declaration and definition
Description: The names of the formal arguments of Simulink Functions are specified via a combination of Function Prototype Control and the Configuration Parameter Code Generation > Symbols > Subsystem method arguments . ?The protoype of the function definition (in the .c file) honors the specification of the names, but the prototype of the function declaration (in the header file) does not. For example, ?a Simulink function with graphical prototype out = f(in)  and symbol rule ARG_$I_N$M  will lead to the following function definition prototype: ?void f(real_T ARG_u_in, real_T *ARG_y_out)?  ?but the function declaration prototype will be? ?void f(real_T rtu_in, real_T *rty_out)?  The names of the arguments in the function declaration prototype will always conform to the naming rule rt$I$N$M , regardless of the Function Prototype Control specification or the naming rule specified in the configuration parameter.
Workaround: To ensure that the names of the formal arguments match between a Simulink Function's declaration and definition:Specify the value rt$I$N$M for the naming rule Code Generation > Symbols > Subsystem method arguments.If you have configured the C/C++ function interface for a global Simulink Function block, in the Configure C/C++ Function Interface dialog box, set the C/C++ Identifier Name of each function argument to?rt$I$N$M.

Summary: mlreportgen.dom.ScaleToFit format expands smaller images in PDF output
Description: ScaleToFit  format should scale an image to fit the page or table entry only if the image dimensions are bigger than the available space for rendering. Currently, this format also expands the smaller images in the PDF output. For example, import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('MyReport', 'pdf');open(d);append(d, Paragraph('Below is a 1in wide image with ScaleToFit format to be false'));image1 = append(d, Image(which('ngc6543a.jpg')));image1.Style = [image1.Style {ScaleToFit(false)}];image1.Width = '1in';image1.Height = [];append(d, PageBreak);append(d, Paragraph('Below is a 1in wide image with ScaleToFit format to be true'));image2 = append(d, Image(which('ngc6543a.jpg')));image2.Style = [image2.Style {ScaleToFit(true)}];image2.Width = '1in';image2.Height = [];close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);

Summary: Error or crash when reading data from MP3, MP4, and MOV files using MATLAB? on Ubuntu? version 16.04
Description: If you are using Ubuntu? version 16.04 or higher, MATLAB? might return an error or crash while reading some audio and video files. For example [y, Fs] = audioread('myaudiofile.mp3'); vidObj = VideoReader('myvideofile.mp4');    will result in the error below: Error using VideoReader/init (line 619)? The VideoReaderplugin libmwgstreamerplugin failed to load properly.?  The file formats impacted include MP3, MP4, and MOV.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the version of libstdc++.so.6 library shipped with MATLAB? with the native version on your system.To do this, ensure that?your system's?libstdc++.so.6?version is higher than the one shipped with MATLAB?,?by completing the following steps: 1. In a Linux terminal, navigate to?matlabroot/sys/os/glnxa64?and type the following:ls -lThe version of the library shipping with MATLAB? R2016b should be?libstdc++.so.6.0.20.2. Navigate to?/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu?and type the following:ls -l libstdc++*If the resulting version is higher than 6.0.20, then continue with the following steps:1. Navigate to?matlabroot/sys/os/glnxa64.2. Execute the following:unlink libstdc++.so.6ln -s /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libstdc++.so.6 libstdc++.so.63. Restart MATLAB?.?

Summary: Conflicting bus object definitions in multi-level Simulink models that use Model blocks with data dictionaries can cause MATLAB crash
Description: ?A crash can occur when all  of these conditions occur: You create a bus object in the base workspace with the same name as a bus object contained in a data dictionary. The parent model is configured to reference the workspace version of the bus object, while the child model is configured to reference the data dictionary version. The bus is then connected to an input port on the child model.  If the bus is nonvirtual, the duplicate definition is detected during the pre-compilation phase and an error is generated. However, if the bus is virtual, the conflict is not detected, and if the buses have different numbers of elements, MATLAB crashes due to a null pointer dereferencing attempt.
Workaround: Because?the bug is due to an undetected error in the model configuration, the workaround is to eliminate the duplicate bus definition from the model hierarchy.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code may be generated for General CRC Generator (HDL Optimized) block
Description: Incorrect code may be generated in the presence of General CRC Generator (HDL Optimized) block when state logging is turned on.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Data Import/Export pane, turn off state logging by verifying Save to workspace or file?parameter is not selected for States and Final states.

Summary: Custom icon does not get displayed in taskbar in Windows 10
Description: In Windows 10, when you create a standalone application using the Application Compilerand specify a custom icon, the custom icon does not get displayed in the taskbar.

Summary: On Windows machines with more than 64 cores MATLAB onlyuses a subset of cores after any matrix math operation

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Integer literal treated as unsigned when used in expression involving custom code
Description: For a Stateflow chart that meets all of the following conditions, some numeric literals might be incorrectly treated as a numeric literal with a U suffix: The Stateflow chart is configured to use the C Action Language The integer numeric literal fits into a C integer type smaller than target int The integer numeric literal is used within an expression containing a variable or function call that originates from custom code The variable or function call has a signed integer type  This can lead to unexpected simulation behavior. See the model issue_example_1404858  in the attached issue_1404858.zip . It contains a relational expression involving involving a numeric literal and a custom code symbol: ? [customCodeSymbol > 140]  In the custom code settings of the chart customCodeSymbol  is defined as an int ?with value -1. So it would be expected that this statement would evaluate to false. But because Stateflow treats 140 as a numeric literal with a U suffix the meaning of the relational expression is changed. The U suffix causes the signed integer component of the relational expression to be converted to unsigned before the comparison is performed. This can also be seen in the generated code for the model ? if(customCodeSymbol > 140U)?  instead of the expected ? ??if(customCodeSymbol > 140)? ?
Workaround: Explicitly cast the integer literal to a signed integer type in the expression:??? [customCodeSymbol > int32(140)]?

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier does not consider the minimum and maximum constraints specified on the unused input signals during creation of the harness model after test generation analysis
Description: In R2015b, if you perform test generation analysis and create harness model, Simulink Design Verifier replaces the unused input signal with a Constant block that has Constant value equal to 0. This Constant block with the default value 0 may violate the minimum and maximum constraints on the unused input signal. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, set Simulation range checking  to error  or warning . If you simulate the test cases in the harness model that consists of replaced Constant blocks the software throws an error  or warning  indicating the constraint violation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multiple subsystems incorrectly share the same state variable for Unit Delay blocks
Description: See?Bug Report ID 1668727 ?for full details on related issues.? Incorrect code generation might occur when all of the following conditions are true: Optimize global data access ?configuration parameter is set to?Use global to hold temporary results ?or Minimize global data access  Reuse local block outputs  configuration parameter is enabled. Multiple subsystems in the model have their?Function packaging  parameter set to Auto  or Reusable.  Unit Delay blocks' input or output are connected to those subsystems' output signal.  Errors might be thrown during the build process.
Workaround: See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727.

Summary: Compilation error?during build of referenced model that includes Stateflow chart that exports graphical functions and accesses root inports or outports
Description: MATLAB displays a compilation error message during a build?of a referenced model the meets?all of the?following conditions: Contains a Stateflow chart that exports a graphical function.  The graphical function directly accesses Stateflow input or output data that is connected to root inports or outports. The Code generation function packaging? property for the Stateflow chart?is set to  Nonreusable function.
Workaround: To avoid the compilation error, do?one of the following:Insert a Signal Conversion block immediately after the root Inport block or immediately before the root Outport block.Set the?Code generation function packaging?property for the Stateflow chart?to Reusable function.

Summary: MATLAB throws an error when trying to generate code for arithmetic or logical operations with hundreds of inputs
Description: When trying to generate code for arithmetic or logical operations with hundreds of inputs, MATLAB throws the following error: Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: b:\  matlab\  src\  cgir_xform\  dom_core\  fixpt\  fixptdispatcher.hpp line 128: Maximum expression depth exceeded". Please report this to MathWorks if you can cause it to recur  The following examples demonstrate some scenarios that can trigger this error: From MATLAB: Manually entered large arithmetic or logical expression. For example, performing a reciprocal or multiply operation on a large vector. Auto-generated MATLAB code containing large arithmetic or logical expressions. This can be created by MathWorks tooling or by customer or third-party tooling.  From Simulink: An arithmetic or logical block configured to have hundreds of inputs.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Vision HDL blocks that contain a line buffer do not work when valid is toggled within a line
Description: If the input valid signal (within the pixelcontrol bus) is not held high for the duration of a line, Vision HDL blocks that contain a line buffer generate incorrect results. This problem occurs in both simulation and generated HDL code. The simulation and generated code also do not react the same way to this type of input, so there is also a mismatch in verification of the generated code. The blocks that do not function correctly are:  Analysis& Enhancement: EdgeDetector, MedianFilter Conversions: Demosaic Interpolator Geometric Transforms: Birds-Eye View? Filtering: ImageFilter, Median Filter, Bilateral Filter Morphological Operations: Closing, Dilation, Erosion, GrayscaleClosing, Grayscale Dilation, GrayscaleErosion, Grayscale Opening, Opening

Summary: Embedded Coder generates C code that references an undefined variable when a model contains multiple instances of subsystems connected to Unit Delay blocks
Description: See?Bug Report ID 1668727 ?for full details on related issues.? Embedded Coder may generate C Code that is not compilable because it references an undefined variable when a model meets these conditions: The model contains a subsystem that has a Unit Delay block which directly connects to the subsystem's output port. There are multiple instances of the subsystem In the subsystem block parameters dialog box, Function packaging  is set to Reusable Function . In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to Structure reference . In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Optimize global data access  parameter is set to Use global to hold temporary results? or?Minimize global data access.
Workaround: See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727.

Summary: Embedded Coder may fail to build a model when a Unit Delay block directly connects to a reusable subsystem output
Description: See?Bug Report ID 1668727 ?for full details on related issues.? A model containing a subsystem may fail to build if the following conditions exist: On the Subsystem Block Parameters dialog box, Function packaging  is set to Reusable Function . The subsystem contains a Unit Delay block which directly connects to a subsystem output port. On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to Individual Arguments  rather than the default Structure reference  On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Combine signal/state structures  parameter is selected. On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Optimize global data access ?parameter is set to?Use global to hold temporary results? or?Minimize global data access   When the build failure occurs, the diagnostic output contains an errror message similar to the following: ### Build procedure for model: '...' aborted due to an error.Error: File: .....Indices may not be negative
Workaround: See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727.

Summary: Embedded Coder may fail to build a model that reuses local block outputs and contains a Unit Delay block
Description: See?Bug Report ID 1668727 ?for full details on related issues.? In rare cases, a model containing a subsystem and Unit Delay block may fail to build if the following conditions exist: On the Subsystem Block Parameters dialog box, Function packaging  is set to Reusable Function . On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization >Signals and Parameters  pane, Reuse local block outputs  is enabled.. On the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  parameter is set to Structure reference . On?the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Optimize global data access ?parameter is set to?Use global to hold temporary results? or?Minimize global data access   When the build failure occurs, the diagnostic viewer contains one or both of the these error messages: Error: File: ...\syslib.tlc Line: 2725 Column: 55The + operator only works on numeric arguments  or Error: File: ...\syslib.tlc Line: 2725 Column: 56 The specified index ... was out of the range 0 - ...
Workaround: See the workaround in?Bug Report ID 1668727.

Summary: In desktop mode, calling the pause function from startup.m or matlabrc.m hangs MATLAB
Description: MATLAB hangs if you call the pause  function (with no arguments) from startup.m  while attempting to run the MATLAB desktop. The desktop handling for processing the key to leave pause  mode has not been fully initialized when startup.m  is called and execution halts indefinitely. The same problem happens for system administrators who use matlabrc.m .
Workaround: Use the -nodesktop option when starting MATLAB.
